The date and time when this optimization event record was last modified in the system. Updated whenever any attribute of the record changes. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
The value of the parameter after the optimization was applied. Stored as string to accommodate various data types (numeric, categorical, JSON).
Free-form text field for additional context, observations, or instructions related to the optimization event. Used by media traders and campaign managers to document rationale and insights.
The granularity level at which the optimization was applied. Indicates whether the change affected the entire campaign, a specific flight, a line item, a placement, or a creative asset.
Current lifecycle status of the optimization event. Tracks whether the optimization is pending execution, successfully applied, failed to apply, rolled back, or expired due to time constraints.
The exact date and time when the optimization action was executed. Represents the business event time when the change took effect in the campaign execution system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Detailed explanation of why the optimization was triggered. Captures the business rationale, performance threshold breach, or strategic decision that prompted the change.
Indicates whether the optimization was triggered manually by a user, automatically by a system algorithm, or by a predefined rule-based condition.

The unit of measurement for the previous and new values. Examples include currency code (USD, EUR), percentage (%), CPM (Cost Per Mille), CPC (Cost Per Click), impressions, or dimensionless for categorical changes.

description: Maximum spend amount authorized for this media buy as approved on the Insertion Order or client budget authorization. Acts as a financial control limit; actual spend must not exceed this value.

description: Classification of the buying modality used to acquire this media inventory. description: Net cost of the media buy after deducting agency commission from the gross amount. Represents the actual vendor payment obligation. Net = Gross minus Agency Commission.

description: Free-text field for buyer annotations, special deal terms, negotiation notes, or operational instructions associated with this media buy. Not used for structured data.

CPM = Cost Per Mille (thousand impressions); CPC = Cost Per Click; CPA = Cost Per Acquisition; GRP = Gross Rating Point cost; flat = fixed fee; CPCV = Cost Per Completed View; vCPM = viewable CPM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cpm|cpc|cpa|grp|flat|cpcv|vcpm — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Unreconciled = not yet matched; in_progress = reconciliation underway; reconciled = fully matched; disputed = discrepancy under review; written_off = irreconcilable variance closed.

description: System audit timestamp recording when this media buy record was last modified. Supports change tracking, reconciliation workflows, and data quality monitoring.

description: Precise date and time when the ad server or media vendor confirmed receipt and acceptance of the trafficking instruction. Used for SLA compliance measurement and operational reporting.
reference: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Ad Trafficking Audit; IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines

description: Date and time when this trafficking instruction record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation marker for data governance and lineage tracking.
reference: GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity; IAB Data Governance Guidelines

description: Pixel dimensions of the creative asset being trafficked, expressed as width x height (e.g., 300x250, 728x90, 160x600). Must conform to IAB standard ad unit sizes. Used for placement compatibility validation.
reference: IAB Standard Ad Unit Portfolio; Google Campaign Manager 360 - Creative Specifications

description: Day-of-week and time-of-day scheduling parameters defining when the ad creative should be served (dayparting). Expressed as a structured schedule string (e.g., 'Mon-Fri 08:00-20:00'). Used to optimize delivery timing per media plan.
reference: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Daypart Scheduling; Mediaocean Prisma - Media Planning

description: The scheduled date on which ad creative delivery is to cease per this trafficking instruction. Aligns with the media plan flight end dates and insertion order terms.
reference: Mediaocean Prisma - Flight Scheduling; Google Campaign Manager 360 - Campaign Scheduling; IAB Standard IO Terms

description: The scheduled date on which ad creative delivery is to commence per this trafficking instruction. Aligns with the media plan flight dates and insertion order terms.
reference: Mediaocean Prisma - Flight Scheduling; Google Campaign Manager 360 - Campaign Scheduling; IAB Standard IO Terms

description: Device type targeting parameters specifying which device categories the ad creative should be served on (e.g., desktop, mobile, tablet, Connected TV (CTV), or all devices). Aligns with media plan targeting specifications.
reference: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Device Targeting; IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines

description: Maximum number of times the ad creative may be served to a single unique user within the defined frequency cap period. Controls ad exposure and prevents overexposure to individual users.
reference: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Frequency Capping; IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines

description: Time window over which the frequency cap impressions limit applies. Defines the reset interval for the per-user impression count (e.g., no more than 3 impressions per day).
reference: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Frequency Capping; IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines

description: Geographic targeting parameters applied to this trafficking instruction, specifying the countries, regions, DMAs, or cities where the ad should be served. Expressed as a structured targeting string or comma-separated location codes.
reference: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Geo Targeting; IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines

description: URL of the impression tracking pixel fired upon each ad impression to record delivery counts. Used for third-party verification, billing reconciliation, and campaign measurement. May include multiple pixels concatenated or the primary pixel URL.
reference: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Impression Tracking; MRC Digital Measurement Guidelines; IAB Measurement Guidelines

description: Externally-known, human-readable reference number for this trafficking instruction, used in communications with ad servers, media vendors, and internal trafficking teams. Typically follows a structured naming convention (e.g., TI-2024-00123).
reference: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Ad Trafficking; IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines

description: Current lifecycle state of the trafficking instruction: pending (created but not yet dispatched), sent (transmitted to ad server or vendor), confirmed (acknowledged by recipient), rejected (refused by recipient), or cancelled (voided before confirmation).
reference: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Ad Trafficking Workflow; IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines

description: Classifies the nature of the trafficking instruction: new (initial dispatch of a creative), revision (update to an existing instruction), cancellation (termination of delivery), pause (temporary halt), or resume (restart after pause).
reference: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Ad Trafficking Workflow; IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines

description: Free-text field for additional operational notes, special instructions, or contextual information relevant to this trafficking instruction. Used by ad operations teams to communicate nuances not captured in structured fields.
reference: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Ad Trafficking; Workfront - Project Notes

description: Free-text description of the reason provided by the ad server or media vendor when a trafficking instruction is rejected. Populated only when instruction_status is 'rejected'. Used for troubleshooting and resubmission workflows.
reference: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Ad Trafficking Error Handling; IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines

description: Sequential version number of this trafficking instruction, incremented each time a revision instruction is issued for the same placement and creative combination. Version 1 represents the original instruction.
reference: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Ad Trafficking Versioning; Workfront - Change Management

description: Precise date and time when the trafficking instruction was transmitted to the ad server or media vendor. Used for SLA tracking, audit trails, and delivery timeline analysis.
reference: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Ad Trafficking Audit; IAB Digital Advertising Guidelines

description: Indicates whether third-party ad verification (e.g., DoubleVerify, Integral Ad Science) is mandated for this trafficking instruction per client or campaign requirements. When true, verification tags must be included in the ad serving setup.
reference: TAG Certified Against Fraud; MRC Accreditation Standards; IAB Quality Assurance Guidelines

description: The date on which the trafficking instruction was formally issued and dispatched to the ad server or media vendor. Represents the principal real-world business event date for this operational record.
reference: Google Campaign Manager 360 - Ad Trafficking; Mediaocean Prisma - Trafficking Workflow

description: Date and time when this trafficking instruction record was last modified. Tracks revisions to the instruction for audit, compliance, and change management purposes.
reference: GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity; IAB Data Governance Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Video Ad Serving Template (VAST) Tag URL
description: The Video Ad Serving Template (VAST) tag URL used for video creative delivery. Provides the ad server with the XML-based instructions for serving video ads, including media file location, tracking events, and companion ads. Applicable for video placements.
reference: IAB VAST Standard (Video Ad Serving Template); Google Campaign Manager 360 - Video Trafficking

description: URL of the viewability measurement tag embedded in the ad creative to track whether the ad was viewable per MRC and IAB viewability standards (e.g., 50% of pixels in view for 1 continuous second for display). Used for viewability reporting and vendor compliance.
reference: MRC Viewability Standards; IAB Viewability Guidelines; Google Campaign Manager 360 - Viewability Measurement

business_glossary_term: Video Player-Ad Interface Definition (VPAID) Tag URL
description: The Video Player-Ad Interface Definition (VPAID) tag URL enabling interactive video ad execution within the video player environment. Used for rich interactive video creatives requiring player-level communication. Applicable for VPAID-enabled placements.
reference: IAB VPAID Standard (Video Player-Ad Interface Definition); Google Campaign Manager 360 - Video Trafficking

description: Unique identifier for the advertising channel. Primary key for the ad_channel entity.

Number of days after an ad interaction (click or view) during which conversions are attributed to this channel. Standard windows are 1, 7, 30, or 90 days depending on channel and business model.

business_glossary_term: California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) Opt-Out Supported
description: Indicates whether this channel supports CCPA Do Not Sell signals for California users. Required for compliance with California privacy regulations.

Number of days after a click during which conversions are attributed to this channel. Typically longer than view attribution window due to higher intent signal.

Format of privacy consent signals passed to this channel. TCF v2 (Transparency and Consent Framework) for GDPR, US Privacy String for CCPA, GPP (Global Privacy Platform) for multi-jurisdiction compliance.

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Consent Required

Description of ad format or creative specifications that are restricted or required under this compliance rule (e.g., IAB LEAN ad standards limiting file size and animation, VAST/VPAID requirements for video ads).
reference: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) LEAN Ad Standards; VAST (Video Ad Serving Template); VPAID (Video Player-Ad Interface Definition)

description: Indicates whether this compliance rule applies to direct media buys negotiated with publishers through insertion orders and managed campaigns.
reference: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) Direct Buy Standards

description: Indicates whether this compliance rule applies to programmatic advertising buys executed through DSPs (Demand-Side Platforms), SSPs (Supply-Side Platforms), and RTB (Real-Time Bidding) exchanges.
Indicates whether this compliance rule mandates the creation and retention of an audit trail documenting compliance verification, user consent, or enforcement actions.
reference: GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) Article 5; Internal Compliance Management Standard

description: The brand safety classification level required by this compliance rule, determining the acceptable risk level for ad placement adjacency to content (e.g., high-risk content exclusion, moderate filtering, minimal filtering).
reference: TAG (Trustworthy Accountability Group) Brand Safety Standards; IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) Content Taxonomy

description: Description of the technical or procedural mechanism used to capture and validate user consent for this compliance rule (e.g., CMP integration, opt-in checkbox, consent string, IAB TCF framework).
reference: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) Transparency and Consent Framework (TCF)

description: The date and time when this compliance rule record was first created in the system.
The maximum number of days that user data or advertising activity data may be retained under this compliance rule, after which it must be deleted or anonymized per privacy regulations.
reference: GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) Article 5; CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act)

description: Description of the mandatory disclosure or labeling that must accompany advertising under this compliance rule (e.g., 'Paid Partnership', 'Sponsored', 'Ad', FTC endorsement disclosure).
reference: FTC (Federal Trade Commission) Endorsement Guides; ASA (Advertising Standards Authority) Disclosure Standards

description: The date from which this compliance rule becomes enforceable and must be applied to campaign trafficking and creative approval workflows.

The severity of enforcement for this compliance rule. Mandatory rules block campaign trafficking if violated, recommended rules generate warnings but allow override, and advisory rules provide guidance without enforcement.
The date on which this compliance rule ceases to be enforceable. Null indicates an open-ended rule with no planned expiration.
The maximum number of times an ad may be shown to a single user within the frequency cap period, as mandated by this compliance rule to prevent ad fatigue or harassment.
reference: Platform-Specific Frequency Policies; IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) Best Practices

description: The time period over which the frequency cap limit is enforced (e.g., per hour, per day, per week, per month, per campaign lifetime).
reference: Platform-Specific Frequency Policies; IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) Best Practices

description: The regulatory authority, platform, or industry organization that mandates or publishes this compliance rule (e.g., FTC, ASA, EASA, Google, Meta, IAB, TAG).
reference: FTC (Federal Trade Commission); ASA (Advertising Standards Authority); EASA (European Advertising Standards Alliance); IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau); TAG (Trustworthy Accountability Group)

description: The geographic or legal jurisdiction where this compliance rule applies, using ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or regional identifiers (e.g., USA, GBR, EUR, global).
reference: ISO 3166-1 Country Codes; GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation); CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act)

description: The date on which this compliance rule was last reviewed and validated for accuracy and currency by the compliance team.
The scheduled date for the next periodic review of this compliance rule to ensure it remains current with regulatory changes and platform policy updates.
Description of the legal, financial, or operational penalties that may result from violating this compliance rule (e.g., fines, account suspension, legal action, reputational damage).
reference: FTC (Federal Trade Commission); ASA (Advertising Standards Authority); GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation)

description: The official document identifier, regulation number, or policy code that formally defines this compliance rule (e.g., GDPR Article 6, FTC Act Section 5, IAB LEAN Ad Standard).
The web URL linking to the official documentation, regulation text, or policy page that defines this compliance rule.
Indicates whether this compliance rule mandates explicit user consent before the advertising activity can proceed (e.g., GDPR consent for targeted advertising, CCPA opt-out mechanisms).
reference: GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation) Article 6; CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act)

description: Comma-separated list of audience segments or demographic groups that cannot be targeted under this compliance rule (e.g., minors under 13, sensitive health conditions, protected classes).
reference: COPPA (Children's Online Privacy Protection Act); GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation); Platform-Specific Targeting Policies

description: Comma-separated list of content types or product categories that are restricted or prohibited under this compliance rule (e.g., alcohol, tobacco, gambling, pharmaceuticals, political advertising).
reference: ASA (Advertising Standards Authority); EASA (European Advertising Standards Alliance); Platform-Specific Content Policies

description: The functional category of the compliance requirement. Disclosure rules mandate advertiser identification, consent rules govern user permission for data usage, targeting rules restrict audience segmentation, creative content rules limit ad messaging, data usage rules govern PII handling, and measurement rules define verification standards.
reference: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) Compliance Framework; GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation); CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act)

description: Unique business identifier code for the compliance rule, used for external reference and system integration.
Detailed narrative description of the compliance rule, including the specific requirement, the business rationale, and the enforcement mechanism.
Human-readable name of the compliance rule for identification and reporting purposes.

Current lifecycle status of the compliance rule. Active rules are enforced in trafficking workflows, inactive rules are not enforced, pending rules are awaiting approval, deprecated rules are phased out, and suspended rules are temporarily disabled.

Classification of the compliance rule by its governing authority or purpose. Regulatory rules are mandated by law, platform policies are vendor-specific, industry standards are self-regulatory, brand safety rules protect advertiser reputation, privacy rules govern data usage, and content restrictions limit creative content.
reference: IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau) Compliance Framework; MRC (Media Rating Council) Standards

description: Indicates whether this compliance rule requires third-party verification or certification (e.g., TAG certification, MRC accreditation, IAS or DoubleVerify brand safety verification).
reference: TAG (Trustworthy Accountability Group); MRC (Media Rating Council); IAB (Interactive Advertising Bureau)

description: The date and time when this compliance rule record was last modified in the system.
The name of the third-party verification vendor or service provider used to validate compliance with this rule (e.g., Integral Ad Science, DoubleVerify, MOAT, Comscore).
reference: TAG (Trustworthy Accountability Group); MRC (Media Rating Council)

description: The minimum viewability percentage required by this compliance rule for an ad impression to be counted as valid, per MRC (Media Rating Council) standards (e.g., 50% of pixels in view for 1 second for display, 2 seconds for video).

The system action taken when this compliance rule is violated during campaign trafficking or creative approval. Block prevents the action, warn notifies the user but allows override, log records the violation silently, and escalate routes to compliance review.

Unique surrogate identifier for this allocation record linking a work package to a media plan
Foreign key linking to the media plan receiving allocated work effort and budget from the work package
Foreign key linking to the work package providing allocated effort and budget to the media plan

Actual labor hours expended from the work package on this media plan. Tracked from time entry systems where workers log hours against both work package and media plan. Used for effort variance analysis and productivity metrics.

Actual cost incurred from the work package allocated to this media plan. Derived from work_package.actual_cost multiplied by allocation_percentage. Used for cost variance analysis and billing attribution.

Planned labor hours from the work package allocated to this media plan. Derived from work_package.planned_effort_hours multiplied by allocation_percentage. Used for resource capacity planning and utilization tracking.

Current billing lifecycle status of this allocation. Tracks whether the allocated work is billable to the client and the invoicing state. Values: not_billable (internal overhead), billable (ready to invoice), billed (included in draft invoice), invoiced (sent to client), paid (payment received).
Timestamp when this allocation record was created in the system. Used for audit trail and allocation history tracking.

Date when this work package allocation to the media plan concludes. May differ from work_package.planned_end_date if the allocation is time-bounded to a specific phase of the media plan (e.g., planning phase only, not buying phase).
Timestamp when this allocation record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Free-form text field for additional context about this allocation, including rationale for allocation percentage, special billing arrangements, scope clarifications, or cross-charging instructions.

Percentage of the work package effort and cost allocated to this specific media plan. Enables proportional distribution when one work package supports multiple media plans. Sum of allocation_percentage across all media plans for a given work package should equal 100.

Planned cost amount from the work package budget allocated to this media plan. Derived from work_package.planned_cost multiplied by allocation_percentage. Used for budget planning and cost forecasting at the media plan level.

Date when this work package allocation to the media plan becomes active. May differ from work_package.planned_start_date if the work package supports the media plan only during a portion of its lifecycle (e.g., work package spans Q1-Q2 but only allocated to this media plan during Q1).
Unique surrogate identifier for each audience rating record in the media vehicle measurement dataset. Primary key for the audience_rating data product.
reference: IAB Audience Measurement Standards; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: Audience ratings measure delivery against specific demographic and behavioral segments for GRP/TRP calculation. Buyers use segment-linked ratings for media planning, buying, and post-campaign delivery
Reference to the publisher or media owner associated with this rated vehicle. Used to aggregate ratings across a publisher's portfolio.
reference: IAB Tech Lab Publisher Standards; Comscore Media Metrix

description: Reference to the media vehicle (network, station, site, or publication) for which this audience rating applies. Links to the media vehicle master record.
reference: IAB Tech Lab Media Taxonomy; Nielsen Station Index

description: Upper bound of the age range for the demographic cell (e.g., 49 for the A18-49 cell). A null value indicates an open-ended upper bound (e.g., '65+').
reference: Nielsen Audience Measurement; Comscore Unified Digital Measurement

description: Lower bound of the age range for the demographic cell (e.g., 18 for the A18-49 cell). Enables precise demographic filtering and TRP validation in media planning.
reference: Nielsen Audience Measurement; Comscore Unified Digital Measurement

description: Average number of persons (in thousands) tuned to or engaged with the media vehicle during the average minute of the measured time slot. The primary audience delivery metric used in GRP/TRP calculations and media plan validation.
reference: Nielsen Average Audience Methodology; MRC Minimum Standards for Audience Measurement

description: Statistical confidence interval (expressed as a decimal, e.g., 0.95 for 95%) associated with the rating estimate, reflecting measurement precision. Used by media planners to assess reliability of ratings for planning and reconciliation decisions.
reference: MRC Minimum Standards for Audience Measurement; Nielsen Statistical Methodology; Comscore Measurement Accuracy Standards

description: Total unduplicated number of persons (in thousands) who were exposed to the media vehicle at least once during the measurement period. Also known as 'reach' or 'cume'. Used in reach and frequency planning.
reference: Nielsen Cume Audience Methodology; Comscore Reach Measurement; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: The audience measurement currency in which this rating is expressed (e.g., 'grp' for Gross Rating Points, 'trp' for Target Rating Points, 'impressions' for digital impression-based measurement). Aligns with IAB and MRC currency standards for cross-media planning.
reference: IAB Cross-Media Audience Measurement Standards; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines; Nielsen One Currency

description: The date on which this specific version of the rating data was published or released by the measurement source. Enables tracking of data revisions and ensures media planners are using the most current ratings vintage for planning and reconciliation.
reference: Nielsen Data Release Calendar; Comscore Data Vintage Standards; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: Standardized code identifying the daypart or time slot for which the rating is measured (e.g., 'PRIME', 'EARLY_NEWS', 'LATE_FRINGE', 'MORNING_DRIVE'). Aligns with Nielsen and MRC daypart definitions used in media planning and GRP/TRP calculations.
reference: Nielsen Daypart Definitions; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines; IAB Video Ad Spend Report

description: Human-readable label for the daypart or time slot (e.g., 'Prime Time', 'Early Morning', 'Late Night'). Used in media planning reports and client-facing deliverables.
reference: Nielsen Daypart Definitions; Mediaocean Prisma Media Planning Standards

description: Gender component of the demographic cell for which the rating is reported. 'persons' and 'all' indicate total audience regardless of gender, consistent with Nielsen P2+ and Comscore total audience reporting.
reference: Nielsen Audience Measurement Demographic Standards; Comscore Unified Digital Measurement; IAB Audience Taxonomy

description: Standardized code identifying the specific geographic market for this rating (e.g., Nielsen DMA code '501' for New York, MSA FIPS code, or 'US' for national). Used to match ratings to media plan geographic targets.
reference: Nielsen DMA Code List; US Census FIPS Codes; IAB Audience Taxonomy

description: Human-readable name of the geographic market for this rating (e.g., 'New York', 'Los Angeles', 'National'). Used in media planning reports and client-facing deliverables.
reference: Nielsen DMA Market Definitions; Comscore Geographic Reporting

description: The geographic scope of the audience measurement (e.g., 'national' for national ratings, 'dma' for Nielsen Designated Market Area, 'msa' for Metropolitan Statistical Area). Determines the applicable universe estimate and planning geography.
reference: Nielsen DMA Definitions; MRC Geographic Measurement Standards; IAB Audience Taxonomy

description: The Gross Rating Point (GRP) value contributed by a single exposure on this vehicle in this daypart for the total audience demographic. GRP = Rating × Number of Spots. Stored at the unit level to support media plan GRP accumulation and delivery validation.
reference: IAB GRP Standards; Nielsen GRP Methodology; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: Timestamp when this audience rating record was first ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer from the source measurement system (Nielsen Ad Intel or Comscore). Supports data lineage and audit trail for the lakehouse pipeline.
reference: MRC Data Provenance Requirements; IAB Data Quality Standards

description: Flag indicating that this rating record has been identified as statistically unreliable due to insufficient sample size, high margin of error, or measurement source quality flags. Low-reliability ratings should not be used for billing reconciliation without additional validation.
reference: MRC Minimum Standards for Audience Measurement; Nielsen Data Quality Flags; Comscore Data Quality Standards

description: The data collection methodology used by the measurement source to produce this rating (e.g., 'panel' for Nielsen People Meter, 'census' for Comscore digital census tags, 'hybrid' for blended panel+census). Affects data quality and comparability across sources.
reference: MRC Minimum Standards for Audience Measurement; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines; Comscore Unified Digital Measurement

description: End date of the audience measurement sweep or reporting period during which this rating was collected. Together with measurement_period_start, defines the temporal scope of the rating data.
reference: Nielsen Sweeps Calendar; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines; Mediaocean Prisma Reconciliation Standards

description: Start date of the audience measurement sweep or reporting period during which this rating was collected (e.g., Nielsen sweeps period start). Used to align ratings data with campaign flight dates for GRP/TRP delivery validation.
reference: Nielsen Sweeps Calendar; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines; Mediaocean Prisma Reconciliation Standards

description: The third-party audience measurement provider that produced this rating data (e.g., 'nielsen', 'comscore'). Determines the methodology, panel, and accreditation status applicable to this record. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: nielsen|comscore|arbitron|mri_simmons|kantar|doubleclick — promote to reference product]
reference: MRC Accreditation Standards; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines; TAG Certified Measurement

description: Indicates whether the measurement source and methodology for this rating record hold current Media Rating Council (MRC) accreditation. MRC-accredited data is the industry standard for media buying and billing reconciliation.
reference: MRC Accreditation Standards; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: The delivery platform for which this audience rating applies (e.g., 'linear_tv', 'streaming', 'digital_video', 'audio'). Supports cross-platform audience deduplication and unified reach planning across CTV/OTT, linear, and digital channels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: linear_tv|streaming|digital_display|digital_video|audio|print|ooh|dooh — promote to reference product]
reference: IAB Cross-Media Audience Measurement Standards; MRC Cross-Platform Measurement; Comscore Cross-Platform Reporting

description: Lifecycle status of the rating record indicating whether the data is preliminary (initial estimate), final (confirmed), revised (updated after initial release), or superseded (replaced by a newer vintage). Critical for media reconciliation — only 'final' ratings are used for billing reconciliation.
reference: Nielsen Data Release Standards; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines; Mediaocean Prisma Reconciliation

description: The audience rating expressed as a percentage of the total universe that viewed/listened to/visited the media vehicle during the measured time slot and demographic cell. A rating of 5.25 means 5.25% of the universe was exposed. Core metric for GRP and TRP delivery validation.
reference: Nielsen Audience Measurement; MRC Minimum Standards for Audience Measurement; IAB Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: Number of panel respondents or measurement units contributing to this rating estimate. Smaller sample sizes indicate lower statistical reliability; used to apply confidence intervals and flag low-reliability ratings in media planning.
reference: MRC Minimum Standards for Audience Measurement; Nielsen Sample Size Reporting; Comscore Methodology Documentation

description: Percentage of the Households Using Television (HUT) or Persons Using Television (PUT) that were tuned to this vehicle during the time slot. Share = (Average Audience / HUT or PUT) × 100. Used alongside rating to assess competitive performance.
reference: Nielsen Audience Share Methodology; MRC Minimum Standards for Audience Measurement

description: End time of the measured time slot in HH:MM (24-hour) format (e.g., '23:00' for 11 PM). Defines the close of the measurement window.
reference: Nielsen Audience Measurement; MRC Minimum Standards for Audience Measurement

description: Start time of the measured time slot in HH:MM (24-hour) format (e.g., '20:00' for 8 PM). Used to precisely define the measurement window within a daypart.
reference: Nielsen Audience Measurement; MRC Minimum Standards for Audience Measurement

description: The Target Rating Point (TRP) value contributed by a single exposure on this vehicle for the specific demographic cell. TRP = Target Demographic Rating × Number of Spots. Used to validate targeted audience delivery against media plan objectives.
reference: IAB TRP Standards; Nielsen TRP Methodology; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: Total estimated population size (in persons) for the demographic cell and geography against which the rating is calculated. Used as the denominator in rating calculations and for projecting absolute audience size from rating percentages.
reference: Nielsen Universe Estimates; Comscore Unified Digital Measurement; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this audience rating record, including revisions from the measurement source or corrections applied during reconciliation. Used to track data freshness and identify revised ratings vintages.
reference: MRC Data Provenance Requirements; IAB Data Quality Standards

description: Classification of the media vehicle by channel type. Determines which measurement methodology and rating currency applies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: broadcast_tv|cable_tv|radio|digital|print|ooh|ctv_ott|podcast — promote to reference product]
reference: IAB Tech Lab Media Taxonomy; MRC Cross-Media Audience Measurement Standards

description: Planned percentage of the target audience expected to be exposed to the ad at least once during the flight period. Key media planning metric used alongside frequency to evaluate campaign effectiveness and GRP decomposition.
reference: Nielsen Audience Measurement; MRC Reach and Frequency Standards; 4As Media Planning Guidelines

description: Total planned gross media spend allocated to this line item for the full flight period, in the plan currency. Primary budget field used for financial planning, client billing, and P&L management. LINE_VALUE_OR_RESULT per TRANSACTION_LINE canonical role.
reference: Mediaocean Prisma — Planned Cost; SAP S/4HANA PS Budget Management; 4As Financial Standards

description: Planned Target Rating Points (TRPs) for this line item, representing audience delivery specifically among the defined target demographic as a percentage of that target population. More precise than GRPs for audience-specific campaign planning.
reference: Nielsen TRP Measurement Standards; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: The planned quantity of media units to be purchased for this line item. Unit interpretation depends on buying_type: impressions for CPM, clicks for CPC, conversions for CPA, spots for broadcast, panels for OOH. LINE_QUANTITY per TRANSACTION_LINE canonical role.
reference: Mediaocean Prisma — Planned Units; MRC Impression Measurement Guidelines

description: Name of the media owner or publisher selling the inventory for this line item (e.g., 'WarnerMedia', 'Google', 'Clear Channel'). Used for vendor management, spend reporting, and competitive analysis.
reference: Mediaocean Prisma — Vendor/Publisher Field; TAG Publisher Registry

description: Name of the specific media property or publisher vehicle where the ad will run (e.g., 'CNN.com', 'NBC Nightly News', 'Times Square Billboard', 'Spotify'). Core planning field used in insertion orders and vendor negotiations.
reference: Mediaocean Prisma — Vehicle Field; Nielsen Ad Intel

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each Share of Voice (SOV) benchmark record in the media planning reference master.

Foreign key linking to media.ad_channel. Business justification: sov_benchmark currently has channel_type as a STRING attribute. SOV benchmarks capture Share of Voice and competitive media spending intelligence for specific advertising channels (e.g., SOV in Displa
Reference to the advertiser or brand whose SOV position is being benchmarked. Links to the advertiser master in the client domain.

Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: Share-of-voice benchmarks are measured and reported against specific audience segments for competitive analysis. Planners use segment-level SOV data to inform budget allocation and channel strategy de

Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: SOV benchmarks measure brand performance against competitive set. Brand profile provides SOV tracking configuration, competitive set definition, and target SOV percentages. Core to SOV reporting, comp
Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this SOV benchmark record, used for cross-system referencing in Nielsen Ad Intel, Comscore, and Mediaocean Prisma.
reference: IAB Data Standards; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines

description: The date on which this SOV benchmark record was captured or published. Represents the as-of date for the competitive intelligence snapshot, distinct from the measurement period dates.
reference: MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines; IAB Data Standards

description: Human-readable label for this benchmark record, typically describing the brand, channel, and measurement period (e.g., 'Acme Brand — Paid Search — Q1 2024 — National').

Current lifecycle status of this SOV benchmark record. Active = current reference used in planning; Superseded = replaced by a newer benchmark; Draft = pending validation; Archived = historical record; Under Review = flagged for data quality review.
reference: IAB Data Standards; MRC Data Quality Guidelines

description: Comma-delimited list of individual competitor brand names included in the defined competitor set for this SOV benchmark. Confidential competitive intelligence sourced from Nielsen Ad Intel or Pathmatics.
Label identifying the defined group of competing brands or advertisers included in the SOV calculation (e.g., 'Tier-1 Auto Competitors — Luxury Segment'). Confidential competitive intelligence.

Qualitative confidence rating for the accuracy of the SOV and spend estimates in this benchmark record. Reflects data source quality, sample size, and methodology robustness. High = MRC-accredited source with full panel; Indicative = modeled or extrapolated estimate.
reference: MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines; IAB Measurement Standards

description: Numeric confidence score (0.00–100.00) quantifying the statistical reliability of the SOV benchmark estimate. Complements the qualitative confidence_level field with a precise measure for analytics and model inputs.
reference: MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines; IAB Measurement Standards

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the SOV benchmark is measured (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN).
Timestamp when this SOV benchmark record was first created in the system. Audit trail field conforming to the yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX format.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); IAB Data Standards

description: The primary data source or platform from which the SOV and competitive spend data was obtained. Drives confidence assessment and methodology notes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: nielsen_ad_intel|comscore|pathmatics|sensor_tower|kantar|mediaocean_prisma|internal_estimate|other — promote to reference product]
reference: MRC Accreditation Standards; Nielsen Ad Intel; Comscore Methodology

description: Nielsen-assigned three-digit numeric code for the Designated Market Area (DMA) associated with this SOV benchmark. Use '000' for national/non-DMA benchmarks.
reference: Nielsen DMA Code Registry; MRC Geographic Standards

description: Estimated total media spend (in USD) for the advertiser's brand within the defined channel, market, and measurement period. Sourced from Nielsen Ad Intel, Comscore, or Pathmatics/Sensor Tower competitive intelligence.
reference: Nielsen Ad Intel Methodology; Comscore Ad Metrix; IAB Data Standards

description: Estimated total media spend (in USD) for all competitors in the defined competitor set within the channel, market, and measurement period. Sourced from competitive intelligence platforms.
reference: Nielsen Ad Intel Methodology; Comscore Ad Metrix; 4As Competitive Intelligence Guidelines

description: The Gross Rating Point (GRP) benchmark value for the advertiser's brand within the defined channel, market, and measurement period. Used for broadcast and video channel SOV benchmarking alongside spend-based SOV.
reference: MRC GRP Measurement Standards; Nielsen GRP Methodology; IAB

description: The advertiser's share of total available impressions within the defined channel, market, and measurement period. Particularly relevant for digital channels (paid search, display) as a complement to spend-based SOV.
reference: IAB Impression Measurement Guidelines; MRC Digital Measurement Standards; Google Campaign Manager 360

description: Indicates whether this SOV benchmark record has been validated and approved by a media strategist or analytics team member before use in client-facing media planning recommendations.
reference: MRC Data Quality Guidelines; 4As Quality Assurance Standards

description: Name of the geographic market or Nielsen Designated Market Area (DMA) for which the SOV benchmark applies (e.g., 'New York DMA', 'National', 'Los Angeles DMA').
reference: Nielsen DMA Definitions; MRC Geographic Standards

description: Granularity of the measurement period for this SOV benchmark (e.g., weekly, monthly, quarterly, annual, or custom date range).
reference: MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines; IAB Measurement Standards

description: Free-text description of the measurement methodology, data collection approach, or estimation technique used to derive the SOV and spend figures. Captures caveats, panel definitions, or extrapolation methods for transparency.
reference: MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines; IAB Measurement Standards

description: Last date of the measurement period covered by this SOV benchmark record. Together with period_start_date defines the competitive intelligence window.
First date of the measurement period covered by this SOV benchmark record. Used to align competitive spend intelligence with media planning cycles.

The media planning cycle or fiscal period for which this benchmark is intended to inform investment decisions (e.g., 'FY2024', 'Q2 2024', 'H1 2024'). Links the benchmark to the relevant planning horizon.
reference: 4As Media Planning Standards; ANA Media Planning Guidelines

description: The IAB or Nielsen-defined product or service category within which the SOV benchmark is measured (e.g., 'Automotive — Luxury Vehicles', 'Quick Service Restaurants'). Defines the competitive universe.
reference: IAB Content Taxonomy; Nielsen Product Category Definitions

description: The media budget (in spend_currency_code) recommended by media strategists to achieve the sov_target_pct for the advertiser's brand in the defined channel and market. Derived from competitive intelligence and used in client budget justification.
reference: 4As Media Planning Standards; ANA Media Planning Guidelines; Mediaocean Prisma

description: The measured or estimated Share of Voice (SOV) percentage for the advertiser's brand within the defined competitor set, channel, and market for the measurement period. Expressed as a percentage (0.0000–100.0000). Core metric driving media budget allocation recommendations.
reference: IAB SOV Measurement Standards; MRC Audience Measurement Guidelines; ANA

description: The planned or recommended SOV percentage target set by media strategists for the advertiser's brand during the planning phase. Used to justify budget recommendations and measure gap versus current SOV.
reference: IAB SOV Measurement Standards; ANA Media Planning Guidelines

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all spend figures in this benchmark record (estimated_brand_spend, estimated_competitor_spend, total_category_spend). Defaults to USD for US markets.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IAB Data Standards

description: Estimated total media spend (in USD) across all advertisers in the product/service category within the channel, market, and measurement period. Denominator used to calculate SOV percentage.
reference: Nielsen Ad Intel Methodology; Comscore Ad Metrix; IAB Data Standards

description: The Target Rating Point (TRP) benchmark value for the advertiser's brand, scoped to the defined target audience segment. Complements GRP by focusing on the specific audience rather than the total population.
reference: MRC TRP Measurement Standards; Nielsen TRP Methodology; IAB

description: Timestamp when this SOV benchmark record was last modified. Supports data lineage tracking and audit compliance in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); IAB Data Standards

description: Name or employee identifier of the media strategist or analytics team member who validated and approved this SOV benchmark record for use in planning.
reference: 4As Quality Assurance Standards; MRC Data Quality Guidelines

description: Date on which this SOV benchmark record was verified and approved by the responsible media strategist or analytics team member.
reference: 4As Quality Assurance Standards; MRC Data Quality Guidelines

description: Unique identifier for the attribution model configuration. Primary key.
Reference to the advertiser for whom this attribution model is configured.

Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Attribution models provided by third-party platforms (Google Analytics, Adobe Analytics, Neustar, Visual IQ). Needed for licensing management, support escalation, model validation, platform fee alloca
Username or identifier of the user who approved this attribution model configuration for activation. Null if not yet approved.
Timestamp when this attribution model configuration was approved for activation. Null if not yet approved.
Detailed business description of the attribution model configuration, including rationale, use cases, and any special rules or exclusions.

Current lifecycle status of the attribution model configuration. Active models are applied to conversion events; inactive models are retained for historical reference; draft models are under development; archived models are deprecated; testing models are in A/B comparison.

Defines which marketing channels this attribution model includes in the customer journey. May be a comma-separated list of channel codes (e.g., 'display,search,social,video') or 'all' for universal application.
Number of days after a click during which a conversion can be attributed to that click event. Standard values range from 1 to 90 days.
reference: IAB Attribution Standards; MRC Measurement Guidelines

description: Identifier linking this attribution model to an A/B testing or incrementality testing group for model comparison workflows. Null if not part of a comparison test.
Defines which conversion events this attribution model applies to. May be a comma-separated list of conversion event types (e.g., 'purchase,lead_form,signup') or 'all' for universal application.
Timestamp when this attribution model configuration record was first created in the system.
Indicates whether the attribution model tracks and attributes conversions across multiple marketing channels (display, search, social, video, email, OOH, CTV, etc.).
Indicates whether the attribution model uses cross-device identity graph to track and attribute conversions across multiple devices (desktop, mobile, tablet, CTV) for the same user.
For data-driven (algorithmic) attribution models, the version identifier of the machine learning algorithm or model used to calculate fractional credit distribution. Null for rule-based models.
For data-driven attribution models, the minimum number of conversions required in the training dataset for the model to be statistically valid. Null for rule-based models.
reference: IAB Attribution Standards; MRC Measurement Guidelines

description: For data-driven attribution models, the minimum number of unique customer journey paths required in the training dataset for the model to be statistically valid. Null for rule-based models.
reference: IAB Attribution Standards; MRC Measurement Guidelines

description: For data-driven attribution models, the end date of the historical data period used to train the machine learning model. Null for rule-based models.
For data-driven attribution models, the start date of the historical data period used to train the machine learning model. Null for rule-based models.
The date on which this attribution model configuration ceases to be active. Null for open-ended models.
The date from which this attribution model configuration becomes active and begins applying to conversion events.
Maximum number of days to look back in the customer journey when attributing conversions. Defines the outer boundary for all touchpoint consideration.
Short alphanumeric code or identifier for the attribution model used in reporting and system integration.
Business-friendly name for the attribution model configuration (e.g., 'Q1 2024 Last-Click Model', 'Brand Awareness Linear Model').

The type of attribution methodology applied: last-click (100% credit to final touchpoint), first-click (100% credit to initial touchpoint), linear (equal credit across all touchpoints), time-decay (exponentially increasing credit toward conversion), position-based (U-shaped: 40% first, 40% last, 20% distributed), or data-driven (algorithmic/machine-learning-based credit distribution).
reference: IAB Attribution Standards; Google Analytics Attribution Models

description: Username or identifier of the user who last modified this attribution model configuration.
Timestamp when this attribution model configuration record was last modified.
For position-based (U-shaped) attribution models, the fractional credit weight assigned to the first touchpoint (e.g., 0.4000 for 40%). Null for non-position-based models.
For position-based (U-shaped) attribution models, the fractional credit weight assigned to the last touchpoint (e.g., 0.4000 for 40%). Null for non-position-based models.
For position-based (U-shaped) attribution models, the fractional credit weight distributed equally among all middle touchpoints (e.g., 0.2000 for 20% total). Null for non-position-based models.
Indicates whether historical conversion events within the attribution window need to be reprocessed due to changes in this model configuration.
The earliest date from which historical conversion events should be reprocessed when model configuration changes. Null if no reprocessing is required.
For time-decay attribution models, the number of days at which a touchpoint receives half the credit of the most recent touchpoint. Defines the exponential decay rate. Null for non-time-decay models.
Number of days after an impression (view) during which a conversion can be attributed to that impression event, even without a click. Standard values range from 1 to 30 days.
reference: IAB Attribution Standards; MRC Measurement Guidelines

description: Username or identifier of the user who created this attribution model configuration.
Unique identifier for this attribution model platform configuration record. Primary key.

business_glossary_term: Model Platform Configuration - Attribution Model Id
description: Foreign key linking to the attribution model configuration being deployed on this platform

business_glossary_term: Model Platform Configuration - Tech Partner Id
description: Foreign key linking to the technology platform partner hosting this attribution model

Current operational status of the API integration between the attribution model and this specific tech partner platform. Values: active, inactive, degraded, maintenance, failed, pending. Used for operational monitoring and alerting.

Lifecycle status of this model-platform configuration. Values: active (in production use), inactive (disabled), testing (pilot/QA), deprecated (scheduled for removal). Governs whether this configuration is used for live attribution processing.
Timestamp when this model-platform configuration record was first created in the system.

Frequency at which conversion and touchpoint data is synchronized between the tech partner platform and the attribution model processing engine. Values: real-time, hourly, daily, weekly, on-demand. Impacts attribution latency and reporting freshness.
Timestamp of the most recent successful data synchronization between the tech partner platform and the attribution model. Used for monitoring integration health and data freshness.

Licensing tier or service level for this specific model-platform combination. Values: basic, standard, premium, enterprise. Determines feature availability, data volume limits, and support entitlements.
Date when this attribution model was first deployed and activated on this specific tech partner platform. Used for tracking model lifecycle and historical analysis.
Version of the technology platform on which this attribution model is deployed (e.g., Google Analytics 4 v2.1, Adobe Analytics 2.8). Critical for compatibility tracking and troubleshooting.

Service level agreement tier for technical support and issue resolution for this model-platform configuration. Values: bronze, silver, gold, platinum. Defines response times and escalation procedures.
Timestamp of the most recent update to this model-platform configuration record.

business_glossary_term: Performance Key Performance Indicator (KPI) Target Identifier
description: Unique identifier for the performance KPI target record. Primary key for this entity.
Reference to the campaign for which this KPI target is defined. Links to the campaign master entity.

Foreign key linking to vendor.vendor_io_line. Business justification: KPI targets often set at IO line level per vendor contracts (guaranteed viewability, CTR floors, completion rates). Needed to track contractual performance guarantees, trigger makegood clauses, and ev
Reference to the specific line item within a media plan or insertion order for which this KPI target applies. Nullable if target is set at campaign or placement level.
reference: IAB Digital Video Ad Serving Template (VAST)

description: Reference to the specific media placement for which this KPI target is defined. Nullable if target is set at campaign or line item level.
Boolean flag indicating whether automated alerts should be triggered when performance deviates from target thresholds. True = alerts active, False = monitoring only without alerts.
Frequency at which performance is evaluated against thresholds and alerts are generated. Real-time for critical KPIs, daily or weekly for less time-sensitive metrics.
Username or identifier of the user who approved this KPI target for activation. Nullable if approval workflow is not required or target is still in draft status.
Timestamp when this KPI target was approved for activation. Nullable if approval workflow is not required or target is still in draft status.
The attribution model used for conversion-based KPIs (CPA, ROAS, conversion_rate). Defines how credit is assigned across touchpoints in the customer journey.
Timestamp when this KPI target record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.

Boolean flag indicating whether this KPI target represents a contractual commitment to the client (typically from Insertion Order or Statement of Work). True = contractual obligation, False = internal goal or aspiration.

The type of performance metric being targeted. CPM = Cost Per Mille (thousand impressions), CPC = Cost Per Click, CPA = Cost Per Acquisition, CTR = Click-Through Rate, ROAS = Return on Ad Spend, VTR = View-Through Rate, GRP = Gross Rating Point, TRP = Target Rating Point, SOV = Share of Voice. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CPM|CPC|CPA|CTR|ROAS|VTR|viewability_rate|GRP|TRP|SOV|conversion_rate|engagement_rate|completion_rate|brand_lift — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: IAB Performance Measurement Guidelines; MRC Viewability Standards

description: Number of days after ad exposure during which conversions are attributed to the campaign for conversion-based KPIs. Standard windows are 1, 7, 14, 30, or 90 days.
reference: IAB Attribution Standards; MRC Measurement Guidelines

description: The minimum acceptable value for this KPI. Performance below this threshold triggers alerts or corrective actions. Calculated as target_value minus tolerance band or set explicitly.
Description of how this KPI is measured and calculated, including data sources, attribution models, and calculation logic. Ensures consistency in performance evaluation.
reference: MRC Measurement Standards; IAB Measurement Guidelines

description: Username or identifier of the user who last modified this KPI target record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Timestamp when this KPI target record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Free-text field for additional context, assumptions, or special instructions related to this KPI target. May include client expectations, historical context, or measurement caveats.
Business priority assigned to this KPI target. Critical targets require immediate attention when thresholds are breached; low priority targets are monitored but may not trigger immediate action.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary KPI targets (CPM, CPC, CPA, ROAS). Nullable for non-monetary KPIs.
Human-readable name or label for this KPI target record, used for identification and reporting purposes.
The end date of the period for which this KPI target is applicable. Used for time-bound performance measurement and pacing analysis.
The start date of the period for which this KPI target is applicable. Used for time-bound performance measurement and pacing analysis.

The origin or basis for this KPI target. Insertion Order (IO) commitment represents contractual obligation, client brief represents client expectation, internal benchmark represents agency standard, historical average represents past performance baseline.
reference: IAB Insertion Order Standards; Internal Performance Management Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the KPI target. Draft = under definition, active = in measurement period, suspended = temporarily paused, achieved = target met, missed = target not met, expired = measurement period ended.

The unit of measure for the target value. Currency for cost-based KPIs (CPM, CPC, CPA), percentage for rate-based KPIs (CTR, VTR, viewability_rate), ratio for ROAS, points for GRP/TRP, count for absolute metrics.
The numeric target value for the specified KPI. Interpretation depends on kpi_type (e.g., dollar amount for CPM/CPC/CPA, percentage for CTR/VTR/viewability_rate, ratio for ROAS, points for GRP/TRP).

Acceptable variance percentage from the target value before triggering alerts or escalations. For example, 10.00 means ±10% from target is acceptable. Used for pacing alerts and performance monitoring.

The maximum acceptable value for this KPI (relevant for cost-based metrics where exceeding target is negative). Performance above this threshold triggers alerts. Calculated as target_value plus tolerance band or set explicitly.
The viewability measurement standard applied for viewability_rate KPI targets. MRC standard = 50% of pixels in view for 1 second (display) or 2 seconds (video). GroupM may have stricter requirements.
reference: MRC Viewability Standards; IAB Viewability Guidelines

description: Username or identifier of the user who created this KPI target record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Primary key for the pixel_deployment association
Foreign key linking to the tech partner platform through which this pixel is deployed
Foreign key linking to the tracking pixel being deployed through this tech partner platform
Version of the tech partner API used for this pixel deployment. Critical for compatibility tracking and upgrade planning.
Timestamp when this pixel deployment record was created in the system.
Platform-specific custom parameters or configuration settings for this pixel deployment. Varies by tech partner platform capabilities.
Date when this pixel was deployed through this specific tech partner platform. Tracks when the integration was established.
Environment context for this pixel-platform deployment. Tracks whether this is a production integration or test deployment.
Calculated error rate (0.00000 to 1.00000) for pixel fires through this tech partner platform over the monitoring window. Key vendor performance metric.
Most recent fire status for this pixel through this tech partner platform. Used for real-time health monitoring.
Current operational status of the pixel deployment through this tech partner platform. Used for health monitoring and troubleshooting.
Timestamp of the most recent successful pixel fire through this tech partner platform. Used to detect stale or broken integrations.
Timestamp of the most recent update to this pixel deployment record.
Unique identifier for the tracking pixel or conversion tag record. Primary key.
Reference to the advertiser client whose property or campaign this pixel is deployed on.

Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Tracking pixels are deployed per brand for measurement and must respect brand-specific consent requirements, data governance policies, and brand safety standards. Essential for GDPR/CCPA compliance wh
Reference to the advertising campaign this pixel is associated with for measurement purposes.

Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Tracking pixels are deployed for brand-specific conversion measurement. Brand managers need to audit which pixels are active for their brands, manage brand-specific conversion definitions, and ensure

Foreign key linking to creative.creative_asset. Business justification: Tracking pixels are deployed within specific creative assets for conversion tracking. Trafficking and analytics teams need this link to validate pixel deployment, troubleshoot firing issues, and recon
Reference to the specific media placement or ad unit this pixel is tracking.

Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: SOWs define measurement and tracking requirements as contractual deliverables. Linking pixels to SOWs enables scope validation, ensures deployed tracking matches contracted specifications, and support

Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Tracking pixels are often provided by third-party tag management or analytics vendors (Google Tag Manager, Tealium, Adobe). Required for troubleshooting pixel fires, SLA management, vendor consolidati
Name of the web browser in which the pixel fired, parsed from the user agent string.
Privacy consent category under which this pixel operates, determining when it may fire based on user consent preferences.
Indicates whether user consent is required before the pixel can fire, per privacy regulations.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the conversion value.
Monetary value associated with a conversion event tracked by this pixel, used for ROAS calculation.
Username or identifier of the person who created this tracking pixel configuration.
Date and time when this tracking pixel record was first created in the system.
Additional key-value pairs or query string parameters passed with the pixel fire for custom tracking dimensions.
Unique identifier used to prevent duplicate counting of the same conversion event across multiple pixels or platforms.
reference: IAB Tech Lab Measurement Guidelines; MRC Standards

description: The digital environment or platform type where the pixel is implemented.
Current operational status of the tracking pixel in the measurement infrastructure.
The advertiser web page, app screen, or landing page URL where this pixel is deployed.
Classification of the device on which the pixel fired, derived from user agent analysis.
Technical error code returned when a pixel fire fails, used for troubleshooting and data quality monitoring.
Human-readable description of the error that occurred during pixel fire, providing diagnostic detail.
Outcome status of the pixel fire attempt, indicating whether the tracking event was successfully recorded.
Exact date and time when the pixel fire event occurred, representing the principal business event for this tracking record.
Business logic or condition that determines when the pixel should fire (e.g., page load, button click, form submission, time on page).
City derived from IP geolocation at time of pixel fire.
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code derived from IP geolocation at time of pixel fire.
State, province, or region derived from IP geolocation at time of pixel fire.
Technical method used to deploy and fire the tracking pixel on the target property.
One-way cryptographic hash of the user IP address, stored for fraud detection and geographic analysis while maintaining GDPR compliance.
reference: GDPR Article 32; IAB Tech Lab Measurement Guidelines

description: Number of days after an ad interaction during which a conversion can be attributed to that interaction.
reference: IAB Tech Lab Measurement Guidelines; MRC Standards

description: Username or identifier of the person who last modified this tracking pixel configuration.
Date and time when this tracking pixel record was last updated or modified.
Free-form text field for additional comments, implementation instructions, or troubleshooting notes related to this pixel.
Operating system of the device on which the pixel fired, parsed from the user agent string.
Unique external code or identifier for the pixel used in trafficking and reporting systems.
Human-readable name assigned to the tracking pixel for identification and management purposes.
Classification of the tracking pixel based on its measurement purpose and firing trigger.
Full URL endpoint that the pixel fires to when triggered, including protocol and domain.
The URL of the page that referred the user to the page where the pixel fired, used for traffic source analysis.
Browser or application user agent string captured at the time of pixel fire, used for device and browser classification.
Primary key for auction
Reference to the advertiser whose creative won the auction and will be served.
Reference to the advertising campaign associated with the winning bid in this auction.
Identifier for a private marketplace (PMP) deal or programmatic guaranteed deal. Null for open auction inventory.
Self-referencing FK on auction (fallback_auction_id)
Unique identifier for the ad impression that resulted from this auction. Links auction to downstream delivery and performance events.
Reference to the specific line item within the campaign that participated in or won this auction.
Reference to the ad placement or inventory slot that was auctioned. Represents the specific location where the ad will be displayed.
Reference to the publisher or content owner providing the ad inventory being auctioned.
Creative format of the ad unit being auctioned. Determines technical specifications and user experience.
Dimensions of the ad unit in pixels (e.g., 300x250, 728x90). Standard IAB ad unit sizes or custom dimensions.
Date and time when the auction was resolved and a winning bid was selected or the auction was closed without a winner.
External business identifier for the auction, typically provided by the ad exchange or supply-side platform (SSP). Used for cross-system reconciliation and reporting.
Current lifecycle state of the auction event. Tracks progression from initiation through bid collection to final resolution or failure.
Maximum time in milliseconds allowed for the auction to complete before timing out. Typical values range from 100-500ms.
Precise date and time when the auction event was initiated by the ad exchange or SSP. Represents the real-world business event time for auction start.

Classification of the auction mechanism used for ad inventory pricing. First-price auctions award to highest bidder at their bid price; second-price auctions award at second-highest bid price plus increment.
Total number of bids received during the auction from all participating demand-side platforms (DSPs) and bidders.
Timestamp when this auction record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage and audit purposes.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this auction (winning bid, floor price).
Unique identifier for the end-user device (IDFA, AAID, or hashed identifier). Used for frequency capping and attribution. May be pseudonymized or hashed.
Classification of the device on which the ad impression will be displayed. Critical for targeting and performance analysis.
Reference to the ad exchange or supply-side platform (SSP) that conducted the auction.
Minimum acceptable bid price set by the publisher or exchange for this auction. Bids below this threshold are rejected.
Pre-bid fraud risk score (0-100) indicating likelihood of invalid traffic (IVT). Higher scores indicate higher fraud risk. Provided by fraud detection vendors.
City name derived from the user's location. Used for hyper-local targeting and performance analysis.
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code derived from the user's IP address or device location. Used for geographic targeting and reporting.
Postal or ZIP code derived from the user's location. Used for precise geographic targeting and demographic inference.
State, province, or region within the country derived from the user's location. Used for sub-national geographic targeting.
IP address of the end-user device at the time of the auction. May be IPv4 or IPv6. Used for geo-targeting and fraud detection. May be truncated for privacy compliance.
IAB content category classification of the publisher site or app. Used for contextual targeting and brand safety.
Domain name of the website or app where the ad will be displayed. Used for brand safety and contextual targeting.
Serialized ads.txt or sellers.json supply chain transparency data. Identifies all intermediaries in the ad transaction chain for fraud prevention.
Timestamp when this auction record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.
Browser or application user agent string identifying the software environment of the end user. Used for device detection and compatibility.
Pre-bid predicted probability (0-100%) that the impression will meet viewability standards (MRC: 50% of pixels in view for 1 second for display, 2 seconds for video).
The monetary value of the winning bid in the auction. Represents the clearing price paid by the advertiser for the impression.
Unique identifier for each click event record. Primary key for the click event entity.
reference: IAB Tech Lab - Digital Ad Event Standards

description: Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: Clicks occur within targeted segments - critical for segment-level CTR analysis, engagement measurement, and audience quality scoring. Enables advertisers to identify high-performing segments and opti

Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Click attribution and engagement analysis require brand context for brand-level CTR reporting, brand engagement dashboards, and multi-brand advertiser performance comparison. Standard in enterprise ad
Reference to the advertising campaign under which this click occurred. Enables campaign-level click aggregation and performance analysis.

Foreign key linking to contract.insertion_order. Business justification: CPC-based IOs require click-level tracking for budget consumption and invoice reconciliation. Enables automated billing validation against contracted click rates and caps. Essential for performance-ba
Reference to the creative asset that was clicked. Identifies the specific ad unit, banner, video, or interactive element that received the user interaction.

On-pace means within acceptable variance, under-delivering means behind target, over-delivering means ahead of target, paused means delivery is stopped, completed means flight has ended, at-risk means significant deviation detected.

Result status of the automated data quality validation. Passed indicates data met quality criteria. Failed indicates data did not meet criteria and requires remediation. Warning indicates potential issue requiring review. Skipped indicates check was not executed.

For data quality check discrepancies, specifies the type of automated validation that failed. Completeness checks for missing required fields. Consistency checks for logical contradictions. Deduplication checks for duplicate events. Latency checks for delayed data arrival. Schema validation checks for structural conformance. Referential integrity checks for orphaned records.

Severity classification of the data quality issue. Critical indicates data unusable for billing or reporting. High indicates significant impact on accuracy. Medium indicates moderate impact requiring attention. Low indicates minor issue with minimal business impact.
Date and time when the discrepancy was first detected by automated monitoring systems or manual review. Represents the business event time of discovery.

Absolute difference between primary and secondary counts. Calculated as primary_count minus secondary_count. Positive values indicate over-delivery by secondary source; negative values indicate under-delivery.
Percentage variance between primary and secondary counts. Calculated as (discrepancy_count / primary_count) * 100. Used to assess materiality against MRC 10 percent threshold.

Current lifecycle status of the discrepancy investigation and resolution workflow. Open indicates newly detected. Under investigation indicates active analysis. Resolved indicates root cause identified and remediation applied. Closed indicates final disposition complete. Escalated indicates elevated to senior management or vendor. Pending vendor indicates awaiting external party response.

Classification of the discrepancy. Count variance represents differences between ad server and vendor reported metrics. Data quality check represents automated validation failures. Latency issue represents delayed data delivery. Schema validation represents structural data problems. Deduplication represents duplicate event detection. Completeness check represents missing data detection.

Monetary value of the discrepancy impact in the media buy currency. Calculated based on the discrepancy count multiplied by the contracted CPM, CPC, or CPA rate. Represents the dollar value at stake requiring financial resolution.

Classification of the financial remedy applied to compensate for the measurement discrepancy. Credit represents monetary refund from vendor. Makegood represents additional ad delivery at no cost. Write-off represents agency absorbing the variance. No adjustment represents variance accepted within tolerance. Pending represents resolution not yet determined.

description: Name or identifier of the individual or team assigned to investigate and resolve the discrepancy. Typically a media operations analyst, data quality engineer, or account manager.

The performance metric type where the discrepancy occurred. Impressions represents ad delivery count. Clicks represents user interaction count. Conversions represents completed actions. Viewable impressions represents MRC-compliant viewability count. Video completions represents full video view count. Engagements represents rich media interaction count.

description: Rigidity level of the dependency constraint. Hard: must be enforced, cannot be violated. Soft: should be enforced but can be overridden with approval. Preferred: advisory only, used for optimization.

Classification of the dependency nature. Mandatory: legally or contractually required. Discretionary: based on best practices or preferences. External: involves third parties or external entities. Internal: within the organization's control.

Current state of the dependency relationship. Active: dependency is enforced. Waived: dependency temporarily ignored by project manager approval. Violated: successor started before predecessor completed. Suspended: dependency enforcement paused. Resolved: dependency satisfied and closed.

Logical relationship type between predecessor and successor. Finish-to-start (FS): successor cannot start until predecessor finishes. Start-to-start (SS): successor cannot start until predecessor starts. Finish-to-finish (FF): successor cannot finish until predecessor finishes. Start-to-finish (SF): successor cannot finish until predecessor starts.
reference: PMI PMBOK Guide - Dependency Determination and Integration
