description: Monotonically incrementing version number for the segment definition. Incremented each time segment criteria, thresholds, or scoring methodology are formally revised. Supports historical segment reconstruction and audit trail requirements.
reference: Internal Data Governance Policy; IAB Data Standards

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each competitive conflict declaration record. Primary key for the competitive_conflict data product.
reference: IAB Data Standards; 4As Agency Management Guidelines

description: Reference to the first advertiser (incumbent client) in the competitive conflict pair. Typically the existing Agency of Record (AOR) client whose category exclusivity may be at risk.
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Standards

description: Reference to the account director responsible for managing and resolving this competitive conflict. This individual is accountable for client communications, resolution implementation, and board-level escalation as required.
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; Salesforce CRM Service Cloud

description: Reference to the Workfront project management task or work item tracking the resolution activities for this competitive conflict. Links conflict management to the agency's project management and resource planning workflows.
reference: Workfront Project Management; 4As Agency Management Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Agency of Record (AOR) Contract Clause Reference
description: Reference to the specific clause(s) in the Agency of Record (AOR) contract(s) that govern competitive conflict obligations for this declaration. Used by legal and account management teams to assess contractual exposure and resolution requirements.
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Standards; Agency E&O Insurance Requirements

description: Reference number or identifier for the board-level or senior leadership approval document authorizing the selected resolution approach. Required for 'critical' severity conflicts and account resignations. Links to governance documentation in the agency's document management system.
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; Agency E&O Insurance Requirements; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Standards

description: The date on which Advertiser A (the incumbent client) was formally notified of the competitive conflict declaration. Required for AOR compliance and contractual notification obligations.
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Standards; FTC Advertising Practices Division

description: The date on which Advertiser A formally granted a written waiver accepting the agency's representation of the competing Advertiser B. Null if no waiver was granted or if resolution_approach is not 'client_waiver'.
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Standards

description: The date on which Advertiser B (the challenger or prospective client) was formally notified of the competitive conflict declaration. Required for new business pitch eligibility screening and AOR compliance.
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Standards; FTC Advertising Practices Division

description: The date on which Advertiser B formally granted a written waiver accepting the agency's representation of the competing Advertiser A. Null if no waiver was granted or if resolution_approach is not 'client_waiver'.
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Standards

description: Detailed narrative description of the competitive conflict, including the specific products, services, or campaigns that create the conflict, the business rationale for the conflict classification, and any relevant context for resolution decision-making. Classified as confidential due to sensitive client business information.
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Standards

description: Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to this conflict declaration, used in correspondence with clients, legal counsel, and board-level approval documentation. Format: CC-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Standards

description: Business-assessed severity level of the competitive conflict, used to prioritize resolution actions and escalation paths. 'critical' typically requires board-level approval or account resignation; 'high' requires senior leadership review; 'medium' may be resolved via information barriers; 'low' may be managed with standard team separation.
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the competitive conflict declaration. 'declared' indicates newly identified conflict; 'under_review' indicates active assessment in progress; 'resolved' indicates a resolution approach has been implemented; 'waived' indicates client has granted a formal waiver; 'escalated' indicates board-level or legal escalation; 'expired' indicates the conflict review period has lapsed without resolution.
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; ANA Conflict of Interest Policy Standards

description: Classification of the nature of the competitive conflict between the two advertiser accounts. 'direct_competitor' indicates brands competing for the same customers in the same category; 'indirect_adjacent' indicates brands in adjacent categories with overlapping audiences; 'category_overlap' indicates partial product category overlap; 'channel_overlap' indicates conflict limited to specific media channels; 'geographic_overlap' indicates conflict limited to specific geographic markets. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: direct_competitor|indirect_adjacent|category_overlap|channel_overlap|geographic_overlap — promote to reference product]
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; ANA Conflict of Interest Policy Standards

description: The timestamp when this competitive conflict record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail entry point for the conflict declaration lifecycle.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Accountability); 4As Agency Management Guidelines

description: The unique record identifier from Salesforce CRM (Sales Cloud) where this competitive conflict is tracked as part of the account management and client services workflow. Enables traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record.
reference: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud; 4As Agency Management Guidelines

description: The date on which the competitive conflict was formally declared and entered into the agency's conflict management system. Serves as the start of the conflict review clock for AOR compliance and E&O insurance purposes.
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; FTC Advertising Practices Division; Agency E&O Insurance Requirements

description: The party who formally identified and declared the competitive conflict. 'agency' indicates internal agency conflict screening; 'client_a' or 'client_b' indicates the conflict was raised by one of the affected advertisers; 'legal_counsel' indicates identification by agency legal team; 'new_business_team' indicates identification during pitch eligibility screening.
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Standards

business_glossary_term: Errors and Omissions (E&O) Insurance Notified Flag
description: Indicates whether the agency's Errors and Omissions (E&O) insurance carrier has been formally notified of this competitive conflict declaration. Required for high and critical severity conflicts to preserve insurance coverage.
reference: Agency E&O Insurance Requirements; 4As Agency Management Guidelines

description: Comma-separated list of specific country codes (ISO 3166-1 alpha-3) or market identifiers where the competitive conflict applies when geographic_scope is 'regional', 'national', or 'local'. Example: 'USA,CAN,GBR'. Null when geographic_scope is 'global'.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; IAB Geo Targeting Standards

description: The geographic extent of the competitive conflict. 'global' indicates the conflict applies across all markets; 'regional' indicates conflict is limited to a multi-country region; 'national' indicates conflict is limited to a single country; 'local' indicates conflict is limited to specific cities or DMAs (Designated Market Areas).
reference: IAB Geo Targeting Standards; 4As Agency Management Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Interactive Advertising Bureau (IAB) Content Category Code
description: Product category code from the IAB Content Taxonomy (e.g., IAB2 for Automotive, IAB9-30 for Video Games) that defines the competitive product category in which the conflict exists. Enables standardized category-level conflict screening across the agency portfolio.
reference: IAB Content Taxonomy v3.0; IAB Tech Lab Standards

business_glossary_term: Interactive Advertising Bureau (IAB) Content Category Name
description: Human-readable name of the IAB content taxonomy category corresponding to iab_category_code (e.g., 'Automotive', 'Consumer Electronics'). Provides business-friendly label for reporting and conflict screening dashboards.
reference: IAB Content Taxonomy v3.0; IAB Tech Lab Standards

description: Indicates whether a formal information barrier (Chinese wall) has been implemented between the account teams serving the two conflicting advertisers. True when system access controls, physical separation, and data governance protocols are in place per the resolution approach.
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; GDPR Article 25 (Data Protection by Design); TAG Brand Safety Standards

description: The date on which the agency's legal counsel completed their formal review of the competitive conflict and provided a written opinion or clearance. Null if legal_review_required is False or review is still in progress.
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; FTC Advertising Practices Division

description: Indicates whether this competitive conflict requires formal review by the agency's legal counsel before a resolution approach can be approved. Typically set to True for critical severity conflicts, account resignations, or conflicts involving active litigation.
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; FTC Advertising Practices Division; Agency E&O Insurance Requirements

description: The date on which this competitive conflict declaration is next scheduled for formal review by the account director and relevant stakeholders. Supports ongoing monitoring of resolved conflicts and ensures timely renewal or closure of conflict records.
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Standards

business_glossary_term: New Business Pitch Eligibility Blocked Flag
description: Indicates whether this active competitive conflict blocks the agency from participating in new business pitches involving either of the conflicting advertiser categories. True when the conflict is unresolved and pitch participation would violate AOR obligations or client contracts.
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Standards

description: The approved method selected to manage or resolve the competitive conflict. 'chinese_wall' (information barrier) restricts data and personnel access between account teams; 'client_waiver' indicates the affected client has formally waived the conflict; 'account_resignation' indicates the agency will resign one of the conflicting accounts; 'category_carve_out' limits the agency's scope for one client to exclude the conflicting category; 'team_separation' establishes dedicated, non-overlapping account teams.
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; ANA Conflict of Interest Policy Standards; Agency E&O Insurance Requirements

description: The date on which the competitive conflict was formally resolved, a waiver was granted, or an account resignation was executed. Null if the conflict is still under review or declared but unresolved.
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Standards

description: Free-text notes documenting the resolution process, key decisions, client communications, and any conditions or limitations attached to the approved resolution approach. Classified as confidential due to sensitive client and legal information.
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Standards

description: The date by which the conflict declaration must be reviewed, renewed, or formally closed. Used to trigger automated review reminders and ensure ongoing AOR compliance. Typically set 12 months from declaration_date or per client contract terms.
reference: 4As Agency Management Guidelines; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Standards; Agency E&O Insurance Requirements

description: The timestamp when this competitive conflict record was most recently modified. Tracks the latest update to any field in the conflict declaration, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Accountability); 4As Agency Management Guidelines

description: Primary key for legal_entity
Name of the holding company or agency network to which this entity belongs (e.g., WPP, Omnicom, Publicis, IPG, Dentsu).
Most recent annual revenue reported by the legal entity, used for credit assessment and relationship sizing.
Timestamp when this legal entity record was first created in the system.
External credit rating assigned by a rating agency (e.g., Moody's, S&P, Fitch) for creditworthiness assessment.
Registration number with data protection authorities (e.g., ICO in UK, CNIL in France) for entities processing personal data.
Date on which the legal entity was officially dissolved, merged, or ceased operations, if applicable.
Trade name or fictitious business name under which the legal entity operates, if different from the legal name.
Nine-digit Dun & Bradstreet identifier used for business credit and identity verification.
Approximate number of employees working for the legal entity, used for sizing and segmentation.
Current operational and legal status of the entity in its lifecycle.
Primary street address line for the legal entity's headquarters or principal place of business.
Secondary address line for suite, floor, or building information for the headquarters location.
City or municipality where the legal entity's headquarters is located.
Three-letter ISO country code for the country where headquarters is located.
Postal or ZIP code for the headquarters address.
State, province, or region where the legal entity's headquarters is located.
Three-letter ISO country code representing the country of incorporation.
The date on which the legal entity was officially incorporated or registered with the governing authority.
The country, state, or province where the legal entity is incorporated or registered.
Standard industry classification code representing the entity's primary business activity (e.g., NAICS, SIC, ISIC).
Human-readable description of the industry classification code.
Indicates whether this legal entity operates as an advertising or marketing agency (as opposed to a direct advertiser).
Indicates whether the legal entity is publicly traded on a stock exchange.
Timestamp when this legal entity record was last updated.
The legal structure and organizational form of the entity (e.g., corporation, LLC, partnership).
The official registered legal name of the entity as it appears on incorporation documents and legal contracts.
20-character ISO 17442 Legal Entity Identifier code for entities engaged in financial transactions.
Reference to the parent legal entity if this entity is a subsidiary or part of a larger corporate structure.
Main business email address for official correspondence with the legal entity.
Main business telephone number for contacting the legal entity.
Official government-issued registration or incorporation number assigned to the legal entity by the jurisdiction of incorporation.
Current compliance status with applicable advertising and marketing regulations (e.g., FTC, ASA, GDPR).
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the annual revenue amount.
ISO 10383 Market Identifier Code (MIC) for the primary stock exchange where the entity is listed.
Ticker symbol under which the entity's shares are traded on a stock exchange, if publicly traded.
Tax identifier assigned by the tax authority in the entity's primary jurisdiction (e.g., EIN in USA, VAT number in EU).
Reference to the top-level parent entity in the corporate hierarchy (ultimate beneficial owner entity).
Official website URL for the legal entity.
Unique surrogate identifier for this preferred vendor relationship record
Foreign key linking to the advertiser who has established this preferred vendor relationship
Reference to the negotiated rate card or pricing agreement specific to this advertiser-supplier relationship, capturing custom pricing terms
Foreign key linking to the supplier who is on the advertiser's preferred vendor list
Current approval workflow status for this advertiser-supplier relationship, driving whether the supplier can be selected for campaigns
The advertiser's own brand safety assessment of this supplier, which may differ from the supplier's general brand_safety_tier based on advertiser-specific content policies and risk tolerance
Timestamp when this preferred vendor relationship record was first created in the system
The date from which this preferred vendor relationship became active and the supplier was approved for use by this advertiser
The date until which this preferred vendor relationship remains valid; null indicates ongoing relationship with no defined end date
The minimum spend amount (in advertiser's preferred currency) that the advertiser has committed to with this supplier over the contract period
Indicates whether the supplier has completed all advertiser-specific onboarding requirements (brand guidelines, creative specs, reporting setup, API integration) for this relationship
Strategic tier classification for this advertiser-supplier relationship indicating priority level, volume commitment, and preferential treatment (platinum = highest priority, approved = baseline)
Timestamp when this preferred vendor relationship record was last modified
Unique surrogate identifier for each client revenue target record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.
Reference to the advertiser account for which this revenue target is set. Links to the client.advertiser master record.
reference: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud — Account Object

description: Reference to the employee (typically a Managing Director or Finance Business Partner) who approved this revenue target. Supports audit trail and governance requirements for P&L sign-off.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Authorization; Internal P&L Governance; 4As Financial Management Guidelines

description: Foreign key linking to client.client_brand. Business justification: Revenue targets in advertising agencies are frequently set at brand level within advertiser accounts, not just advertiser-level. Enables brand-specific revenue forecasting, pipeline management, accoun

Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Revenue targets drive budget allocation decisions and resource planning. Account directors set client revenue targets that inform budget approvals, headcount planning, and investment decisions for cli

Reference to the account director or senior account manager who owns and is accountable for achieving this revenue target. Used for performance management, QBR accountability, and Workday HCM talent performance review alignment.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR — Worker Object; Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud — Owner Field

description: The year-over-year or period-over-period percentage growth rate embedded in this target relative to the prior comparable period's target. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1500 = 15%). Used in account growth planning and QBR target-setting rationale.
reference: ANA Agency Compensation Guidelines; Internal Account Growth Planning Framework

description: The date on which the revenue target was formally approved by the designated approver. Marks the transition from pending_approval to approved status and is used in audit trails for P&L governance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Approval Workflow; Internal P&L Governance

description: Account team's assessed confidence in achieving this revenue target: high (strong pipeline, contracted SOW coverage), medium (pipeline in progress, partial SOW coverage), low (speculative, dependent on new business wins). Used in risk-adjusted P&L forecasting.
reference: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud Forecast Category; Internal P&L Governance

business_glossary_term: Contracted Statement of Work (SOW) Coverage Percentage
description: The proportion of the revenue target already covered by signed Statements of Work (SOW) or Insertion Orders (IO) at the time of target setting or last update. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.6500 = 65%). Supports pipeline risk assessment and P&L confidence scoring.
reference: IAB Insertion Order Standards; Mediaocean Prisma SOW Management; ANA Agency Compensation Guidelines

description: SAP S/4HANA CO cost centre code to which this revenue target is allocated for internal P&L management and financial reporting. Enables drill-down from agency-level P&L to individual account team cost centres.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Centre Accounting; GAAP/IFRS Segment Reporting

description: The timestamp when this revenue target record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit trail creation anchor for data lineage and governance.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Accountability); Internal Data Governance Standard

description: The Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud Opportunity record ID linked to this revenue target, where the target originates from or is tracked against a specific sales opportunity. Enables traceability from target to pipeline to closed-won revenue.
reference: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud — Opportunity Object

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the target_amount is denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency advertiser portfolios and cross-market P&L consolidation.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Multi-Currency Configuration

description: The fiscal quarter to which this target applies (Q1–Q4). Populated when target_period_type is 'quarterly'; null for annual targets. Used in QBR preparation and quarterly pacing analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Period; Internal QBR Governance

description: The four-digit fiscal year to which this revenue target applies (e.g., 2025). Aligns with the SAP S/4HANA FI fiscal year calendar for P&L management and budget reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Year Variant; GAAP/IFRS Reporting Period

description: The exchange rate used to convert target_amount to target_amount_usd at the time of target creation or last revision. Stored for auditability and variance analysis when FX rates fluctuate during the target period.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Exchange Rate Table; ISO 4217

description: Indicates whether this target is designated as a stretch (aspirational) target above the base committed target. True = stretch target used for incentive compensation modelling; False = base committed target used for P&L planning. Supports Workday HCM talent performance review and bonus calculation.
reference: Workday HCM Talent Management — Performance Goals; ANA Agency Compensation Guidelines

description: Free-text field for account team commentary on the revenue target, including assumptions, dependencies, client commitments, or QBR talking points. Not used for structured data; supports qualitative context in account growth planning.
reference: 4As Account Management Best Practices; Internal QBR Governance

description: The initial revenue target amount set at the start of the period before any revisions. Preserved for variance analysis comparing original plan vs. revised plan vs. actual billed revenue. Populated only when revision_number > 0.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Budget Version Management; ANA Agency Compensation Guidelines

description: The calendar date on which the revenue target period ends. Defines the boundary for target attainment measurement and QBR reporting cycles.
reference: GAAP/IFRS Revenue Recognition (ASC 606 / IFRS 15); SAP S/4HANA FI

description: The calendar date on which the revenue target period begins. Used to align target pacing with actual billed revenue from the finance domain and to support time-series analytics.
reference: GAAP/IFRS Revenue Recognition (ASC 606 / IFRS 15); SAP S/4HANA FI

description: The total value of identified but not yet contracted revenue opportunities in the Salesforce CRM pipeline that are expected to contribute to this target. Distinct from target_amount (the committed plan) and actual billed revenue. Supports gap-to-target analysis.
reference: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud — Opportunity Pipeline; Internal P&L Governance

description: SAP S/4HANA CO profit centre code associated with this revenue target, enabling segment-level P&L reporting by business unit, geography, or service line. Supports EBITDA reporting and management accounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Profit Centre Accounting; GAAP/IFRS Segment Reporting (IFRS 8)

description: Sequential integer indicating how many times this revenue target has been revised (0 = original). Enables version tracking of target changes throughout the fiscal period, supporting variance analysis between original and revised targets.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Budget Version Management; Internal P&L Governance

description: Free-text explanation for why the revenue target was revised (e.g., client budget reduction, scope expansion, new service line added, market conditions). Supports QBR narrative and P&L variance commentary.
reference: Internal P&L Governance; 4As Account Management Best Practices

description: The target Return on Ad Spend (ROAS) ratio associated with this revenue target, where applicable (primarily for performance-based media service lines). Expressed as a multiplier (e.g., 4.0000 = $4 revenue per $1 ad spend). Used in digital performance optimization and KPI alignment with The Trade Desk programmatic buying.
reference: IAB Performance Marketing Standards; The Trade Desk Bid Management KPIs

description: The agency service line to which this revenue target is attributed: media (Media Planning and Buying), creative (Creative Development and Production), digital (Digital Marketing and Performance Optimization), pr (Public Relations and Communications), analytics (Data Analytics and Audience Insights), strategy (Brand Strategy and Positioning). Enables service-line-level P&L reporting.
reference: 4As Service Line Classification; ANA Agency Compensation Guidelines

description: The gross or net revenue target value set for the advertiser account for the specified period and service line, expressed in the currency defined by currency_code. This is the planned figure; actual billed revenue is owned by the finance domain. Core input for P&L management and account growth planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Profit Center Planning; ANA Agency Compensation Guidelines; GAAP/IFRS ASC 606

description: The revenue target amount converted to US Dollars (USD) using the exchange rate applied at the time of target setting. Enables cross-currency portfolio aggregation and global P&L reporting without requiring runtime FX conversion.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Currency Translation; ISO 4217

description: Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for this revenue target record, used in Quarterly Business Review (QBR) reporting, P&L management, and cross-system reconciliation with SAP S/4HANA CO module.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO — Profit Center Planning; Internal Naming Convention

description: Human-readable label for this revenue target, typically combining the advertiser name, service line, and target period (e.g., 'Acme Corp — Media — FY2025 Q1'). Used in QBR decks and account growth planning documents.

Display name of the account director or account manager who owns this revenue target. Stored denormalized for reporting convenience in QBR decks and P&L dashboards without requiring a join to the talent domain.
reference: 4As Account Management Best Practices; Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud

description: Granularity of the target period: annual (full fiscal year), quarterly (fiscal quarter), or monthly (calendar month). Determines how the target_amount is interpreted and how actuals are paced against it in QBR reporting.
reference: ANA Agency Compensation Guidelines; Internal P&L Governance

description: The methodology used to derive this revenue target: top_down (allocated from agency-level P&L plan), bottom_up (built from individual campaign and SOW estimates), negotiated (agreed bilaterally with client), market_indexed (benchmarked against market growth indices such as Nielsen Ad Intel competitive data).
reference: ANA Agency Compensation Guidelines; Nielsen Ad Intel Market Intelligence

description: Current lifecycle state of the revenue target record. draft: being authored; pending_approval: submitted for sign-off; approved: signed off by account director and finance; active: in-period and being tracked; revised: superseded by a revised target; closed: period ended; cancelled: target withdrawn. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|revised|closed|cancelled — promote to reference product]
reference: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud Opportunity Stage; Internal P&L Governance

description: Classification of the revenue target by income recognition method: gross_revenue (total billings before agency commission deduction), net_revenue (billings net of media costs), fee_income (project or retainer fees), commission_income (media commission earned), retainer_fee (recurring fixed-fee arrangement). Drives P&L line allocation in SAP S/4HANA FI.
reference: IAB Revenue Recognition Guidelines; SAP S/4HANA FI Revenue Accounting

description: The timestamp of the most recent modification to this revenue target record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change detection in ETL pipelines and audit trail completeness.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Accountability); Internal Data Governance Standard

description: Unique surrogate identifier for this advertiser-segment license relationship. Primary key.
Foreign key linking to the advertiser who has licensed or owns access to this segment
Foreign key linking to the audience segment being licensed or accessed

Defines the scope of permissions the advertiser has for this segment: full_access (can view, activate, and export), read_only (can view segment definition and size), activation_only (can target but not view underlying data), reporting_only (can see performance metrics only), restricted (limited by contract terms). Enforced by DSP and DMP access control systems.

Current operational status of this advertiser-segment license: active (segment is available for targeting in campaigns), inactive (license exists but segment not pushed to DSPs), pending_approval (awaiting legal/compliance review), suspended (temporarily disabled due to contract issue or data quality concern), expired (license term ended). Drives segment availability in campaign planning tools.

Reference identifier for the legal contract or data partnership agreement governing this license. Links to contract management system (e.g., DocuSign envelope ID, SAP Ariba contract number). Used for compliance audits and renewal tracking.

Percentage of segment licensing or data acquisition costs allocated to this advertiser, expressed as decimal (0.00 to 100.00). Used for shared segments where multiple advertisers split costs (e.g., co-op segments, agency-negotiated bulk licenses). Null for first-party owned segments with no external cost.
UTC timestamp when this license record was created in the system.

Name of the third-party data provider or partner who supplies this segment (e.g., Nielsen, Experian, LiveRamp, Acxiom). Null for first-party owned segments. Used for vendor management, contract tracking, and data lineage.

Date when this advertiser-segment license expires or is terminated. Null for perpetual licenses (e.g., first-party owned segments). Used to enforce contract terms, auto-deactivate expired third-party data, and trigger renewal workflows.

Date when this advertiser-segment license becomes active and the segment becomes available for targeting by this advertiser. Used for contract start dates, data partnership go-live dates, and segment release schedules.
User ID or email of the person who last modified this license record. Used for audit trail.
UTC timestamp when this license record was last modified.

Total cost amount for this advertiser to license or access this segment, in the currency specified by license_cost_currency. Null for first-party owned segments. Used for cost attribution, budget tracking, and ROI analysis.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the license cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Null if license_cost_amount is null.

Pricing model for this segment license: flat_fee (fixed annual or monthly cost), cpm (cost per thousand impressions), per_activation (cost per campaign activation), revenue_share (percentage of media spend), no_cost (first-party owned or complimentary access).

Classification of the relationship between advertiser and segment: first_party_owned (advertiser owns the underlying data), second_party_licensed (direct data partnership), third_party_licensed (purchased from data vendor), co_op_shared (industry consortium segment), lookalike_derived (modeled from advertiser seed data). Drives cost allocation and data governance policies.
User ID or email of the person who created this advertiser-segment license record. Used for audit trail and accountability.
Unique surrogate identifier for the brand-supplier approval record. Primary key.

business_glossary_term: Brand Supplier Approval - Client Brand Id
description: Foreign key linking to the client brand that has approved this supplier relationship
Foreign key to client.client_brand.client_brand_id
Foreign key linking to the approved supplier for this brand
The committed annual media spend from this brand to this supplier, as negotiated in the commercial agreement. Used for volume-based rate negotiations and supplier relationship management.
The date on which this supplier was approved for this brand. Used for tracking approval history and contract lifecycle.
Current lifecycle status of the brand-supplier approval. Controls whether the supplier is eligible for media buys on behalf of this brand.
Comma-separated list of media channels approved for this brand-supplier relationship (e.g., display, video, social, CTV). A supplier may be approved for different channels for different brands.

Brand-specific safety tier rating for this supplier. Overrides the supplier's default brand_safety_tier when serving this brand. Different brands within the same advertiser may have different safety requirements for the same supplier.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the annual spend commitment (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
The expiration date of the commercial agreement between this brand and supplier. Used for contract renewal tracking.
The effective start date of the commercial agreement between this brand and supplier.
Brand-specific content exclusion keywords or categories for this supplier relationship. Defines content adjacency restrictions beyond the supplier's default brand safety tier.
Free-text notes about this brand-supplier relationship, including special terms, restrictions, or historical context.
Comma-separated list of preferred ad formats for this brand-supplier relationship (e.g., 300x250, 728x90, video_15s, native). Format preferences may vary by brand even for the same supplier.
Name or identifier of the agency account manager responsible for this brand-supplier relationship.
Status of commercial rate negotiation between this brand and supplier. Indicates whether brand-specific rates have been negotiated or standard rates apply.
Unique surrogate identifier for each brief approver record. Primary key.
Foreign key linking to the client contact who is an approver in this approval relationship
Foreign key linking to the creative brief being approved

Ordered list of stakeholders and roles who must review and approve the creative brief and resulting assets, including sequence and approval authority. [Moved from creative_brief: The approval_chain attribute in creative_brief is a denormalized representation of the approval workflow. The proper normalized model stores each approver as a separate record in the brief_approver association with their role, sequence, and status. This allows for operational management of individual approvals rather than storing a static string.]

The functional role this contact plays in the approval process for this specific brief. Defines their approval authority and perspective (e.g., Brand Manager, Legal Reviewer, Finance Approver). Sourced from detection phase relationship data.

The position of this approver in the approval chain workflow. Defines the order in which approvals must be obtained (e.g., 1 = first approver, 2 = second approver). Sourced from detection phase relationship data.
Current status of this specific approver's review of the brief. Tracks whether they have approved, rejected, or are still pending review. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.
Date and time when this approver provided their approval decision. Null if still pending. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.
Target date by which this approver should complete their review. Used to manage approval workflow timing and escalations.
Comments, feedback, or conditions provided by this approver during their review. Captures approval notes, requested changes, or rejection reasons. Sourced from detection phase relationship data.
Indicates whether this approver's approval is mandatory for the brief to proceed to production, or if they are an optional/advisory reviewer.
Date when this approver was notified that the brief is ready for their review. Used to track approval cycle time and SLA compliance.
Unique system-generated identifier for the client brief record. Primary key for the client_brief data product.
Identifier of the client (advertiser or agency) who submitted this brief. Links to the client master record.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Sales Cloud Account Object

description: Foreign key linking to audience.segment. Business justification: Campaign briefs specify target audience segments for media planning and activation. This is the primary mechanism linking client requirements to targetable audiences in advertising operations. Essenti
Identifier of the specific brand within the client portfolio for which this brief is submitted. Supports multi-brand client accounts.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Sales Cloud Account Hierarchy

description: Identifier of the client-side contact who submitted or is the primary owner of this brief. Maps to the CRM contact record.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Sales Cloud Contact Object

description: Foreign key linking to project.initiative. Business justification: Client briefs initiate projects/initiatives that are tracked in project management systems. Currently workfront_project_id is stored as an unlinked string. Normalizing to initiative_id FK enables join

Foreign key linking to talent.worker. Business justification: Client briefs require agency ownership assignment for accountability, workload management, and brief-to-execution handoff tracking. The contact_id tracks client submitter; brief_owner_worker_id tracks

Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Briefs frequently specify preferred suppliers/publishers for campaign execution. Media planners reference approved vendor lists when briefing campaigns. Essential for RFP routing and vendor selection

Foreign key linking to audience.persona. Business justification: Briefs reference personas to guide creative strategy, messaging, and channel selection. Standard practice in advertising where personas inform campaign development beyond just segment targeting. Suppo
Calendar date on which the brief was formally approved by the agency account team and accepted for execution. Null if not yet approved.
reference: Workfront - Approval Date; 4As Agency Operations Standards

description: Version identifier of the client brand guidelines document that must be adhered to for all creative executions under this brief (e.g., 'v3.2', '2024-Q1'). Ensures creative compliance with current brand standards.
reference: Adobe Creative Cloud DAM - Asset Version Control; Workfront DAM

description: Externally-visible, human-readable reference number assigned to the brief at submission (e.g., BRF-2024-000123). Used in client communications, Workfront project creation, and Mediaocean Prisma work orders.
reference: Workfront Project Management - Work Request Numbering; IAB Data Standards

description: Current lifecycle state of the client brief. Drives workflow routing: draft (being authored), submitted (awaiting review), approved (accepted for execution), in_progress (active work underway), on_hold (paused), closed (completed or cancelled).
reference: Workfront - Work Request Status; 4As Agency Operations Standards

description: Classification of the brief by the type of work being requested. Determines the downstream workflow and team assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: campaign|creative|media|pr|digital|brand_strategy — promote to reference product]
reference: 4As Agency Operations Standards; Workfront Work Request Type

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the budget indication amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency client engagements.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Currency Configuration

description: Client-indicated total budget for the campaign or project described in this brief, expressed in the operating currency. This is a directional figure provided at brief stage, not a committed spend. Used for resource planning and scoping.
reference: SAP S/4HANA - PS Project Budget; ANA Budget Management Guidelines

description: Client-requested end date for the campaign or deliverable described in the brief. Defines the desired flight window.
reference: Mediaocean Prisma - Campaign Flight Dates; IAB Campaign Standards

description: Primary marketing objective the client wants to achieve with this campaign or project. Drives KPI selection and channel strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: brand_awareness|lead_generation|sales_conversion|retention|consideration|reach|engagement|app_install — promote to reference product]
reference: IAB Digital Advertising Standards; ANA Marketing Objectives Framework

description: Client-requested start date for the campaign or deliverable described in the brief. Represents the desired go-live date as specified by the client.
reference: Mediaocean Prisma - Campaign Flight Dates; IAB Campaign Standards

description: Client-specified media channels or platforms required or preferred for this campaign (e.g., CTV, DOOH, OTT, social, search, OOH, programmatic). Free-text to capture complex multi-channel requirements.
reference: IAB Channel Taxonomy; Mediaocean Prisma Media Planning Standards

description: Date on which the brief was formally closed, either upon successful completion of all deliverables or cancellation. Null if the brief is still active. Supports lifecycle reporting and pipeline analytics.
reference: Workfront - Project Completion Date; 4As Agency Operations Standards

description: Reason code explaining why the brief was closed. Populated when brief_status transitions to 'closed'. Supports win/loss analysis and client relationship management.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Opportunity Close Reason; 4As Agency Operations Standards

description: Client-provided description of the competitive landscape relevant to this campaign, including key competitors, market positioning, and Share of Voice (SOV) considerations. Confidential business intelligence.
reference: Nielsen Ad Intel Competitive Monitoring; ANA Competitive Intelligence Guidelines

description: System timestamp recording when the client brief record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field aligned with Silver Layer ingestion standards.
reference: GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity; IAB Data Standards

description: Specific creative formats, sizes, or specifications required by the client (e.g., IAB standard banner sizes, VAST video specs, print dimensions). Informs creative production scope.
reference: IAB Ad Unit Guidelines; VAST/VPAID Standards; ASA Creative Standards

description: Client-specified deadline by which the primary deliverable (creative, media plan, strategy deck, etc.) must be completed and delivered. May differ from campaign start date.
reference: Workfront - Task Due Date; 4As Agency Operations Standards

description: Description of the geographic markets or regions targeted by this campaign (e.g., national, specific DMAs, countries). Free-text to support complex geo-targeting requirements.
reference: IAB Geo-Targeting Standards; Mediaocean Prisma Market Configuration

description: Indicates whether this brief is submitted through an intermediary agency on behalf of the end advertiser (True) or directly by the advertiser (False). Affects billing, reporting, and account team assignment.
reference: 4As Agency-Client Relationship Standards; Salesforce CRM Account Type

description: Indicates whether the client has designated this brief as confidential, restricting access to named account team members only. Drives data access controls and document handling procedures.
reference: GDPR Article 5; CCPA Section 1798; 4As Confidentiality Standards

description: The primary communication message or value proposition the client wants to convey to the target audience. Core creative direction provided in the brief.
reference: 4As Creative Brief Standards; ASA Advertising Standards

description: Client-specified elements, themes, competitors, or content that must NOT appear in any deliverable. Includes brand safety restrictions and competitive separation requirements.
reference: TAG Brand Safety Standards; ASA Advertising Standards; IAB Brand Safety Guidelines

description: Client-specified elements that must appear in all creative executions or deliverables (e.g., legal disclaimers, brand taglines, product claims, regulatory copy, logo usage requirements).
reference: ASA Advertising Standards; FTC Advertising Practices Division Guidelines; ICC Advertising and Marketing Communications Code

description: The single most important Key Performance Indicator (KPI) the client will use to measure campaign success. Drives optimization strategy and reporting requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ctr|cpm|cpc|cpa|roas|vtr|grp|trp|reach|frequency|engagement_rate|conversion_rate — promote to reference product]
reference: IAB Digital Advertising Standards; MRC Measurement Guidelines

description: Numeric target value the client expects to achieve for the primary KPI (e.g., a CTR of 0.0250 means 2.5%, a CPM of 5.00 means $5.00 per thousand impressions). Precision to 4 decimal places supports rate-based KPIs.
reference: IAB Digital Advertising Standards; MRC Measurement Guidelines

description: Client or account team-assigned priority level for this brief, used to triage work intake and allocate agency resources. Drives scheduling in Workfront project management.
reference: Workfront - Work Request Priority; 4As Resource Management Standards

description: Client-specified regulatory, legal, or compliance requirements that must be observed in all deliverables (e.g., GDPR consent requirements, CCPA disclosures, FTC endorsement guidelines, industry-specific advertising restrictions).
reference: FTC Advertising Practices Division; GDPR Article 6; CCPA Section 1798; ASA CAP Code; EASA Best Practice Guidelines

description: Narrative explanation of why the brief was revised from a prior version. Populated when brief_version > 1. Supports change management and audit trail.
reference: Workfront - Change Request Notes; 4As Brief Management Standards

description: Reference identifier of the associated Salesforce CRM Opportunity record, linking the brief to the revenue pipeline and account management context.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Sales Cloud Opportunity Object

description: Secondary KPI used to provide additional performance context beyond the primary KPI. Free-text to accommodate custom client KPI definitions not covered by standard enumerations.
reference: IAB Digital Advertising Standards; MRC Measurement Guidelines

description: Calendar date on which the client formally submitted the brief to the agency. The principal business event date for this record.
reference: Workfront - Work Request Submission Date; IAB Data Standards

description: Short, descriptive title of the client brief as provided by the requesting client contact (e.g., 'Q3 2024 Summer Campaign Launch Brief').
System timestamp recording when the client brief record was most recently modified. Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance.
reference: GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity; IAB Data Standards

description: Incrementing version number of the brief document, starting at 1. Incremented each time the client submits a revised brief. Supports brief revision history and change management.
reference: Workfront - Document Version Control; 4As Brief Management Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each client consent record in the advertising platform. Primary key for the client_consent_record data product.
reference: IAB Transparency and Consent Framework (TCF) v2.2; GDPR Article 7

description: Reference to the advertiser organisation whose consent record this is. Links to the client.advertiser master entity.
Reference to the individual contact (person) at the advertiser organisation who provided or withdrew consent. Supports GDPR data subject rights management.

business_glossary_term: California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) Applicable Flag
description: Indicates whether CCPA/CPRA applies to this consent record based on the data subject's California residency or the processing context. True if CCPA obligations apply; False otherwise. Drives CCPA-specific opt-out and data deletion workflows.
reference: CCPA Section 1798.100; CPRA Amendment; FTC Advertising Practices

description: The legal basis under which consent or data processing is justified. Maps to GDPR Article 6 lawful bases (consent, legitimate interest, contract performance) or CCPA opt-out/opt-in mechanisms, or CAN-SPAM compliance. Drives regulatory reporting and audit obligations.
reference: GDPR Article 6; CCPA Section 1798.120; CAN-SPAM Act 2003; FTC Advertising Practices

description: The channel or interface through which consent was captured or recorded. Supports audit trail requirements and channel-specific compliance obligations (e.g., GDPR Article 7 demonstrability). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web_form|email|crm_portal|paper|api|phone|in_person — promote to reference product if additional channels are required]
reference: GDPR Article 7(1); IAB TCF v2.2; FTC Advertising Practices

description: The precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the data subject provided consent or the consent record was first captured. This is the principal business event timestamp for the consent lifecycle. Required for GDPR Article 7 demonstrability.
reference: GDPR Article 7(1); CCPA Section 1798.100; IAB TCF v2.2

description: Reference identifier or URL pointing to the stored evidence of consent (e.g., screenshot of consent form, signed document ID in DAM, email confirmation message ID, API request log ID). Required for GDPR Article 7(1) demonstrability. May reference a Workfront DAM asset or document management system record.
reference: GDPR Article 7(1); ICO Consent Guidance; TAG Trustworthy Accountability Group

description: Externally-visible alphanumeric reference number assigned to this consent record, used in regulatory correspondence, audit trails, and data subject access requests. Format: CNS- followed by 8–16 alphanumeric characters.
reference: GDPR Article 7(1); IAB TCF v2.2 Section 3

description: Current lifecycle state of the consent record. Active indicates valid consent in force; withdrawn indicates the data subject has revoked consent; expired indicates the consent has passed its expiry date; pending indicates awaiting confirmation; refused indicates the data subject explicitly declined.
reference: GDPR Article 7(3); CCPA Section 1798.120; IAB TCF v2.2

description: Classification of the specific consent category captured. Distinguishes between marketing communications consent, data processing consent, third-party data sharing consent, profiling, retargeting, and analytics use. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: marketing_communications|data_processing|third_party_sharing|profiling|retargeting|analytics — promote to reference product if additional types are required]
reference: GDPR Article 6(1)(a); CCPA Section 1798.120; IAB TCF v2.2 Purpose definitions

description: Version identifier of the privacy notice, consent form, or cookie banner under which this consent was obtained (e.g., v1.0, v2.3). Enables tracking of consent obtained under different notice versions for regulatory audit and re-consent workflows when notices are updated.
reference: GDPR Article 13/14; IAB TCF v2.2 Section 3.1; ICO Consent Guidance

description: The date and time at which this consent record was first created in the system. Audit field supporting data lineage, regulatory demonstrability, and GDPR Article 7 record-keeping obligations.
reference: GDPR Article 7(1); ISO 8601; IAB TCF v2.2

business_glossary_term: Customer Relationship Management (CRM) Consent Record ID
description: The native record identifier for this consent record in Salesforce CRM (Marketing Cloud or Service Cloud). Enables bi-directional synchronisation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational CRM system of record for consent management.
reference: Salesforce Marketing Cloud Consent Management; GDPR Article 7

description: Comma-separated list or structured description of the personal data categories covered by this consent record (e.g., 'email address, browsing behaviour, device identifiers, location data'). Required for GDPR Article 13/14 transparency and maps to IAB TCF data category definitions.
reference: GDPR Articles 4, 9, 13, 14; IAB TCF v2.2; CCPA Section 1798.140

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 or alpha-3 country code representing the jurisdiction of the data subject at the time consent was captured. Determines which regulatory framework (GDPR, CCPA, LGPD, PIPEDA, etc.) governs this consent record and associated data processing obligations.
reference: GDPR Article 3; CCPA Section 1798.140; ISO 3166-1; NAI Code of Conduct

description: Indicates whether the consent was confirmed via a double opt-in mechanism (e.g., email confirmation link clicked). True if double opt-in confirmation was received. Provides stronger demonstrability of consent under GDPR Article 7 and is required for certain marketing communication channels.
reference: GDPR Article 7; ICO Consent Guidance; CAN-SPAM Act 2003

business_glossary_term: Double Opt-In Confirmation Date and Time
description: The precise date and time at which the double opt-in confirmation was received (e.g., confirmation link clicked). Null if double opt-in was not used or not yet confirmed. Supports audit trail for GDPR Article 7 demonstrability.

The date from which this consent record becomes operationally effective and data processing under this consent may commence. May differ from consent_date if there is a confirmation or cooling-off period.

The date on which this consent record expires and must be renewed or re-confirmed. Null indicates open-ended consent with no defined expiry. Supports periodic consent refresh workflows mandated by GDPR best practice and IAB TCF guidelines.
reference: GDPR Article 7; ICO Consent Guidance; IAB TCF v2.2

business_glossary_term: General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) Applicable Flag
description: Indicates whether GDPR applies to this consent record based on the data subject's jurisdiction or the processing context. True if GDPR obligations apply; False otherwise. Drives GDPR-specific processing rules and data subject rights workflows.

business_glossary_term: IAB Transparency and Consent Framework (TCF) Consent String
description: The encoded IAB TCF v2.2 consent string representing the data subject's vendor and purpose consent selections. Used by DSPs, SSPs, and DMPs to enforce consent downstream in programmatic advertising supply chains. Null for non-digital or non-programmatic consent records.
reference: IAB TCF v2.2; IAB Europe Consent Framework; GDPR Article 6

business_glossary_term: IAB Transparency and Consent Framework (TCF) Version
description: Version of the IAB Transparency and Consent Framework under which the TCF consent string was generated. Required for correct decoding and downstream enforcement of the consent string in programmatic advertising systems.
reference: IAB TCF v2.2; IAB Europe Consent Framework

description: IP address of the device from which consent was captured (for digital channels). Used as part of the consent audit trail to demonstrate the origin of consent. Classified as confidential PII as IP addresses may identify individuals in certain jurisdictions under GDPR.
reference: GDPR Article 4(1); CJEU Case C-582/14 (Breyer); ICO Consent Guidance

description: Free-text field for additional context, annotations, or operational notes related to this consent record. May include details of regulatory correspondence, data subject access request references, or account management observations. Not used for structured data.

Indicates whether the consent record represents an affirmative opt-in (True) or an opt-out election (False). Distinguishes between opt-in consent models (GDPR default) and opt-out models (CCPA default). Critical for determining permissible data processing activities.
reference: GDPR Article 6(1)(a); CCPA Section 1798.120; IAB TCF v2.2

description: URL of the privacy notice or consent form presented to the data subject at the time consent was captured. Provides an auditable link to the exact notice version shown, supporting GDPR Article 13/14 transparency and demonstrability requirements.
reference: GDPR Articles 13, 14; FTC Privacy Policy Requirements; CCPA Section 1798.100

description: Specific, granular description of the data processing activity for which consent was obtained, as required by GDPR Article 13/14 transparency obligations. Examples include: 'Delivery of targeted programmatic advertising via DSP', 'Audience segmentation for lookalike modelling', 'Cross-channel attribution reporting'. Free-text field aligned to the IAB TCF purpose taxonomy.
reference: GDPR Articles 5(1)(b), 13, 14; IAB TCF v2.2 Purpose definitions; FTC Section 5

description: Date on which the most recent consent renewal reminder communication was sent to the data subject. Supports consent lifecycle management workflows and prevents inadvertent processing of expired consent.
reference: GDPR Article 7; ICO Consent Guidance; CAN-SPAM Act 2003

description: Indicates whether this consent record requires active renewal before the expiry_date. True triggers automated re-consent workflow in Salesforce Marketing Cloud. Supports proactive consent lifecycle management to prevent lapses in permissible data processing.
reference: GDPR Article 7; ICO Consent Guidance; IAB TCF v2.2

description: Indicates whether this contact/advertiser is currently on a marketing suppression list as a result of this consent record (e.g., following opt-out or withdrawal). True means the contact must be excluded from all marketing communications. Enforced in Salesforce Marketing Cloud and campaign execution systems.
reference: GDPR Article 7(3); CCPA Section 1798.120; CAN-SPAM Act 2003; FTC Advertising Practices

description: Name of the third-party vendor or partner organisation with whom data may be shared under this consent record (applicable when consent_type is 'third_party_sharing'). Supports IAB TCF vendor list alignment and GDPR Article 28 data processor obligations.
reference: GDPR Articles 28, 46; IAB TCF v2.2 Vendor List; NAI Code of Conduct

description: The date and time at which this consent record was most recently modified. Supports audit trail requirements and change detection for downstream consent enforcement systems.

Browser or application user-agent string recorded at the time of digital consent capture. Supports audit trail completeness and fraud detection for consent records. Null for non-digital consent channels.
reference: GDPR Article 7(1); IAB TCF v2.2; ICO Consent Guidance

description: The date on which the data subject withdrew or revoked their consent. Null if consent has not been withdrawn. When populated, downstream data processing activities must cease within the regulatory timeframe. Required for GDPR Article 7(3) compliance.
reference: GDPR Article 7(3); CCPA Section 1798.120; FTC Advertising Practices

description: Categorised reason for the withdrawal of consent. Supports root-cause analysis of consent churn, regulatory reporting, and CRM-driven re-engagement eligibility assessments.
Primary key for client_engagement
Foreign key linking to the advertiser (client) who is engaging the talent for campaign work
Unique surrogate identifier for this engagement record in the Silver Layer lakehouse
Foreign key linking to the talent profile being engaged for the advertiser's campaign work

The negotiated billing rate for this specific advertiser-talent engagement, which may differ from the talent's standard day_rate_usd due to volume discounts, retainer terms, or client-specific pricing. Used for project costing and invoice generation.
User ID or email of the approver (typically talent director, finance controller, or account director) who authorized this engagement. Used for audit trail and approval workflow compliance.
Timestamp when this engagement was approved and moved to active status. Used for SLA tracking and engagement velocity reporting.
Current lifecycle state of the engagement. Active engagements are eligible for campaign assignment and time tracking. Drives workflow routing and capacity planning visibility.

The external contract or purchase order reference number from the legal or procurement system (e.g., Salesforce CPQ, DocuSign, SAP Ariba) that governs this engagement. Used for audit trail and legal compliance.
Timestamp when this engagement record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and engagement velocity analytics.
The contracted end date or expiry date of the engagement. Used for renewal workflows, capacity forecasting, and contract expiration alerts. Null for open-ended retainer engagements.
The contractual nature of the engagement, determining billing structure, legal terms, and workflow routing. Drives rate card application and contract template selection.

Indicates whether this engagement includes exclusivity restrictions preventing the talent from working with competing advertisers or brands during the engagement period. Used for conflict checking and compliance monitoring.

Defines the scope of exclusivity restrictions when exclusivity_flag is true (e.g., 'category exclusive - automotive', 'brand exclusive - competitor brands only', 'full exclusive - all brands'). Used for conflict checking workflows.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the agreed_rate (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Must align with advertiser preferred_currency or be explicitly agreed in contract terms.

The specific role or job title under which the talent is engaged for this advertiser (e.g., 'Senior Art Director', 'Brand Ambassador', 'CTV Media Specialist'). May differ from the talent's primary_discipline to reflect client-specific role assignments.
The effective start date of the engagement contract. Used for capacity planning, availability calculations, and contract compliance reporting.

Textual description or structured enumeration of the media usage rights granted under this engagement (e.g., 'digital only', 'global all media 12 months', 'social media perpetual'). Critical for influencer and creative talent engagements to ensure compliance with usage restrictions.
User ID or email of the talent manager, account manager, or procurement specialist who created this engagement record. Used for audit trail and workflow accountability.
Unique surrogate identifier for each client interaction record in the Silver layer. Primary key for the client_interaction data product.

description: Reference to the advertiser or agency-managed client account that is the subject of this interaction. Links to the client master record.

description: Reference to the Statement of Work (SOW), Insertion Order (IO), or master contract discussed or referenced during this interaction.

description: Reference to the specific client brand discussed during this interaction, where the client manages a portfolio of brands and the interaction pertains to a specific brand.

description: Reference to the active advertising campaign linked to this interaction, if the discussion pertains to an in-flight or planned campaign.
reference: Google Campaign Manager 360; IAB Campaign Standards

description: Reference to the specific client-side contact person who participated in this interaction. Links to the client contact master record.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object; GDPR Article 4

description: Foreign key linking to creative.creative_brief. Business justification: Client interactions (meetings, QBRs, calls) frequently discuss specific creative briefs—reviewing concepts, providing feedback, approving directions. Account teams need to track which briefs were disc

Foreign key linking to project.initiative. Business justification: Client interactions (meetings, QBRs, escalations) are frequently tied to specific projects/initiatives for context tracking, follow-up actions, and project-specific relationship management. Essential
Reference to the agency staff member responsible for completing the follow-up actions arising from this interaction.
reference: Salesforce CRM Task Owner; 4As Account Management Standards

description: Reference to the primary agency-side account manager or relationship owner who led or logged this interaction.
reference: Salesforce CRM User Object; 4As Account Management Standards

description: Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Client meetings frequently discuss specific vendor performance, rate negotiations, publisher selection, or tech platform issues. QBRs review vendor scorecards. Essential for tracking vendor-related cl
Comma-separated list of agency staff names or employee IDs who participated in the interaction. Captures the full agency-side attendance roster beyond the primary account manager.
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity Invitees; 4As Account Management Standards

description: Pre-interaction agenda or discussion topics as prepared by the account team. Supports meeting preparation, QBR structuring, and relationship planning.
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity Description; 4As Account Management Standards

description: Primary business topic or purpose of the interaction. Categorises the interaction by its strategic business function to support analytics and relationship health reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: campaign_review|media_planning|creative_review|budget_discussion|contract_renewal|new_business|reporting|escalation|onboarding|strategy|compliance — promote to reference product]
reference: 4As Account Management Standards; ANA Client Services Standards; IAB Standards

description: The communication medium through which the interaction was conducted. Distinct from interaction_type (which describes the business purpose); this field describes the delivery channel (e.g., in-person, video conference, phone call).
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity Channel; IAB Digital Standards

description: Comma-separated list of client-side participant names or contact IDs who attended the interaction. Captures the full client attendance roster beyond the primary contact.
reference: Salesforce CRM Activity Invitees; GDPR Article 4; CCPA

description: Account manager's qualitative assessment of client sentiment observed during the interaction. Supports relationship health monitoring, NPS tracking, and early warning of at-risk accounts.
reference: ANA Client Relationship Health Standards; Salesforce CRM; NPS Framework

description: Timestamp when this interaction record was first created in the source Salesforce CRM system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and SLA compliance reporting.

description: Source system identifier from Salesforce CRM Activity or Task object. Used for lineage tracing and reconciliation with the operational system of record.

business_glossary_term: Io Authorization - Media Insertion Order Id
description: Foreign key linking to the media insertion order being authorized

Date on which the authorizing party formally approved this IO. Establishes the legal effective date of the contractual commitment. [Moved from media_insertion_order: This attribute currently stores a single authorization date on the IO record, but in reality multiple contacts sign at different times. This should be removed from media_insertion_order and replaced by signature_date in the io_authorization association, which tracks the specific timestamp for each signatory.]

description: The level or tier of authorization this signature represents (e.g., primary approver, secondary approver, conditional approval). Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.

Method by which the IO was authorized by the client or agency. Supports legal audit trails and e-signature compliance under applicable regulations. [Moved from media_insertion_order: This attribute currently stores a single authorization method on the IO record, but different signatories may use different methods (e.g., legal uses DocuSign, finance uses email approval). This should be removed from media_insertion_order and replaced by signature_method in the io_authorization association, which tracks the method used by each specific signatory.]
reference: Mediaocean Prisma — Authorization Method; FTC Advertising Practices; ESIGN Act

description: Free-text notes or conditions attached to this authorization by the signatory (e.g., 'Approved pending legal review', 'Conditional on budget availability').
Current status of this specific authorization record. Tracks whether the signature is still valid or has been revoked/expired.

Full name of the individual (client or agency representative) who authorized and signed off on this IO. Required for legal enforceability and audit compliance. [Moved from media_insertion_order: This attribute currently stores a single authorizing party name on the IO record, but in reality multiple contacts authorize each IO in different roles. This attribute should be removed from media_insertion_order and replaced by the io_authorization association, which properly models the many-to-many relationship and captures which specific contact authorized in which role.]
reference: Mediaocean Prisma — Authorized By; FTC Advertising Practices; IAB Standard Terms and Conditions

description: Timestamp when this authorization record was first created in the system (may differ from signature_date if authorization was recorded retroactively).

The functional role in which this contact authorized the IO. Multiple contacts may sign the same IO in different capacities (e.g., legal signatory, budget approver). Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.
Precise timestamp when this contact completed their signature or authorization action for this IO. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.
The mechanism used to capture this contacts authorization (e.g., DocuSign, wet signature, email approval). Supports legal audit requirements. Explicitly identified in detection reasoning.
Timestamp when this authorization record was last modified (e.g., status change, notes added).

description: The contract or Statement of Work (SOW) reference number associated with this relationship event. Links the history record to the formal contractual instrument in SAP S/4HANA or Mediaocean Prisma.

description: The date and time at which this relationship history record was first created in the data platform. Immutable audit field — this record is append-only and this timestamp never changes after initial insert.

description: Salesforce CRM Opportunity record identifier associated with this relationship event, such as an Agency of Record (AOR) win or pitch outcome. Enables traceability back to the originating CRM pipeline record.

description: Indicates whether this relationship event represents or was triggered by a formal client escalation (True) or is a routine relationship update (False). Enables rapid filtering of escalation events for executive review and risk management.

description: Severity classification of the escalation when escalation_flag is True. Drives prioritisation of executive response and account recovery actions. Null when escalation_flag is False.
reference: 4As Account Management Standards; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Guidelines

description: The estimated annual revenue impact (positive or negative) on the agency resulting from this relationship event, expressed in the client's preferred currency. Used for P&L forecasting and executive pipeline reporting. Positive values indicate revenue gain; negative values indicate revenue loss.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO — Revenue Planning; 4As Financial Management Standards

description: The business date on which the relationship event occurred or was formally recognised (e.g., the date an AOR contract was signed, the date a scope change was approved). Distinct from the record creation timestamp.
reference: 4As Account Management Standards; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Guidelines

description: Detailed free-text notes authored by the account team or executive sponsor describing the circumstances, rationale, and business context of the relationship event. Supports Quarterly Business Review (QBR) documentation and institutional knowledge retention.
reference: 4As Account Management Standards; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Guidelines

description: Precise date and time (with timezone offset) at which the relationship event was recorded in the system. Supports audit trail requirements and event sequencing within the same calendar day.
reference: GDPR Article 5(2) Accountability; FTC Advertising Practices — Record Retention

description: Categorises the nature of the significant relationship event being recorded. Drives downstream reporting and executive dashboards.
