reference: 4As Account Management Standards; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Guidelines

description: The Insertion Order (IO) number associated with this relationship event where the event is linked to a specific media buy or campaign commitment. Relevant for scope change and contract renewal events.
reference: IAB Standard Insertion Order Terms; Mediaocean Prisma — IO Module

description: Indicates whether this relationship history event is classified as confidential and should be restricted to senior leadership only (True). Supports row-level security enforcement for sensitive events such as AOR losses or executive escalations.
reference: GDPR Article 5; FTC Advertising Practices — Confidentiality; 4As Data Governance Framework

description: Free-text narrative describing the new state of the relationship, scope, team composition, or contract terms after this event. Provides the post-event context for executive reporting and QBR preparation.
reference: 4As Account Management Standards; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Guidelines

description: The relationship status of the client account immediately after this event was applied. Together with previous_status, provides a complete state transition record for lifecycle management.
reference: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud — Account History Object; 4As Account Management Standards

description: The Net Promoter Score (NPS) recorded from the client at or near the time of this relationship event, on the standard -100 to +100 scale. Provides a quantitative client satisfaction signal to complement the qualitative event narrative.
reference: ANA Client-Agency Relationship Guidelines; NPS Methodology (Bain & Company)

description: Free-text narrative describing the prior state of the relationship, scope, team composition, or contract terms before this event. Captures contextual detail beyond the structured status field (e.g., 'AOR for digital and social channels only').
reference: 4As Account Management Standards; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Guidelines

description: The relationship status of the client account immediately before this event was triggered. Enables before/after state comparison for lifecycle analysis and executive reporting.
reference: Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud — Account History Object; 4As Account Management Standards

description: A numeric score (typically 1–10) representing the assessed health of the client relationship at the time of this event, as recorded by the account team. Supports at-risk client identification and proactive retention strategies. Not a calculated metric — this is the human-assessed score captured at event time.
reference: ANA Client-Agency Relationship Guidelines; Salesforce CRM Sales Cloud — Account Health Scoring

description: The date on which an escalation or at-risk relationship event was formally resolved or closed. Null for non-escalation events or unresolved escalations. Enables measurement of escalation resolution cycle time.
reference: 4As Account Management Standards; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Guidelines

description: Free-text description of the actions taken and outcome achieved to resolve an escalation or relationship risk event. Populated when resolution_date is set. Supports institutional learning and account recovery playbook development.
reference: 4As Account Management Standards; ANA Client-Agency Relationship Guidelines

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the estimated_revenue_impact amount (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Ensures correct financial interpretation in multi-currency agency environments.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI — Currency Configuration

description: The native record identifier from the originating source system (e.g., Salesforce CRM Account History ID, Workfront task ID). Enables traceability and deduplication during ETL processing.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Lineage Standards; IAB Data Standards

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this relationship history event originated (e.g., Salesforce CRM, Workfront, Mediaocean Prisma). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Silver Layer lakehouse.
reference: IAB Data Standards; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Data Lineage Standards

description: Full name of the agency staff member or executive who initiated or recorded this relationship event. Stored for human-readable audit trail and executive reporting without requiring a join to HR systems.
reference: GDPR Article 5(2) Accountability; 4As Account Management Standards

The current approved budget amount after any revisions, amendments, or reforecasts. Reflects the most up-to-date budget allocation for this line.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Budget Revision Management

description: Sequential version number tracking how many times this budget line has been revised. Supports change management and audit requirements.
Textual explanation for why this budget line was revised. Required for financial governance and Quarterly Business Review (QBR) reporting.
The absolute difference between revised budget amount and actual spend. Positive values indicate under-spend, negative values indicate over-spend.
The variance amount expressed as a percentage of the revised budget. Key Performance Indicator (KPI) for budget management and financial control.
Identifier of the user who created this budget line record. Supports accountability and audit requirements.
Unique identifier for the cost center record. Primary key.
Reference to the parent cost center in a hierarchical cost center structure. Enables roll-up reporting and multi-level budget consolidation. Null for top-level cost centers.

The method used to allocate costs from this cost center to other cost objects or client accounts. Direct allocation assigns costs explicitly, activity-based uses cost drivers, headcount allocates by employee count, revenue allocates by revenue contribution, square footage by physical space, and Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) by staffing levels.
The total approved budget amount allocated to this cost center for the fiscal year. Used for budget-versus-actual variance analysis and financial planning.
Boolean indicator of whether expenditures charged to this cost center require explicit approval before posting. True indicates approval workflow is enforced; false indicates automatic posting.
The monetary threshold above which expenditures charged to this cost center require additional approval. Amounts below this threshold may be auto-approved based on delegation rules.
Boolean indicator of whether costs incurred in this cost center are directly billable to clients. True indicates billable work; false indicates non-billable overhead or internal support.
reference: Agency Financial Management Standards - American Association of Advertising Agencies (4As)

description: The email address of the budget owner for communication regarding budget approvals, variance reporting, and financial planning.
The name of the individual responsible for managing and approving the budget for this cost center. Typically a department head, practice leader, or senior manager.
The company code or legal entity identifier to which this cost center belongs. Used for multi-entity financial consolidation and reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Financial Accounting (FI) Module Standards

description: The controlling area in SAP S/4HANA CO that groups cost centers for management accounting and internal reporting purposes. A controlling area may span multiple company codes.

High-level functional category of the cost center. Production centers create deliverables, service centers provide internal services, support centers enable operations, sales centers drive revenue, marketing centers manage campaigns, and administration centers handle corporate governance.
reference: Management Accounting Best Practices - Institute of Management Accountants (IMA)

description: The externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the cost center in financial systems and reports. Used as the business identifier in SAP S/4HANA CO module and Mediaocean Prisma billing.
The human-readable name or title of the cost center, used for display and reporting purposes.

Current lifecycle status of the cost center. Active centers are operational and accepting charges, inactive centers are temporarily disabled, suspended centers are under review, closed centers are permanently retired, and pending approval centers are awaiting activation.

Classification of the cost center based on its function and cost allocation model. Overhead centers support general operations, billable centers track client-facing work, shared services centers provide cross-functional support, direct centers relate to revenue-generating activities, indirect centers support operations, and administrative centers manage corporate functions.
reference: Management Accounting Best Practices - Institute of Management Accountants (IMA)

description: Boolean indicator of whether this cost center functions as a cost pool that aggregates costs for subsequent allocation to other cost centers or client accounts. True indicates a cost pool; false indicates a standard cost center.
reference: Management Accounting Best Practices - Institute of Management Accountants (IMA)

description: The date and time when this cost center record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage tracking.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which this cost center's budget and expenses are denominated. Examples include USD, EUR, GBP, CAD.
The date on which this cost center configuration ceases to be effective. Null for currently active configurations. Used for historical analysis and audit trails.
The date on which this cost center configuration becomes effective and begins accepting cost allocations. Supports temporal validity and historical tracking.
The fiscal year for which this cost center configuration is applicable. Cost centers may have different configurations across fiscal years for budget planning and reporting purposes.
The username or identifier of the user who last modified this cost center record. Supports audit requirements and change management.
reference: Audit Trail Standards - Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) Section 404

description: The date and time when this cost center record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and audit trails.
Three-letter code identifying the primary geographic location or office where this cost center operates. Examples include NYC, LON, SFO, TOR. Used for location-based cost analysis and reporting.
Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or contextual information about the cost center. Used for documentation and knowledge transfer.
The percentage of overhead costs allocated to this cost center from shared services or administrative cost pools. Used in fully-loaded cost calculations and margin analysis.
The department or organizational unit that owns and manages this cost center. Examples include Creative Services, Media Planning, Account Management, Finance, Human Resources, IT, Operations.
The specialized practice area or service line associated with this cost center, such as Digital Marketing, Brand Strategy, Public Relations, Data Analytics, Creative Production, Media Buying.
reference: Agency Practice Management Standards - American Association of Advertising Agencies (4As)

description: Boolean indicator of whether this cost center has direct profit and loss accountability. True indicates the center is a profit center with revenue and expense responsibility; false indicates a pure cost center.
reference: Management Accounting Best Practices - Institute of Management Accountants (IMA)

description: The budget amount allocated to this cost center for the current quarter. Supports quarterly business review (QBR) processes and short-term financial management.
The corresponding cost center identifier in the SAP S/4HANA Controlling (CO) module. Used for integration and reconciliation between the lakehouse and the source ERP system.
The username or identifier of the user who created this cost center record. Supports audit requirements and accountability.
reference: Audit Trail Standards - Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) Section 404

description: Unique identifier for the budget record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Advertising budgets are allocated by brand for brand-level P&L tracking and investment analysis. Brand managers require brand_profile attribution to measure ROI and spending against brand-specific tar
Reference to the campaign this budget is allocated to, if applicable.

Foreign key linking to client.client_brief. Business justification: Client briefs define campaign objectives, budgets, and deliverables that directly drive budget creation and allocation in advertising operations. Budget planning starts with approved client briefs spe
Reference to the cost center this budget is allocated to for internal financial tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO (Controlling) Cost Center Accounting

description: Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Budget references GL account via natural key (gl_account_code). Adding surrogate key FK gl_account_id enables proper referential integrity. Remove redundant gl_account_code as it can be retrieved via

Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: SOWs define the scope and budget for client engagements; finance creates budgets based on SOW commitments for spend control and forecasting.
Amount of the budget that has been allocated or committed to specific activities or sub-budgets, but not yet spent.
Date when the budget was officially approved by the authorized approver.
Current approval workflow status of the budget: draft (being prepared), pending (awaiting approval), approved (authorized for use), rejected (not approved), cancelled (voided).
The total approved budget amount in the specified currency. This is the baseline for spend authorization and variance analysis.
Name or identifier of the individual who approved the budget.

High-level financial category of the budget: operating (day-to-day operational expenses), capital (long-term asset investments), project (specific project-based allocation), discretionary (flexible spend allocation).
Detailed description of the budget purpose, scope, and any special conditions or notes.
Descriptive name of the budget for easy identification and reporting purposes.
Externally-known unique business identifier for the budget record, used for reference in financial documents and approvals.

Classification of the budget by its purpose: campaign (client campaign spend), overhead (agency operational costs), media (media buying allocation), production (creative production costs), talent (talent and usage rights fees), creative (creative development costs).
Timestamp when this budget record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the approved budget amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Date when this budget expires and spend authorization ends. Nullable for open-ended budgets.
Date when this budget becomes effective and spend authorization begins.
The fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year this budget applies to, typically 1-12 for monthly periods or 1-4 for quarterly periods.

description: The fiscal year this budget applies to, represented as a four-digit year (e.g., 2024).

description: Indicates whether the budget is locked (true) and cannot be modified, or unlocked (false) and can still be edited. Locked budgets are typically final versions used for variance reporting.

Name or identifier of the individual or role responsible for managing and authorizing spend against this budget.
Profit center code in SAP S/4HANA CO for Profit and Loss (P&L) reporting and margin analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO (Controlling) Profit Center Accounting

description: Remaining unspent budget amount, calculated as approved amount minus spent amount and allocated amount.

Business justification or reason for the most recent budget revision.
Reference to the SAP S/4HANA CO internal order number used for cost collection and budget control in the ERP system.
Actual amount spent against this budget to date, used for budget-vs-actual variance analysis.
Acceptable variance threshold as a percentage of the approved budget. Exceeding this threshold triggers alerts or requires additional approval.
Version status of the budget: original (initial approved budget), revised (updated after changes), final (locked final version), forecast (projected budget for planning).
User identifier or name of the person who created this budget record.
Unique identifier for the accrual record. Primary key for the accrual entity.
Reference to the client account associated with this accrual, enabling client-level revenue and cost tracking for Profit and Loss (P&L) reporting.

Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Month-end accruals for unbilled media or services are estimated and tracked by brand for accurate brand P&L closure. Essential for brand financial reporting accuracy, brand profitability measurement,
Foreign key linking to media.media_buy. Business justification: Accruals for media buys awaiting vendor invoices - critical for period-end expense recognition and financial statement accuracy.

Reference to the advertising campaign associated with this accrual, if applicable. Used to allocate media costs, production costs, and revenues to specific campaigns for margin analysis and Return on Ad Spend (ROAS) calculation.
Foreign key linking to performance.click_event. Business justification: CPC cost accruals based on delivered clicks not yet invoiced; requires click volume for accurate accrual estimation.
Reference to the cost center responsible for this accrual, enabling departmental and functional cost tracking.
reference: SAP CO Cost Center Master Data

description: Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Accruals are often tracked against approved budgets to monitor estimated spend and ensure accrued costs are within budget allocations. This FK enables budget-to-actual variance analysis including accr

Foreign key linking to finance.financial_close. Business justification: Accruals are posted during financial close periods as part of period-end adjustments. This FK links the accrual to the financial close period it belongs to, enabling close workflow tracking and ensuri

Foreign key linking to performance.impression_event. Business justification: Media cost accruals estimate costs for delivered-but-not-yet-billed impressions; requires impression volume data for accurate accrual calculation.

Foreign key linking to media.media_insertion_order. Business justification: Accruals posted for insertion order commitments not yet invoiced - essential for accurate financial close and expense matching.

Foreign key linking to creative.production_job. Business justification: Accruals are estimated for in-flight production jobs (unbilled vendor work, labor costs). Links accruals to jobs for work-in-progress accounting, period-end close accuracy, and job cost forecasting.
Reference to the vendor or supplier for whom this accrual represents an estimated payable, used for vendor reconciliation and accounts payable management.
The date on which the accrual entry is recorded in the financial system, typically the last day of the accounting period.
Detailed narrative description of the accrual, explaining the nature of the cost or revenue, the business rationale, and any supporting context for period-end close and audit purposes.

Classification of the accrual by nature of the transaction: expense accrual for general operating costs, revenue accrual for earned but unbilled revenue, media cost accrual for media buys incurred but not invoiced, production cost accrual for creative production work completed but not billed, talent cost accrual for freelance or contractor work performed but not invoiced, or overhead accrual for allocated indirect costs.

The actual invoiced amount received after the accrual was posted, used to calculate accrual variance and improve future estimation accuracy. Populated when the actual invoice is received and matched to the accrual.

Current approval status of the accrual entry. Draft indicates the accrual is being prepared; pending_approval indicates the accrual is awaiting finance manager approval; approved indicates the accrual has been approved and posted; rejected indicates the accrual was rejected and requires revision.
Date and time when the accrual entry was approved for posting, supporting audit trail and period-end close timeline tracking.
User ID or name of the finance manager or controller who approved this accrual entry for posting to the general ledger.

The methodology used to calculate the accrual amount. Contract indicates the accrual is based on contractual terms; estimate indicates management estimate based on available information; historical indicates the accrual is based on historical spending patterns; percentage_of_completion indicates the accrual is calculated based on project completion percentage.
Date and time when this accrual record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the currency in which the accrual amount is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
The estimated monetary value of the accrued cost or revenue in the specified currency. This is the best estimate at period-end based on available information, contracts, and historical patterns.
reference: GAAP Accrual Accounting Principles (ASC 450), IFRS IAS 37

description: The fiscal period within the fiscal year to which this accrual applies. Format: Q1-Q4 for quarters, M01-M12 for months, or P01-P13 for SAP posting periods.
The fiscal year to which this accrual applies, represented as a four-digit year (e.g., 2024).
SAP CO internal order number for project-based or campaign-based cost tracking, used when accruals need to be allocated to specific internal orders for detailed cost control.

Boolean flag indicating whether this accrual is considered material for financial reporting purposes based on corporate materiality thresholds. Material accruals require additional review and documentation.
Reference identifier from Mediaocean Prisma system for media cost accruals, enabling reconciliation between media planning/buying platform and financial system.
Date and time when this accrual record was last modified, supporting change tracking and audit trail requirements.
Additional free-text notes or comments providing supplementary context, assumptions, or supporting documentation references for the accrual. Used during period-end close review and audit.
The date on which the accrual entry was posted to the general ledger in the financial system, which may differ from the accrual date if the entry was prepared in advance and posted later.
Name or user ID of the finance analyst or accountant who prepared this accrual entry, supporting accountability and audit trail requirements.
The profit center code to which this accrual is allocated, enabling profitability analysis by business unit, service line, or geographic region.
Business-facing unique reference number for the accrual entry, used for tracking and reconciliation purposes.
reference: SAP FI Accrual Document Numbering Standard

description: The date on which this accrual is scheduled to be reversed, typically the first day of the subsequent accounting period. Accruals are reversed when the actual invoice or payment is received and recorded.

Current status of the accrual reversal process. Pending indicates the accrual is active and awaiting reversal; reversed indicates the accrual has been reversed upon receipt of actual invoice; cancelled indicates the accrual was cancelled without reversal; adjusted indicates the accrual amount was modified before reversal.

business_glossary_term: SAP Financial Accounting (FI) Accrual Document Number
description: The SAP FI document number assigned to this accrual entry in the SAP S/4HANA system, used for traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse and the source ERP system.

The difference between the estimated accrual amount and the actual invoice amount (actual_invoice_amount minus estimated_amount). Positive variance indicates over-accrual; negative variance indicates under-accrual. Used for accrual accuracy analysis and process improvement.

The variance amount expressed as a percentage of the estimated amount, calculated as (variance_amount / estimated_amount) * 100. Used to assess accrual estimation quality and identify systematic over- or under-accrual patterns.
Unique identifier for the financial close record. Primary key.

Indicates whether all required accruals have been posted for the period.
The actual date when the financial close was completed and the period was locked.
Indicates whether the accounts payable subledger has been reconciled to the general ledger control account.
Indicates whether the accounts receivable subledger has been reconciled to the general ledger control account.
Indicates whether all documentation and supporting evidence is prepared and ready for external audit review by Big 4 auditors.
Reference number or identifier linking this close to audit documentation and supporting evidence for Big 4 audit readiness.
Indicates whether all bank account reconciliations have been completed and cleared for the period.

Free-text commentary and notes about the close process, including issues encountered, resolutions, and key decisions made during the period.

Email address of the close owner for communication and escalation purposes.
Business reference number for the close period, used for external communication and audit trail.

Current status of the financial close workflow: open (not started), in-progress (tasks being executed), pending-review (awaiting approval), closed (completed and locked), or reopened (unlocked for adjustments).
Type of financial close being performed: monthly, quarterly, annual, or special (ad-hoc).
SAP company code for which this close is being performed, representing the legal entity.
Name of the controller who signed off on the close.
Indicates whether the controller has reviewed and approved the close for the period.
Date and time when the controller signed off on the close.
Date and time when the financial close record was created in the system.

business_glossary_term: Days to Close Key Performance Indicator (KPI)
description: Number of calendar days taken to complete the close from period end to actual close date. Key performance indicator for close efficiency.
The fiscal period number within the fiscal year (1-12 for monthly, 1-4 for quarterly).
The fiscal quarter number (1-4) within the fiscal year.
The fiscal year for which this close period applies.
Deadline imposed by the holding company for completion of the financial close and submission of consolidated reporting packages.
Indicates whether all intercompany transactions have been identified and elimination entries posted for consolidated reporting.
Indicates whether all media cost reconciliations between planned and billed amounts have been cleared and approved.
User ID or name of the person who last modified the financial close record.
Date and time when the financial close record was last modified.
Indicates whether all revenue recognition entries have been posted according to performance obligations and contract terms.

business_glossary_term: SAP Financial Accounting (FI) Period Lock Date
description: Date when the period was locked in SAP S/4HANA FI.

business_glossary_term: SAP Financial Accounting (FI) Period Lock Status
description: Status of the period lock in SAP S/4HANA FI: open (postings allowed), locked (no postings allowed), or partially-locked (restricted postings only).
Indicates whether all SOX 404 internal control requirements have been met for this close period.
The planned date by which the financial close should be completed, typically set by holding company or regulatory deadlines.
Explanation of significant variances identified during the close process, including budget-to-actual and period-over-period variances.
User ID or name of the person who created the financial close record.
Unique identifier for the general ledger account. Primary key for the GL account master data.

description: The externally-known unique account number used in financial transactions and reporting. Typically a 4-10 digit numeric code aligned to the chart of accounts structure.

description: Extended description or explanatory text for the general ledger account, providing additional context, usage guidelines, or business rules for posting.
The hierarchical grouping or category within the chart of accounts (e.g., 'Current Assets', 'Operating Expenses', 'Client Revenue'). Used for rollup and reporting.

description: The human-readable name or title of the general ledger account (e.g., 'Media Costs - Digital', 'Client Revenue - Retainer', 'Salaries and Wages').
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Chart of Accounts

description: The fundamental accounting classification of the account: asset, liability, equity, revenue, or expense. Determines the account's role in the balance sheet or profit and loss statement.
reference: IFRS Conceptual Framework; GAAP; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: The detailed balance sheet classification for asset, liability, and equity accounts: current asset, non-current asset, current liability, non-current liability, equity, or not applicable (for P&L accounts).
The chart of accounts identifier to which this account belongs. Defines the account structure and numbering scheme for the organization or group.

description: The current lifecycle status of the general ledger account: active (in use), inactive (not in use but retained), pending (awaiting approval), or archived (historical only).
The IFRS account classification or line item code to which this account maps for IFRS-compliant financial reporting.

description: The date from which this general ledger account is valid and available for use in financial transactions. Supports time-dependent account structures.

Foreign key reference to the client (advertiser) associated with this journal entry. Used for client-level revenue recognition and billing reconciliation. Null for internal or non-client entries.

Foreign key linking to contract.amendment. Business justification: Significant contract amendments trigger accounting adjustments via journal entries for deferred revenue, accruals, or revenue recognition changes.

Foreign key linking to media.media_buy. Business justification: Journal entries post media buy expenses for financial reporting and cost recognition in general ledger accounting.

For accrual-type entries, the date on which the accrual will be automatically reversed. Typically the first day of the following fiscal period. Null for non-accrual entries.

description: Status of the automatic reversal for accrual entries: not_applicable (non-accrual entry), pending (scheduled for reversal), reversed (reversal completed), or failed (reversal encountered an error).

description: For accrual entries, the category of accrual: expense (general operating expenses), revenue (deferred or unbilled revenue), media_cost (media buy accruals), production_cost (creative production accruals), or not_applicable (non-accrual entry).

description: User ID or name of the individual who approved this journal entry for posting. Null if no approval workflow is required or if entry is still pending approval.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Approval Workflow; Internal Controls Framework

description: Date and time when this journal entry was approved for posting. Null if no approval is required or if entry is still pending approval.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Approval Workflow; Internal Controls

description: The batch identifier for grouped journal entries posted together in a single batch run. Used for batch-level reconciliation and error tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Batch Input; Internal Processing Standards

description: The legal entity or company code within the corporate structure to which this journal entry belongs. Used for multi-entity consolidation and reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code; GAAP Consolidation Standards

description: User ID or name of the individual who created this journal entry in the accounting system. Used for audit trail and accountability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI User Tracking; SOX Compliance

description: Date and time when this journal entry record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Audit Trail; SOX Compliance

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP) indicating the transaction currency for this journal entry.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Currency Management

description: The date of the source document or transaction that triggered this journal entry. May differ from posting date for backdated or forward-dated entries.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Date; IFRS Accounting Standards

description: Classification of the journal entry indicating the nature of the posting: standard (regular operational postings), accrual (period-end accruals with auto-reversal), reversal (reverses a prior entry), intercompany (cross-entity transactions), reclassification (reallocation between accounts), or adjustment (corrective postings).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Types; GAAP Accounting Standards

description: Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether the amounts in this journal entry are estimates (True) or actuals (False). Common for accruals where final invoice amounts are not yet known.
reference: GAAP Estimation Principles; SAP S/4HANA FI Accrual Logic

description: The fiscal period (e.g., 01, 02, ..., 12, or 13 for year-end adjustments) to which this journal entry is assigned. Used for period-end close and financial statement preparation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Period; GAAP Period Close Requirements

description: The fiscal year (e.g., 2023, 2024) to which this journal entry belongs. Combined with fiscal_period, this determines the reporting period for financial statements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Year; GAAP Reporting Standards

description: For intercompany entries, the legal entity code or name of the counterparty entity within the corporate group. Null for non-intercompany entries.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Intercompany Accounting; GAAP Consolidation Standards

description: Free-text description providing business context for the journal entry, such as the nature of the transaction, campaign reference, or accounting rationale.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Header Text; Internal Documentation Standards

description: Business-facing unique journal entry document number assigned by the accounting system. Used for external reference and audit trails.
Additional free-text notes or comments providing supplementary context, audit explanations, or special instructions related to this journal entry.
reference: Internal Documentation Standards; SAP S/4HANA FI Long Text

description: For reversal-type entries, the journal entry number of the original entry being reversed. Null for non-reversal entries.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Reversal Document Link; GAAP Reversal Traceability

description: Date and time when this journal entry was posted to the general ledger. Null if entry is still in draft or pending approval status.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Log; GAAP Posting Requirements

description: The date on which the journal entry was posted to the general ledger. This is the effective date for financial reporting and determines the fiscal period assignment.
reference: GAAP Revenue Recognition; SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Date

description: Current lifecycle status of the journal entry: draft (not yet posted), posted (committed to the general ledger), reversed (entry has been reversed), cancelled (entry voided before posting), or pending_approval (awaiting authorization).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Status; Internal Controls Framework

description: The profit center code for internal profitability analysis and segment reporting. Used for EBITDA tracking and P&L reporting by business unit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Profit Center Accounting; Internal P&L Reporting

description: For reclassification entries, a textual explanation of why the reallocation between accounts was necessary. Null for non-reclassification entries.
reference: Internal Accounting Policy; SAP S/4HANA FI Reclassification

description: External reference number linking this journal entry to a source document such as an invoice, purchase order, media insertion order (IO), or contract. Used for audit trail and reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Reference Field; Internal Audit Standards

description: The journal entry number of the reversing entry that offsets this original entry. Null if this entry has not been reversed.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Reversal Document Link; GAAP Reversal Traceability

description: User ID or name of the individual who reversed this journal entry. Null if entry has not been reversed.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI User Tracking; Internal Audit Standards

description: Date and time when this journal entry was reversed. Null if entry has not been reversed.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Reversal Log; GAAP Reversal Standards

description: The native SAP S/4HANA FI document number for this journal entry, used for system-to-system reconciliation and traceability back to the source ERP system.
The name or code of the upstream system that originated this journal entry (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Mediaocean Prisma, Workday HCM). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: Data Governance Framework; ETL Source Tracking

description: The sum of all credit amounts across all line items in this journal entry. Must equal total_debit_amount per double-entry accounting principles.
reference: GAAP Double-Entry Accounting; SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Logic

description: The sum of all debit amounts across all line items in this journal entry. Must equal total_credit_amount per double-entry accounting principles.
reference: GAAP Double-Entry Accounting; SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Logic

description: Unique identifier for the journal entry line item. Primary key for the journal entry line product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI General Ledger Line Item

description: Reference to the client account associated with this line item. Supports client-level revenue recognition and margin analysis.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account; SAP S/4HANA Customer Master

description: Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Individual GL line items need brand attribution for detailed brand-level financial reporting and consolidation. Required for brand P&L drill-down, brand cost allocation transparency, and supporting br
Foreign key linking to media.media_buy. Business justification: Journal entry lines post buy-level expenses for detailed GL accounting and financial statement line-item support.
Reference to the advertising campaign associated with this line item. Enables campaign-level cost allocation and ROI analysis.
reference: Campaign Management; SAP S/4HANA PS Project

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Journal entry lines are allocated to cost centers for management accounting. Adding cost_center_id FK replaces natural key reference.

Reference to the parent journal entry header. Links this line item to its containing journal entry document.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Journal Entry Header

description: Foreign key linking to media.media_insertion_order. Business justification: Journal entry lines detail insertion order-level postings for granular accounting and audit trail in financial systems.
Reference to the original journal entry line that this entry reverses. Null if this is not a reversal entry.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Reversal Document Reference

description: Free-text assignment field used for grouping related line items, linking to external references, or supporting reconciliation processes.
Baseline date for payment terms calculation. Used to determine due dates and cash discount periods for payables and receivables.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Terms; Accounts Payable/Receivable

description: Business area for cross-company code reporting and segment analysis. Enables consolidated P&L by business line or service offering.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Business Area Accounting

description: Date on which this line item was cleared (matched with offsetting entry). Null for open items. Supports accounts payable and receivable reconciliation.
Document number of the clearing entry that closed this line item. Null for open items. Links payments to invoices and supports reconciliation.
User ID of the person who created this journal entry line. Supports audit trails, segregation of duties, and SOX compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI User Audit; SOX Section 404

description: Timestamp when this journal entry line record was first created in the system. Supports audit trails and data lineage tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Audit Trail; SOX Compliance

description: Credit amount for this line item in the transaction currency. Null or zero if the line is a debit entry.
reference: GAAP Double-Entry Accounting; SAP S/4HANA FI Line Item

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction amounts. Supports multi-currency accounting and foreign exchange reconciliation.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Currency Management

description: Debit amount for this line item in the transaction currency. Null or zero if the line is a credit entry.
reference: GAAP Double-Entry Accounting; SAP S/4HANA FI Line Item

description: Date of the source document (invoice, receipt, etc.) that originated this line item. May differ from posting date for backdated or forward-dated entries.
Two-character document type code that classifies the journal entry (e.g., SA for G/L account document, DR for customer invoice, KR for vendor invoice). Controls number ranges and posting rules.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Document Type Configuration

description: Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year for this line item. Enables monthly P&L reporting and budget variance analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Period; GAAP Reporting Periods

description: Fiscal year in which this line item is posted. Supports period-based financial reporting and year-end closing processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Fiscal Year Variant; GAAP Reporting Periods

description: Descriptive text providing additional context for the line item. Used for audit trails, reconciliation notes, and financial statement drill-down.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Line Item Text Field

description: Sequential line number within the journal entry. Determines the ordering of line items within a single journal entry document.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Line Item Numbering

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the company code local currency used in financial statements.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code Configuration

description: Credit amount converted to the company code local currency for consolidated financial reporting and P&L analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Currency Translation; GAAP Reporting

description: Debit amount converted to the company code local currency for consolidated financial reporting and P&L analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Currency Translation; GAAP Reporting

description: User ID of the person who last modified this journal entry line. Supports audit trails and change management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI User Audit; SOX Section 404

description: Timestamp when this journal entry line record was last modified. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Change Document; SOX Compliance

description: Payment terms code defining due date calculation and cash discount conditions for vendor and customer line items.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Payment Terms Configuration

description: Date on which the line item is posted to the general ledger. Determines the fiscal period and affects financial statement balances.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Date; GAAP Revenue Recognition

description: Two-digit posting key that determines whether the line is a debit or credit and controls field status and account type. Standard SAP posting keys include 40 (debit GL), 50 (credit GL), 01 (debit customer), 11 (credit customer), 31 (debit vendor), 21 (credit vendor).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Posting Key Configuration

description: Profit center responsible for this line item. Supports P&L reporting and EBITDA tracking by business unit or client account.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Profit Center Accounting

description: Reference key for external system integration and cross-system reconciliation. May contain invoice numbers, purchase order numbers, or other source document identifiers.
Flag indicating whether this line item is a reversal of a previous entry. Supports audit trails and error correction processes.
Tax amount calculated for this line item in the transaction currency. Supports tax reconciliation and compliance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Calculation; GAAP Tax Accounting

description: Tax code that determines the tax rate and tax account for this line item. Supports VAT, sales tax, and other jurisdiction-specific tax calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Tax Configuration; GAAP Tax Accounting

description: Trading partner company code for intercompany transactions. Supports elimination entries and consolidated financial statements.
Value date for cash management and liquidity planning. Represents the effective date for interest calculation and cash flow forecasting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Cash Management; Treasury Operations

description: Primary key for bank_account
Reference to the cost center responsible for transactions and reconciliation of this bank account.

Reference to the legal entity that owns or controls this bank account.
Self-referencing FK on bank_account (sweep_to_bank_account_id)
The date on which the bank account was closed or deactivated, if applicable.
The registered name or title of the bank account as it appears on bank statements.
The unique account number assigned by the financial institution. May be domestic format or IBAN.
The date on which the bank account was originally opened with the financial institution.
The primary business purpose or function for which this bank account is designated within the agency's financial operations.
Current operational status of the bank account in the agency's financial system.
Classification of the bank account based on its purpose and functionality.

The current balance of the bank account as of the last reconciliation or statement date.
The date on which the current balance amount was last updated or verified.
The BIC or SWIFT code uniquely identifying the financial institution for international transactions.
The legal name of the financial institution holding the account.
The frequency at which the bank provides account statements.
The unique code identifying the bank branch within the financial institution's network.
The name of the specific bank branch where the account is held.
Indicates whether check writing privileges are enabled for this account.
The timestamp when this bank account record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO currency code representing the primary currency of the bank account.
The annual interest rate percentage applied to the account balance, if applicable.
Indicates whether this is the primary or default bank account for the associated legal entity or cost center.
The date of the most recent bank reconciliation performed for this account.
The minimum balance that must be maintained in the account as per bank or internal policy requirements.
The timestamp when this bank account record was last modified or updated.
Indicates whether the account supports transactions in multiple currencies.
Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the bank account.
Indicates whether online banking access is enabled for this account.
The maximum overdraft or credit facility limit approved for this bank account.
The email address of the primary bank contact for account management and support.
The name of the primary contact person at the bank for account-related inquiries and issues.
The phone number of the primary bank contact for this account.
The scheduled frequency at which bank reconciliation is performed for this account.
The domestic routing number (ABA routing number in US, sort code in UK) used for electronic funds transfers.
The method by which bank statements are delivered to the account holder.
Indicates whether wire transfer functionality is enabled for this account.
Unique identifier for the media cost reconciliation record. Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Media cost reconciliations compare planned vs actual spend by brand to ensure accurate brand-level cost accounting. Critical for brand budget variance analysis, brand profitability accuracy, and resol
Reference to the media buy that this reconciliation record pertains to. Links to the media buy master record.

Foreign key linking to performance.click_event. Business justification: Click discrepancy reconciliation between ad server and publisher systems; financial adjustments require linking to click events.
FK to finance.cost_center
Foreign key linking to performance.delivery_pacing. Business justification: Pacing data informs reconciliation of planned vs actual delivery and spend; variance analysis requires pacing metrics.

Foreign key linking to performance.impression_event. Business justification: Media cost reconciliation compares planned vs actual impressions; core use case requires linking reconciliation records to actual delivery events.
Identifier for the media vendor or publisher who provided the media inventory and submitted the invoice.

Foreign key linking to finance.vendor_payable. Business justification: Media cost reconciliations match planned media costs against actual vendor invoices and result in vendor payables being created or adjusted. This FK links the reconciliation to the resulting vendor pa

Foreign key linking to performance.viewability_measurement. Business justification: Viewability discrepancies drive make-goods and financial adjustments; reconciliation requires linking to viewability measurement data.
The actual cost per thousand impressions calculated from billed cost and delivered impressions. Calculated as (billed_cost_amount / delivered_impressions) * 1000.
Name or identifier of the person who approved the final reconciled amount. Used for accountability and audit trail.
Date and time when the reconciliation was formally approved for payment processing and client billing. Represents the completion of the reconciliation workflow.
The actual cost amount invoiced by the vendor for the media delivery. Represents the vendor's claim for payment based on delivered impressions or placements.
End date of the billing period covered by this reconciliation record. Defines the conclusion of the media delivery window being reconciled.
Start date of the billing period covered by this reconciliation record. Defines the beginning of the media delivery window being reconciled.
The difference between planned CPM and actual CPM. Used to identify rate discrepancies and pricing issues.
Date and time when this reconciliation record was first created in the system. Represents the start of the reconciliation lifecycle.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this reconciliation record. Ensures consistent currency handling across multi-currency campaigns.
The actual number of impressions delivered by the vendor as confirmed by third-party ad serving platforms (Google Campaign Manager 360, DSP platforms). Used for delivery variance calculation.
Boolean indicator of whether this reconciliation record is currently under dispute. True indicates active dispute; False indicates no dispute or resolved dispute.
Date when a dispute was formally opened for this reconciliation record. Null if no dispute has been raised.
Free-text notes documenting the dispute resolution process, including vendor communications, adjustments made, and final agreement terms.
Date when the dispute was formally resolved and the reconciliation was cleared. Null if dispute is still open or no dispute was raised.
Date when the reconciled amount was posted to the general ledger in SAP S/4HANA FI. Used for financial period closing and P&L reporting.
The difference between planned impressions and delivered impressions. Positive values indicate over-delivery; negative values indicate under-delivery.
The impression variance expressed as a percentage of planned impressions. Calculated as (impression_variance / planned_impressions) * 100. Used for delivery performance tracking.
The insertion order number associated with this media buy. Unique identifier for the IO document authorizing the media placement.
Date and time when this reconciliation record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail.
Boolean indicator of whether this reconciliation includes make-good impressions provided by the vendor to compensate for under-delivery or quality issues.
Number of additional impressions provided by the vendor as make-good compensation. Null or zero if no make-good was provided.
Date by which payment to the vendor is due based on contract payment terms. Used for accounts payable scheduling and cash flow management.
The originally planned media cost as specified in the insertion order and media plan. Represents the budgeted amount for this media buy.
The planned cost per thousand impressions as negotiated in the media buy. Used as the baseline rate for cost reconciliation.
The number of impressions originally planned and contracted in the insertion order. Used as the baseline for delivery reconciliation.
The final agreed-upon cost amount after reconciliation adjustments, dispute resolution, and variance analysis. This is the amount approved for payment and client billing.
Unique document number generated by Mediaocean Prisma for this reconciliation transaction. Used for audit trail and cross-system reference.
Current workflow status of the reconciliation record. Tracks the lifecycle from initial variance detection through dispute resolution to final clearance and payment approval.
Date when the third-party verification data was received or confirmed. Used for audit trail and data freshness tracking.
Name of the third-party ad serving or verification platform that provided the delivered impressions data (e.g., Google Campaign Manager 360, The Trade Desk, Nielsen, Comscore).
The difference between planned cost and reconciled amount. Positive values indicate over-billing or over-delivery; negative values indicate under-delivery or cost savings.
The variance amount expressed as a percentage of the planned cost. Calculated as (variance_amount / planned_cost_amount) * 100. Used for threshold-based alerting and variance reporting.
Standardized code indicating the primary reason for the cost variance. Used for root cause analysis and vendor performance tracking.
Detailed free-text explanation of the variance reason, including context, supporting evidence, and resolution notes. Provides audit trail for financial reconciliation.
External reference identifier provided by the media vendor or publisher for this billing cycle. May include invoice number or billing statement reference.
Unique identifier for the payment transaction record. Primary key for the payment entity.
Reference to the client or advertiser associated with this payment. Populated for inbound AR receipts and client-related outbound payments.
Reference to the bank account from which the payment was debited (outbound) or to which it was credited (inbound). Links to the agency's cash management system.

Reference to the invoice being settled by this payment. Links payment to the originating billing document for reconciliation.
Foreign key linking to media.media_insertion_order. Business justification: Payments allocated to insertion orders for cash flow tracking and payment reconciliation in media finance operations.
Reference to the original payment record that this payment reverses. Populated only when is_reversal is True.
Reference to the vendor, publisher, or supplier associated with this payment. Populated for outbound AP disbursements.

The gross monetary value of the payment transaction in the specified currency. Represents the total cash movement before any adjustments.
The date on which the payment was approved. Null if not yet approved or if approval is not required.
Indicates whether the payment has been approved for processing: pending approval (awaiting authorization), approved (authorized for execution), or rejected (denied by approver).
The name or identifier of the person who approved the payment for processing. Used for audit trail and segregation of duties compliance.
The unique transaction identifier assigned by the bank or payment processor. Used for bank reconciliation and dispute resolution.
The interface or channel through which the payment was initiated or received: online portal, mobile app, direct bank transfer, lockbox service, manual entry by AP/AR clerk, or API integration.
The document number assigned during the clearing process in the financial system. Links payment to the clearing entry that offsets the receivable or payable.
The date and time when this payment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the currency in which the payment was made (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY).
Indicates whether this is a payment received from a client (inbound/AR receipt) or a payment made to a vendor (outbound/AP disbursement).
The monetary value of any early payment discount or settlement discount applied to this payment. Null if no discount was taken.
The foreign exchange rate applied to convert the payment amount to the agency's functional currency. Null for payments in functional currency.
The fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year in which this payment was recorded. Used for period-based financial reporting.
The fiscal year in which this payment was recorded, based on the payment date. Used for financial period reporting and year-end closing.
The payment amount converted to the agency's functional currency using the exchange rate. Used for consolidated financial reporting and P&L (Profit and Loss) analysis.
Boolean flag indicating whether this payment is a reversal of a previous payment transaction. True if this is a reversal entry, False otherwise.
Free-text memo or notes field providing additional context about the payment. May include invoice references, campaign details, or special instructions.
The date and time when this payment record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.
The name of the party receiving the payment. For inbound payments, this is the agency entity. For outbound payments, this is the vendor, publisher, or supplier.
The name of the party making the payment. For inbound payments, this is the client or advertiser. For outbound payments, this is the agency entity.
The date on which the payment transaction was executed or received. This is the business event date for cash flow recognition.
The financial instrument or mechanism used to execute the payment: ACH (Automated Clearing House), wire transfer, check, credit card, debit card, or electronic transfer.

Current lifecycle status of the payment: pending (initiated but not cleared), cleared (funds transferred), failed (transaction rejected), cancelled (voided before clearing), reversed (funds returned after clearing), or reconciled (matched to invoice/payable).

Classification of the payment purpose: invoice payment (standard settlement), advance payment (prepayment), refund (return of funds), rebate (volume discount payment), commission (agency fee), or retainer (upfront fee).
The date on which the payment was successfully reconciled to its corresponding invoice or bank statement. Null if not yet reconciled.

Indicates whether the payment has been matched to its corresponding invoice, payable, or bank statement entry: unreconciled (no match), partially reconciled (partial match), fully reconciled (complete match), or disputed (discrepancy identified).
Externally-known unique reference number assigned to this payment transaction for tracking and reconciliation purposes. Used in bank statements and remittance advice.
Reference number or identifier from the remittance advice document accompanying the payment. Used to match payment to specific invoices or line items.
The payment document number generated in SAP S/4HANA FI module. Primary reference for integration with the ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) system.
The contractual payment terms applicable to this payment (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30). Indicates the agreed-upon payment schedule and any early payment discounts.
The date on which the funds become available in the receiving account or are debited from the paying account. Used for cash position and liquidity management.
The amount of tax withheld from the payment as required by tax regulations. Common for cross-border payments and vendor payments subject to withholding.
Unique identifier for the vendor payable record. Primary key for the vendor payable entity.
Reference to the client account for which this vendor expense was incurred. Supports client-level profitability analysis and budget tracking.

Reference to the advertising campaign for which this vendor cost was incurred. Enables campaign-level margin analysis and Return on Investment (ROI) calculation.

The date the vendor payable was approved for payment. Marks the transition from pending review to authorized for payment processing.
The user identifier of the person who approved the vendor payable for payment. Required for financial controls and audit trail compliance.
The timestamp when this vendor payable record was first created in the system. Required for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payable amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency financial reporting and foreign exchange management.
The discount amount available if payment is made by the early payment discount deadline. Supports cash management optimization through early payment incentives.
The last date by which payment must be made to qualify for the early payment discount. Used for cash flow optimization decisions.
The date the payable was flagged as disputed. Used for aging analysis of unresolved disputes and vendor relationship management.
Free-text description of the reason for disputing the vendor payable. Populated when payment_status is disputed. Used for vendor negotiation and dispute tracking.
The date by which payment must be made to the vendor according to the agreed payment terms. Used for cash flow planning and aging analysis.
The fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year in which this vendor payable is recorded. Used for period-based financial reporting and variance analysis.
