description: Number of hours worked beyond the standard scheduled hours in the pay period that qualify for overtime pay. Sourced from Workday HCM Time Tracking. Required for FLSA compliance and production cost reporting.
reference: Workday HCM Time Tracking; Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA)

description: Compensation paid for hours worked beyond the standard work schedule in the pay period. Relevant for production crews, shoot days, and campaign crunch periods. Tracked separately for FLSA compliance and project cost allocation.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA)

description: The actual date on which compensation was disbursed to the worker. This is the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP (date-precision) for the payroll transaction — the real-world event date when funds were released.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA)

description: The cadence at which the worker is paid. Drives payroll run scheduling and annualization of compensation figures for P&L reporting and FTE cost analysis.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA)

description: The last calendar date of the pay period covered by this payroll record. Defines the closing boundary of the earnings window.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA)

description: The first calendar date of the pay period covered by this payroll record. Together with pay_period_end_date, defines the earnings window for gross pay calculation.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA)

description: The worker's compensation rate applicable for this pay period — expressed as hourly rate for non-exempt workers or as the annualized salary divided by pay periods for exempt employees. Sourced from Workday HCM Compensation module.
reference: Workday HCM Compensation Module; Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA)

description: Indicates whether the worker's compensation is structured as an hourly rate, fixed salary, daily rate (common for freelancers and production crew), or project-based flat rate. Determines how pay_rate is applied to calculate base_pay_amount.
reference: Workday HCM Compensation Module; IAB Talent Standards

description: The mechanism by which net pay was disbursed to the worker. Direct deposit is standard for FTE employees; wire transfer may be used for international contractors and production talent.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; NACHA Operating Rules

description: Externally-known business identifier for this payroll record, formatted as PR-{YYYY}-{MM}-{sequence}. Used for reconciliation with SAP S/4HANA FI/CO postings and Mediaocean Prisma billing reconciliation.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: Current lifecycle state of this payroll record within the payroll processing workflow. Tracks progression from initial calculation through final disbursement or reversal. LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this TRANSACTION_HEADER entity.

Employee pre-tax or Roth retirement plan contribution (e.g., 401(k), 403(b)) deducted from gross pay in this pay period. Tracked separately from other benefits deductions for IRS Form W-2 Box 12 reporting.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; IRS Publication 560; ERISA

description: Flags whether this payroll record represents a reversal of a previously processed payroll disbursement. Reversals occur due to overpayments, incorrect bank details, or payroll corrections. Linked to the original record via original_payroll_record_id.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: Employee portion of Social Security (OASDI) tax withheld in this pay period. Calculated at the statutory rate on wages up to the annual wage base. Required for IRS Form W-2 reporting.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; IRS Publication 15; Social Security Act

description: Amount of state income tax withheld from the worker's gross pay in this pay period. Varies by state of employment. Relevant for agencies with multi-state operations and talent working across production locations.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; State Tax Authorities

description: The primary tax jurisdiction code applicable to this payroll record, identifying the state/locality combination for tax withholding purposes. Critical for agencies with talent working across multiple states and production locations.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; IRS Publication 15; State Tax Authorities

description: Aggregate of all pre-tax and post-tax deductions applied in this pay period, including benefits contributions, garnishments, and voluntary deductions. Represents the adjustment component of the MONETARY_TRIPLET (gross_pay_amount minus total_deductions_amount yields net_pay_amount).
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: The timestamp of the most recent modification to this payroll record in the Workday HCM Payroll module. RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED field for the TRANSACTION_HEADER. Tracks corrections, status changes, and reversals.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; SOX Section 404; GDPR Article 5

description: Classification of the worker receiving this payroll disbursement. Determines applicable tax withholding rules, benefits eligibility, and payment processing logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FTE|contractor|freelancer|influencer|actor|photographer|director|production_crew — promote to reference product]
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; IRS Publication 15; GDPR Article 4

description: Cumulative gross pay earned by the worker from the start of the calendar year through this pay period. Required for W-2 preparation, Social Security wage base tracking, and annual compensation reporting.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; IRS Form W-2

description: Cumulative total of all federal, state, and local taxes withheld from the worker's pay from the start of the calendar year through this pay period. Required for W-2 preparation and tax reconciliation.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll Module; IRS Form W-2

description: Primary key for payroll_run
Self-referencing FK on payroll_run (correction_of_payroll_run_id)
Reference to the primary cost center to which payroll expenses from this run are allocated for management accounting.
Reference to the user who initiated or created this payroll run.
Reference to the legal entity (company, subsidiary, or branch) under which this payroll run is executed for tax and compliance purposes.
Reference to the user who last modified this payroll run record.
Reference to the department or organizational unit for which this payroll run is executed, used for segmentation and reporting.
Reference to the original payroll run that this run reverses or corrects, populated only when is_reversal is true.
Reference to the payroll calendar that governs the schedule and timing rules for this payroll run.
Reference to the user (typically HR manager or payroll administrator) who approved this payroll run for processing.
Date and time when this payroll run was approved for processing.
External batch identifier assigned by the payroll processing system for tracking and reconciliation purposes.
Date and time when this payroll run record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which payroll amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).
Number of employees, contractors, freelancers, and talent included in this payroll run.
Total employer-side tax obligations for this payroll run including employer portion of social security, Medicare, unemployment taxes, and other statutory contributions.
Fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year to which this payroll run is attributed.
Fiscal year to which this payroll run is attributed for financial reporting and tax compliance purposes.
The recurring schedule frequency for this payroll run: weekly, biweekly (every two weeks), semimonthly (twice per month), monthly, quarterly, or annual.
Date on which payroll expenses and liabilities from this run are posted to the general ledger for financial accounting.
Total gross compensation amount for all employees and talent included in this payroll run before any deductions.
Boolean flag indicating whether this payroll run is a reversal or correction of a previous payroll run.
Date and time when this payroll run record was last modified or updated.
Total net compensation amount disbursed to employees and talent after all deductions, representing the actual payment amount.
Free-text notes or comments about this payroll run, including special circumstances, adjustments, or processing instructions.
The last date of the pay period covered by this payroll run.
The first date of the pay period covered by this payroll run.
The date on which payments from this payroll run are scheduled to be disbursed to employees, contractors, and talent.
Primary payment disbursement method used for this payroll run: direct deposit (ACH), physical check, wire transfer, payroll card, or cash.
Date and time when this payroll run completed processing and payments were initiated.
Name or identifier of the payroll processing system or platform used to execute this payroll run (e.g., Workday HCM, ADP, internal system).
Business identifier for the payroll run, typically formatted as PR-YYYYMMDD or similar convention used for external reference and reporting.
Classification of the payroll run indicating the purpose: regular scheduled payroll, off-cycle payment, bonus distribution, commission payout, adjustment run, or final settlement.
Current lifecycle status of the payroll run indicating its workflow state from draft through completion or cancellation.
Date and time when payment instructions were transmitted to the banking institution for disbursement.
Primary tax jurisdiction or geographic region governing tax withholding and reporting requirements for this payroll run (e.g., state, province, country).
Sum of all deductions applied in this payroll run including taxes, benefits, garnishments, and other withholdings.
Unique system-generated identifier for the capacity plan record. Primary key for the capacity_plan data product in the talent domain.
reference: Workday HCM - Resource Planning; Workfront - Resource Management

description: Identifier of the specific team or squad within the department for which capacity is being planned (e.g., Brand Creative Team, Programmatic Buying Team). More granular than department.
reference: Workfront - Resource Planning; Workday HCM - Talent Management

description: Identifier of the agency department or business unit for which this capacity plan is prepared (e.g., Creative, Media, Strategy, Account Management, Production). Links to the organizational hierarchy.
reference: Workday HCM - Core HR; SAP S/4HANA - CO Cost Center

description: Identifier of the employee (typically a Resource Manager, Department Head, or Operations Lead) responsible for authoring, maintaining, and approving this capacity plan. Maps to Workday HCM worker record.
reference: Workday HCM - Core HR; Workfront - Resource Planning

description: External reference identifier for the corresponding resource plan record in Workfront Resource Planning module. Enables bi-directional synchronization between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational system of record.
Calendar date on which the capacity plan was formally approved by the designated approver. Nullable until plan status transitions to approved.
reference: Workday HCM - Workforce Planning; SAP S/4HANA - CO Controlling

description: Total available productive hours across all planned FTE-equivalent resources for the planning period, net of holidays, PTO, and non-billable overhead. Primary supply-side metric for capacity gap analysis.
reference: Workday HCM - Time Tracking; Workfront - Resource Planning

description: Comma-separated list of pipeline campaign or pitch identifiers whose forecasted resource demand is incorporated into this capacity plan. Links capacity planning to new business development activity.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Sales Cloud; Workfront - Project Management

description: Net difference between available_hours and forecasted_demand_hours for the planning period. Negative values indicate a capacity shortfall requiring freelancer augmentation or scope negotiation; positive values indicate surplus bench capacity.
reference: Workday HCM - Workforce Planning; Workfront - Resource Planning; 4As Resource Planning Guidelines

description: Hours of resource demand backed by signed SOWs, confirmed Insertion Orders (IOs), or approved campaign briefs. Subset of forecasted_demand_hours representing firm commitments, used for conservative capacity planning.
reference: Mediaocean Prisma - Buying; Salesforce CRM - Service Cloud; IAB IO Standards

description: Timestamp when this capacity plan record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity; IAB Data Governance Standards

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which labor_cost_budget and related financial figures are denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Supports multi-currency agency operations.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA - FI Financial Accounting

description: Functional discipline or craft specialty covered by this capacity plan (e.g., Art Direction, Copywriting, Media Planning, Data Analytics, Account Management, Production, UX Design). Aligns with Workday HCM job families. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: art_direction|copywriting|media_planning|data_analytics|account_management|production|ux_design|strategy|public_relations|digital_marketing — promote to reference product]
reference: Workday HCM - Talent Management; 4As Workforce Classification Standards

description: Total estimated hours of resource demand for the planning period, derived from confirmed SOWs, active campaign schedules, and pipeline-weighted new business opportunities. Primary demand-side metric for capacity gap analysis.
reference: Workfront - Resource Planning; Mediaocean Prisma - Media Planning; Salesforce CRM - Sales Cloud

description: Total hours planned to be sourced from freelancers and contractors (non-FTE resources) during the planning period. Informs freelancer budget allocation and vendor management.
reference: Workday HCM - Payroll; SAP S/4HANA - MM Procurement; Workfront - Resource Planning

description: Number of freelance or contract resources planned to be available in the talent pool for this discipline/team during the planning period. Used for bench management and rapid campaign ramp-up planning.
reference: Workday HCM - Talent Management; Workfront - Resource Planning

description: Count of fixed-term contractors and agency-of-record staff included in this capacity plan. Distinct from permanent FTEs and freelancers for cost classification and P&L reporting purposes.
reference: Workday HCM - Core HR; SAP S/4HANA - CO Controlling

description: Count of permanent, full-time employees included in this capacity plan for the planning period. Excludes contractors and freelancers. Used for P&L headcount reporting and EBITDA margin analysis.
reference: Workday HCM - Core HR; SAP S/4HANA - CO Controlling

description: Indicates whether this capacity plan was created specifically to support a new business pitch or RFP response, as opposed to ongoing campaign delivery. Enables separate tracking of pitch-related resource commitments.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Sales Cloud; 4As New Business Planning Guidelines

description: Total planned labor cost (in base currency) for the team/discipline during the planning period, covering salaries, contractor fees, and freelancer rates. Key input to finance for margin planning and P&L forecasting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA - CO Controlling; Workday HCM - Payroll; ANA Agency Compensation Guidelines

description: Hours of resource demand attributable to pipeline opportunities (RFPs, pitches, and proposals in progress) weighted by probability of conversion. Supports new business pitch resourcing and bench management decisions.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Sales Cloud; 4As New Business Planning Guidelines

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the capacity plan, used in cross-system references, SOW alignment, and finance reporting (e.g., CP-BRAND01-2025).
reference: Workfront - Resource Planning; Mediaocean Prisma - Media Planning

description: Last calendar date of the planning period covered by this capacity plan. Defines the effective end of the capacity window. Nullable for rolling/open-ended plans.
reference: Workday HCM - Workforce Planning; Workfront - Resource Planning

description: Human-readable name describing the capacity plan, typically referencing the team, discipline, campaign, or planning period (e.g., 'Q3 2025 Creative Studio Capacity Plan').
reference: Workday HCM - Workforce Planning; Workfront - Resource Planning

description: Free-text field for resource managers to document planning assumptions, risk factors, scenario rationale, or escalation notes associated with this capacity plan version.
reference: Workfront - Resource Planning; Workday HCM - Workforce Planning

description: First calendar date of the planning period covered by this capacity plan. Defines the effective start of the capacity window for resource allocation and demand matching.
reference: Workday HCM - Workforce Planning; Workfront - Resource Planning

description: Current lifecycle state of the capacity plan, governing whether it is editable, under review, approved for use in resource allocation, or replaced by a newer version.
reference: Workday HCM - Workforce Planning; Workfront - Project Management

description: Monotonically incrementing version number of the capacity plan, enabling scenario comparison and audit of plan revisions over time. Version 1 is the initial baseline.
reference: Workfront - Resource Planning; IAB Workforce Planning Standards

description: Total planned headcount expressed in Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) units for the planning period, combining permanent employees, contractors, and freelancers normalized to a standard 40-hour work week. Core input to utilization target setting.
reference: Workday HCM - Workforce Planning; 4As FTE Calculation Standards

description: Granularity of the planning horizon covered by this capacity plan — weekly for sprint-level resource management, monthly for campaign ramp-ups, quarterly for QBR-aligned forecasting, or annual for strategic workforce planning.
reference: Workday HCM - Workforce Planning; 4As Resource Planning Guidelines

description: Projected revenue attributable to the team/discipline for the planning period, derived from confirmed SOW values and pipeline-weighted opportunities. Used by finance for revenue forecasting and EBITDA planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA - CO Controlling; Salesforce CRM - Sales Cloud; ANA Agency Compensation Guidelines

description: Scenario modeling classification for this capacity plan version — best case (maximum pipeline win rate), expected (most likely outcome), worst case (minimum pipeline conversion), or base (approved baseline). Supports sensitivity analysis for finance margin planning.
reference: 4As Resource Planning Guidelines; ANA Workforce Analytics Standards

description: Comma-separated list of SOW identifiers whose confirmed resource demand is incorporated into this capacity plan. Provides traceability between capacity commitments and contractual obligations.
reference: Salesforce CRM - Service Cloud; Mediaocean Prisma - Buying; IAB SOW Standards

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this capacity plan record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used to detect stale plans and trigger re-approval workflows.
reference: GDPR Article 5 - Data Integrity; IAB Data Governance Standards

description: Target billable utilization rate (as a percentage of available hours) set by finance for this team/discipline during the planning period. Drives margin planning and FTE sizing decisions. Typically 65–85% for agency talent.
reference: SAP S/4HANA - CO Controlling; 4As Financial Benchmarking Standards; ANA Agency Compensation Guidelines

description: Primary key for holiday_calendar
Self-referencing FK on holiday_calendar (derived_from_holiday_calendar_id)
Reference to the organization or business unit that owns and uses this holiday calendar. Applicable for organizational and production calendars; nullable for national/regional calendars.
Indicates whether this holiday calendar applies to contractors and freelancers. True if contractors observe these holidays; false if they follow different schedules.
Indicates whether this holiday calendar applies to full-time employees. True if FTE staff observe these holidays; false if excluded.

Indicates whether this holiday calendar applies to production crews (directors, photographers, actors, production staff). True if production schedules must respect these holidays; false if production operates independently.
The date on which this holiday calendar was formally approved for use in resource planning, payroll, and time tracking systems.
Name or identifier of the person or role who approved this holiday calendar for use. Typically an HR manager, operations director, or authorized administrator.
Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the calendar for system integration and reference (e.g., 'US_FED_2024', 'UK_BANK', 'PROD_CAL').
Business-friendly name of the holiday calendar (e.g., 'US Federal Holidays 2024', 'UK Bank Holidays', 'Agency Production Calendar').

Classification of the holiday calendar indicating its scope and purpose: national (country-wide public holidays), regional (state/province holidays), organizational (company-specific holidays), production (shoot/production blackout dates), project (campaign-specific non-working days).
Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the primary country jurisdiction for this holiday calendar (e.g., USA, GBR, CAN, FRA). Used for national and regional calendars.
The date and time when this holiday calendar record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Detailed narrative describing the purpose, scope, and applicability of this holiday calendar, including any special rules or exceptions.
The last date on which this holiday calendar is applicable. Nullable for open-ended calendars that remain in effect until superseded.
The first date on which this holiday calendar becomes applicable for resource planning, payroll, and time tracking purposes.
The unique identifier for this holiday calendar in the source system (e.g., Workday calendar ID, Workfront calendar GUID). Used for integration and synchronization with upstream systems.
Indicates whether this is the default holiday calendar for the organization or region. True if this calendar should be automatically applied to new employees or projects; false otherwise.
The date and time when this holiday calendar record was last updated. Used for change tracking and synchronization with downstream systems.
Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or exceptions related to this holiday calendar (e.g., 'Includes floating holidays', 'Production blackout dates for Q4 campaign').

Defines how holidays that fall on weekends are observed. Values: actual_date (holiday observed on the actual date regardless of day of week), nearest_weekday (shifted to nearest weekday), following_monday (shifted to following Monday if on weekend), preceding_friday (shifted to preceding Friday if on weekend).

State, province, or regional subdivision code for calendars that apply to specific geographic regions within a country (e.g., 'CA' for California, 'ON' for Ontario, 'NSW' for New South Wales). Nullable for national or organizational calendars.
The system of record from which this holiday calendar was sourced or integrated (e.g., 'Workday HCM', 'Workfront', 'Manual Entry', 'Government API'). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.

Current lifecycle status of the holiday calendar. Active calendars are in use for resource planning and time tracking; draft calendars are under review; inactive calendars are temporarily disabled; archived calendars are retained for historical reference only.
IANA time zone identifier for this holiday calendar (e.g., 'America/New_York', 'Europe/London', 'Asia/Tokyo'). Used to determine exact holiday start/end times for global operations.
The total number of distinct holiday dates defined in this calendar. Used for quick reference and capacity planning calculations.
Sequential version number for this holiday calendar. Incremented each time the calendar is updated or revised. Supports change tracking and audit history.

Defines the standard weekend days for this calendar. Values: sat_sun (Saturday and Sunday, common in Western countries), fri_sat (Friday and Saturday, common in Middle East), sun_only (Sunday only), none (no standard weekend, e.g., shift-based operations).
The calculated number of working days in the calendar year after excluding weekends and holidays. Used for resource capacity planning, project scheduling, and workload forecasting.
The calendar year (YYYY) for which this holiday calendar is defined. Many organizations maintain separate calendar records per year to accommodate year-specific holiday variations.
Unique system-generated identifier for each employee leave and absence request record in the Workday HCM Absence Management module. Serves as the primary key for this entity.
Reference to the manager or HR representative who reviewed and approved or rejected this leave request. Supports approval workflow tracking and accountability.

Reference to the primary Workfront project most impacted by this leave request. Enables direct linkage between absence management and project resource allocation, supporting capacity planning and WIP (Work in Progress) management for advertising campaigns.

description: Reference to the employee (Full-Time Equivalent, freelancer, contractor, or production crew member) who submitted this leave request. Links to the talent/employee master record.

description: Free-text comments entered by the approver during the review process, documenting conditions, modifications, or reasons for approval or rejection. Sourced from Workday HCM Absence Management workflow comments.

Total number of working days formally approved for the leave period. May differ from requested_duration_days if the approver modified the leave window or partial approval was granted.
The last calendar date of the leave period as formally approved. May differ from the requested end date if the approver modified the leave window.
The first calendar date of the leave period as formally approved by the manager or HR. May differ from the requested start date if the approver modified the leave window.

External reference number or identifier for the legal representation contract document stored in the contract management system or document repository.
System timestamp when the representation agreement record was created in the data platform.

Defines the exclusivity terms of the representation agreement. reference: 4As Standard Contract Terms; IAB Talent Rights Framework

description: Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Usage rights are granted for specific brand identities, not just campaigns. Critical for brand compliance checks, exclusivity enforcement across brand portfolios, and rights clearance workflows. Adver
Reference to the advertising campaign for which this usage right was acquired. Enables campaign-level rights compliance tracking and reporting.
reference: Google Campaign Manager 360; IAB Campaign Standards

description: Foreign key linking to performance.conversion_event. Business justification: Supports talent performance attribution—measuring which on-screen talent (actors, influencers) drive conversions to justify usage rights investments and inform renewal/rate negotiations. Standard ROI
Reference to the specific creative asset (advertisement, photograph, video, audio recording) to which this usage right applies. Links to the creative domain asset registry.
reference: Adobe Creative Cloud DAM; Workfront DAM; IAB Digital Asset Standards

description: Foreign key linking to contract.sow. Business justification: Usage rights are often negotiated within SOW scope; proper FK enables tracking which SOW governs talent usage rights, critical for rights clearance workflows, compliance audits, and ensuring usage ter

Foreign key linking to vendor.supplier. Business justification: Usage rights for talent (actors, influencers, models) are negotiated through and managed by vendor suppliers (talent agencies, rights management firms). Critical for rights clearance workflows, paymen

The Workday HCM system worker identifier for the talent, enabling integration between the usage rights record and the talent's HR, payroll, and time-tracking records in Workday. Supports residual payment processing through the payroll module.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; Workday Payroll Integration Standards

description: The name or identifier of the business affairs manager, legal counsel, or authorized agency representative who formally approved and cleared this usage right. Provides audit trail for rights clearance accountability.
reference: 4As Business Affairs Best Practices; ANA Talent Rights Governance

description: The California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) opt-out status for the talent's personal data usage in California-targeted campaigns. 'opted_in' permits use; 'opted_out' restricts use of personal data for California audiences; 'not_applicable' for non-California markets.
reference: CCPA California Civil Code Section 1798.100; FTC Privacy Regulations; NAI Compliance Standards

description: The date on which the usage right was formally cleared by the legal or business affairs team, confirming all contractual and union obligations have been satisfied and the creative asset may be deployed.
reference: 4As Business Affairs Best Practices; ANA Rights Clearance Standards

description: The timestamp when this usage right record was first created in the system. Corresponds to the MASTER_AGREEMENT RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category. Supports data lineage, audit trails, and compliance reporting.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Accountability); 4As Data Governance Standards; Workday HCM Audit Trail

description: The product or service category within which exclusivity applies when exclusivity_scope is 'category_exclusive' (e.g., 'automotive', 'financial services', 'consumer packaged goods'). Null when exclusivity_scope is 'exclusive' or 'non_exclusive'.
reference: 4As Standard Talent Contract Terms; ANA Talent Rights Best Practices

description: Defines whether the usage right is exclusive to this agency/client, non-exclusive (talent may appear in competing campaigns), or category-exclusive (exclusive within a specific product/service category). Exclusive rights typically command higher fees and restrict talent from competitor engagements.
reference: 4As Standard Talent Contract Terms; ANA Talent Rights Best Practices; SAG-AFTRA Exclusivity Provisions

description: The agreed monetary fee paid to the talent or their representative for this usage right grant. For buyout structures, this is the total one-time fee. For residual structures, this is the base session fee. Expressed in the currency specified by fee_currency_code.
reference: SAG-AFTRA Scale Rates; ACTRA Fee Schedules; BECTU Rate Cards; 4As Financial Reconciliation Standards

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the fee amount (e.g., 'USD', 'GBP', 'EUR', 'CAD'). Required for multi-territory rights where fees may be denominated in local currency.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Currency Management

description: Indicates whether explicit GDPR-compliant consent has been obtained from the talent for processing and using their personal data (including likeness, voice, and biometric data) in the specified territories. Mandatory for EU/EEA market usage.
reference: GDPR Article 6 and Article 9; EASA Cross-Border Advertising Standards; IAB Europe Consent Framework

description: Indicates whether the talent (influencer) is contractually and regulatorily required to disclose the paid/sponsored nature of content under FTC Endorsement Guidelines or ASA CAP Code. Applicable primarily when talent_role is 'influencer'. Critical for regulatory compliance.
reference: FTC Endorsement Guides 16 CFR Part 255; ASA CAP Code Section 2; EASA Digital Advertising Standards

description: Indicates whether this usage right is granted in perpetuity with no expiration date. When True, rights_end_date should be null. Perpetual rights are typically associated with full buyout arrangements and carry higher upfront fees.
reference: 4As Standard Talent Contract Terms; ANA Talent Rights Best Practices

description: The timestamp when this usage right record was most recently modified, including status changes, renewals, fee updates, or clearance decisions. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.

The date on which the usage right was first granted, regardless of subsequent renewals or extensions. Provides the anchor point for tracking the full lifecycle of the rights relationship with the talent.
reference: SAG-AFTRA Contract Administration; 4As Talent Rights Lifecycle Management

description: The fee arrangement governing talent compensation for this usage right. 'buyout' means a one-time payment granting unlimited use within agreed scope; 'residual' means ongoing payments per use cycle per union scale; 'session_fee' means payment for the recording session only with limited use; 'hybrid' combines upfront buyout with residual obligations for certain use categories.
reference: SAG-AFTRA Residuals Schedule; ACTRA Payment Terms; BECTU Fee Structures; 4As Talent Compensation Standards

description: The contractually stipulated financial penalty payable to the talent or their union if the usage right is violated (e.g., unauthorized use beyond territory, media type, or expiry date). Expressed in fee_currency_code. Critical for financial risk management and compliance monitoring.
reference: SAG-AFTRA Liquidated Damages Provisions; ACTRA Penalty Schedule; 4As Risk Management Standards

description: Specific digital platforms or media outlets explicitly excluded from or restricted within this usage right (e.g., 'no TikTok', 'YouTube only', 'no competitor-owned media'). Null if no platform-specific restrictions apply beyond the usage_type classification.
reference: IAB Digital Platform Standards; 4As Digital Rights Management Guidelines; NAI Platform Compliance

description: The number of times this usage right has been renewed or extended since the original grant. Tracks the full renewal history lifecycle and informs cumulative cost analysis and talent relationship management.
reference: 4As Standard Talent Contract Terms; Workday HCM Contract Tracking

description: The date by which the agency must exercise the renewal option to extend the usage right period. Missing this deadline forfeits the renewal option and may require renegotiation at potentially higher rates. Critical for proactive rights management.
reference: 4As Standard Talent Contract Terms; SAG-AFTRA Option Period Provisions; ANA Talent Rights Best Practices

description: Indicates whether the contract includes an option to renew or extend the usage right period beyond the original rights_end_date. When True, renewal_option_deadline and renewal_fee_amount should be populated.
reference: 4As Standard Talent Contract Terms; SAG-AFTRA Option Period Provisions

description: The per-use or per-cycle residual payment rate applicable when payment_structure is 'residual' or 'hybrid'. Expressed as a monetary amount per broadcast cycle or as a percentage of the original session fee per union scale. Null for pure buyout arrangements.
reference: SAG-AFTRA Residuals Schedule; ACTRA Residual Payment Terms; BECTU Repeat Fee Structures

description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for this usage right, used in correspondence with talent unions, legal teams, and talent agencies. Follows the agency's internal rights numbering convention (e.g., UR-2024-00123).
reference: SAG-AFTRA Contract Administration; ACTRA Rights Management; 4As Standard Practices

description: Current lifecycle status of the usage right. 'active' indicates rights are cleared and in use; 'pending_clearance' indicates awaiting legal or union approval; 'expired' indicates the usage period has lapsed; 'suspended' indicates rights are temporarily halted pending dispute resolution; 'renewed' indicates an extension has been executed; 'terminated' indicates rights have been revoked or cancelled.
reference: SAG-AFTRA Residuals and Rights Administration; ACTRA Usage Rights Guidelines; BECTU Talent Agreements

description: The legal clearance status of this usage right as determined by the agency's legal or business affairs team. 'cleared' indicates all rights have been legally verified and approved for use; 'pending' indicates clearance is in progress; 'rejected' indicates rights could not be cleared; 'under_review' indicates a dispute or ambiguity is being resolved.
reference: 4As Business Affairs Best Practices; ANA Rights Clearance Standards; FTC Advertising Practices

description: The date on which the usage right expires and the talent's likeness or creative output must be withdrawn from all specified media and territories. Null for perpetual buyout rights. Corresponds to the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_UNTIL category. Critical for compliance monitoring to prevent unauthorized usage.
reference: SAG-AFTRA Contract Administration; ACTRA Usage Period Definitions; 4As Standard Contract Terms

description: The date on which the usage right becomes effective and the talent's likeness or creative output may legally be used in the specified media and territory. Corresponds to the MASTER_AGREEMENT EFFECTIVE_FROM category.
reference: SAG-AFTRA Contract Administration; ACTRA Usage Period Definitions; 4As Standard Contract Terms

description: The professional role or category of the talent whose rights are being managed. Determines applicable union agreements, rate scales, and rights clearance requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: actor|model|photographer|director|influencer|voice_talent|musician|dancer|stunt_performer|extra|presenter — promote to reference product]
reference: SAG-AFTRA Performer Categories; ACTRA Talent Classifications; Workday HCM Job Classifications

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code(s) or region designator indicating the geographic market(s) where the usage right is valid. Multiple territories may be pipe-delimited (e.g., 'USA|CAN|GBR'). 'WW' denotes worldwide rights. Critical for determining applicable union agreements and residual payment obligations.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; SAG-AFTRA International Use Provisions; ACTRA Territorial Rights; EASA Cross-Border Advertising Standards

description: The talent union or guild agreement governing this usage right and its associated payment obligations. Determines applicable scale rates, residual schedules, and usage restrictions. 'NON-UNION' indicates talent not covered by a collective bargaining agreement.
reference: SAG-AFTRA Collective Bargaining Agreement; ACTRA ICA; BECTU Agreements; 4As Union Compliance Standards

description: The specific union contract or signatory agreement number under which this usage right is administered. Required for SAG-AFTRA, ACTRA, and BECTU residual reporting and audit compliance. Null for non-union talent.
reference: SAG-AFTRA Contract Administration; ACTRA ICA Administration; BECTU Contract Registry

description: Free-text field capturing any specific usage restrictions, conditions, or limitations not captured in structured fields (e.g., 'talent must be credited', 'no political advertising use', 'no alteration of likeness permitted', 'must not be used in conjunction with alcohol brands').
reference: 4As Business Affairs Best Practices; FTC Endorsement Guidelines; ASA Advertising Standards

description: The total duration of the usage right period expressed in months, calculated from rights_start_date to rights_end_date. Used for residual cycle calculations, budget forecasting, and rights expiry alerting.
reference: SAG-AFTRA Residuals Schedule; ACTRA Usage Period Definitions

description: Granular classification within the usage type, specifying the exact format or placement (e.g., 'pre-roll video', 'banner display', 'story ad', 'billboard static', 'magazine full-page'). Provides precision for rights clearance and union residual calculations.
reference: IAB Ad Format Taxonomy; SAG-AFTRA Use Category Definitions; MRC Digital Measurement Standards

description: The media channel or format category for which the talent's likeness or creative output may be used. Broadcast covers TV and streaming; digital covers online display and video; OOH (Out-of-Home) covers billboards and transit; social covers social media platforms; print covers magazines and newspapers; radio covers audio-only placements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: broadcast|digital|ooh|social|print|radio|cinema|podcast|dooh|ctv — promote to reference product]
reference: IAB Media Channel Taxonomy; MRC Media Measurement Standards; SAG-AFTRA Use Category Definitions

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each talent acquisition (recruiting) record in the Workday HCM Talent Acquisition module. Primary key for the talent_acquisition data product.
Reference to the candidate profile record associated with this talent acquisition pipeline entry. Links to the candidate master record in the talent domain.

Foreign key linking to talent.org_unit. Business justification: Talent acquisition requisition is for a position within a specific organizational unit. One requisition targets one org_unit; one org_unit can have many requisitions over time. This links the recruitm
Reference to the parent job requisition that initiated this talent acquisition record. Links to the requisition that authorized the open headcount.
Reference to the internal employee record of the hiring manager responsible for this requisition and candidate evaluation.
Actual date on which the hired employee or contractor commenced their engagement. May differ from target_start_date due to notice periods, background check delays, or onboarding scheduling.
reference: Workday HCM Onboarding; Workfront Resource Planning

description: Agreed fee percentage of the hired candidate's first-year base salary payable to the external staffing agency upon successful placement. Used for cost-per-hire calculation and vendor cost management.
reference: Workday HCM Vendor Management; SAP S/4HANA MM Module; ANA Procurement Standards

description: Name of the external staffing or executive search agency engaged to source candidates for this requisition, if applicable. Null for direct-sourced or internally recruited positions.
reference: Workday HCM Vendor Management; SAP S/4HANA MM Module

description: Date on which the candidate submitted their application for the position. Marks the entry point of the candidate into the recruiting pipeline for this requisition.
reference: Workday HCM Candidate Application; GDPR Article 13

description: Number of approved positions authorized under this requisition. Most requisitions are for a single hire, but bulk requisitions for production crews, campaign teams, or seasonal staff may authorize multiple hires.
reference: Workday HCM Requisition; SAP S/4HANA PS Module

description: Current status of the pre-employment background screening process for the candidate. Required for compliance with agency client contracts and regulatory requirements. Blocks onboarding until Passed or Waived.
reference: Workday HCM Onboarding; FTC Background Check Compliance; FCRA

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the offered compensation amounts. Required for multi-currency agencies operating across global markets. Examples: USD, GBP, EUR.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA FI Module

description: Timestamp when this talent acquisition record was first created in the Workday HCM system. Serves as the audit trail creation marker and is used for data lineage and pipeline velocity reporting.
reference: Workday HCM Audit Trail; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

description: Reason recorded when a candidate declines an offer or is rejected during the pipeline. Examples include 'Compensation Below Expectation', 'Accepted Competing Offer', 'Skills Mismatch', 'Culture Fit'. Used for pipeline quality analysis and offer competitiveness benchmarking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Compensation Below Expectation|Accepted Competing Offer|Skills Mismatch|Culture Fit|Role Changed|Candidate Withdrew|Failed Assessment — promote to reference product]
reference: Workday HCM Recruiting Disposition; 4As Talent Acquisition Best Practices

description: Classification of the engagement type for the open position. FTE (Full-Time Equivalent) indicates a permanent headcount addition; Freelancer and Contractor indicate project-based or time-limited engagements common in advertising production and campaign delivery.
reference: Workday HCM Worker Type; ANA Workforce Standards; GDPR Article 4

description: Classification of whether this requisition represents a replacement for a departing employee (Backfill), a net-new addition to the team (New Headcount), a conversion from contractor to FTE (Conversion), or a role upgrade/reclassification (Upgrade). Drives budget approval workflows.
reference: Workday HCM Headcount Planning; SAP S/4HANA PS Module

description: Total number of interview rounds completed for this candidate on this requisition. Used to measure interview process efficiency and candidate experience quality.
reference: Workday HCM Recruiting; 4As Talent Acquisition Best Practices

description: Indicates whether the position is eligible for fully remote or hybrid work arrangements. Relevant for talent pool expansion and workforce flexibility planning in the advertising industry.
reference: Workday HCM Job Requisition; ANA Workforce Standards

description: Broad functional grouping of the role within the agency's workforce taxonomy. Examples include 'Creative', 'Media', 'Account Management', 'Analytics', 'Technology', 'Finance', 'Talent'. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Creative|Media|Account Management|Analytics|Technology|Finance|Talent|Strategy|Production|Operations — promote to reference product]
reference: Workday HCM Job Family; 4As Workforce Classification

description: Seniority level of the open position within the agency's career framework. Drives compensation banding, approval workflows, and resource planning capacity calculations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Junior|Mid-Level|Senior|Lead|Manager|Director|VP|C-Suite — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Workday HCM Job Level; 4As Compensation Benchmarking

description: Official job title for the open position as defined in the requisition. Examples include 'Senior Media Planner', 'Creative Director', 'Programmatic Campaign Manager', 'Account Executive'.
reference: Workday HCM Job Profile; 4As Workforce Classification

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this talent acquisition record in the Workday HCM system. Used for incremental data extraction (ETL) and audit trail compliance.
reference: Workday HCM Audit Trail; GDPR Article 5 (Accountability)

description: Date on which the formal employment offer was extended to the candidate. Used to calculate offer-to-acceptance cycle time and to track offer expiry.
reference: Workday HCM Offer Management; 4As Talent Acquisition Metrics

description: Date by which the candidate must accept or decline the offer. After this date, the offer is automatically rescinded if no response is received.
reference: Workday HCM Offer Management; 4As Talent Acquisition Best Practices

description: Current status of the employment offer extended to the candidate. Tracks the offer lifecycle from issuance through acceptance or decline. Critical for headcount forecasting and onboarding planning.
reference: Workday HCM Offer Management; 4As Talent Acquisition Best Practices

description: Annual base salary amount offered to the candidate in the employment offer. Expressed in the local currency of the office location. Sensitive compensation data used in offer approval workflows and P&L headcount cost planning.
reference: Workday HCM Compensation; SAP S/4HANA FI Module; 4As Compensation Benchmarking

description: Target annual bonus as a percentage of base salary included in the offer. Reflects the variable compensation component of the total compensation package offered to the candidate.
reference: Workday HCM Compensation; SAP S/4HANA FI Module; 4As Compensation Benchmarking

description: Current stage of the candidate in the recruiting pipeline as tracked in Workday HCM. Represents the candidate's progression from application through onboarding. Drives recruiter workflow and hiring velocity reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Applied|Screening|Interview|Assessment|Offer|Onboarding|Withdrawn|Rejected — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Workday HCM Recruiting Stage; 4As Talent Acquisition Best Practices

description: Primary external channel on which the job was posted to attract candidates. Examples include 'LinkedIn', 'Indeed', 'Glassdoor', 'Agency Website', 'Industry Boards (4As, ANA)'. Distinct from source_of_hire which tracks where the hired candidate originated. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: LinkedIn|Indeed|Glassdoor|Agency Website|4As Job Board|ANA Job Board|Campus Portal|Internal Portal — promote to reference product]
reference: Workday HCM Job Posting; IAB Workforce Standards

description: Name of the internal recruiter or talent acquisition specialist assigned to manage this requisition and candidate pipeline. Used for workload distribution and recruiter performance reporting.
Date on which the requisition was formally closed, either because the position was filled, cancelled, or put on indefinite hold. Used to calculate time-to-fill metrics.
reference: Workday HCM Requisition; 4As Talent Acquisition Metrics

description: Externally visible, human-readable requisition number assigned by Workday HCM at the time the job requisition is created. Used in communications with candidates, agencies, and job boards.
reference: Workday HCM Talent Acquisition Module; IAB Workforce Standards

description: Date on which the job requisition was formally opened and approved for recruiting. Used as the start point for time-to-fill and time-to-hire KPI calculations.
reference: Workday HCM Requisition; 4As Talent Acquisition Metrics

description: Current lifecycle status of the job requisition. Tracks whether the headcount need is actively being recruited against, paused, or resolved. Used in open headcount reporting and budget reconciliation.
reference: Workday HCM Requisition Status; SAP S/4HANA PS Module

description: Maximum of the approved compensation band for this job level and family. Used to validate that the offered salary falls within the approved range and to support pay equity analysis.
reference: Workday HCM Compensation Grade; 4As Compensation Benchmarking; EEOC Pay Equity Guidelines

description: Minimum of the approved compensation band for this job level and family. Used to validate that the offered salary falls within the approved range and to support pay equity analysis.
reference: Workday HCM Compensation Grade; 4As Compensation Benchmarking; EEOC Pay Equity Guidelines

description: Channel or method through which the candidate was sourced. Examples include 'LinkedIn', 'Employee Referral', 'Agency', 'Job Board', 'Direct Application', 'Campus Recruiting'. Used to measure recruiting channel effectiveness and cost-per-hire by source. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: LinkedIn|Employee Referral|Staffing Agency|Job Board|Direct Application|Campus Recruiting|Internal Transfer|Social Media — promote to reference product]
reference: Workday HCM Source of Hire; ANA Talent Sourcing Standards

description: Planned date on which the hired candidate is expected to begin employment. Used for onboarding scheduling, resource capacity planning in Workfront, and campaign staffing alignment.
reference: Workday HCM Onboarding; Workfront Resource Planning

description: Candidate's legal authorization to work in the country of the office location. Determines whether visa sponsorship is required and impacts time-to-start planning. Relevant for global advertising agencies with international talent pools.
reference: Workday HCM Compliance; USCIS I-9 Compliance; GDPR Article 9

description: Primary key for candidate
Identifier of the hiring manager or department head who is evaluating this candidate.
Identifier of the job requisition or open position the candidate is being considered for.
Self-referencing FK on candidate (referred_by_candidate_id)
Identifier of the internal recruiter or talent acquisition specialist assigned to this candidate.
The date when the candidate submitted their application or was first entered into the system.
The date when the candidate is available to start work if hired.
Classification of the candidate based on the type of talent role they are being considered for (employee, freelancer, contractor, influencer, actor, photographer, director, production crew).
List of professional certifications, licenses, or credentials held by the candidate.
Timestamp when the candidate record was first created in the system.
Name of the candidate's current employer or most recent employer if currently unemployed.
The candidate's current or most recent job title or role.
The primary field of study or major for the candidate's highest degree.
The candidate's desired or expected salary amount for the position.
Primary email address for candidate communication and correspondence.
The first name or given name of the candidate.
The highest level of education attained by the candidate (high school, associate, bachelor, master, doctorate, professional degree).
Total number of interviews conducted with this candidate.
List of languages the candidate speaks and their proficiency levels.

The last name or family name of the candidate.
URL to the candidate's LinkedIn professional profile.
City where the candidate is currently located or resides.

State, province, or region where the candidate is currently located.
Free-form text notes and comments about the candidate from recruiters, interviewers, or hiring managers.
Date when the candidate accepted the job offer.
Date when a job offer was extended to the candidate.
Primary contact phone number for the candidate.
URL to the candidate's online portfolio showcasing their creative work, projects, or professional samples.
Name of the person or entity who referred the candidate, if applicable.
Reason or explanation for why the candidate was rejected or not selected for the position.
URL or file path to the candidate's resume or curriculum vitae document.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the desired salary (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Numerical score assigned during initial screening or assessment of the candidate's qualifications (0-100 scale).
Comma-separated list or text description of the candidate's key skills, competencies, and areas of expertise relevant to advertising and marketing roles.
The channel or source through which the candidate was acquired (job board, referral, agency, direct application, social media, career site).
Current lifecycle status of the candidate in the recruitment process (new, screening, interviewing, offer extended, hired, rejected, withdrawn).
Timestamp when the candidate record was last modified or updated.
Indicates whether the candidate is willing to relocate for the position (True/False).
The candidate's current work authorization status in the target country (citizen, permanent resident, work visa, requires sponsorship, not authorized).
Total number of years of relevant professional experience the candidate possesses.
Unique surrogate identifier for each formal talent performance evaluation record in the Workday HCM Talent Management module.

Foreign key linking to brand.brand_profile. Business justification: Performance reviews assess talent effectiveness on specific brand accounts. Agencies evaluate brand-specific competencies, client relationship strength, and brand guideline adherence. Essential for ta
Reference to the talent record of the employee being evaluated in this performance review cycle.
Reference to the performance review cycle definition (e.g., Annual FY2024, Mid-Year H1 2024, Probationary 90-Day) under which this evaluation was conducted.
Reference to the talent record of the manager or designated reviewer who conducted and submitted this performance evaluation.

The final performance rating assigned after the HR calibration session, which may differ from the reviewer's initial overall_rating. This is the authoritative rating used for compensation and talent decisions.
reference: Workday HCM Talent Management; ANA Talent Calibration Standards

description: Status of the HR calibration process for this review, ensuring rating consistency and fairness across the agency workforce. Calibration is typically conducted by HR Business Partners before reviews are finalized.
reference: Workday HCM Talent Management; ANA Talent Calibration Standards

description: Numeric score for the client service and account management competency dimension, reflecting the employee's effectiveness in managing client relationships, QBRs, and SOW delivery.
reference: Workday HCM Talent Management; 4As Account Management Standards

description: Numeric score for the collaboration and teamwork competency dimension, assessing cross-functional cooperation across campaign, media, creative, and analytics teams.
Average numeric score across all evaluated competency dimensions (e.g., strategic thinking, client service, creative excellence, collaboration, leadership) for this review period.
reference: Workday HCM Talent Management; 4As Competency Framework

description: The actual date on which the performance review was finalized and marked as completed in Workday HCM, after all required approvals and employee acknowledgement.
The date and time at which this performance review record was first created in Workday HCM, establishing the audit trail for the review lifecycle.
reference: Workday HCM Talent Management; GDPR Article 5 (storage limitation)

description: Numeric score for the creative excellence competency dimension, evaluating the quality, originality, and effectiveness of creative output produced during the review period.
reference: Workday HCM Talent Management; ASA Creative Standards

description: Narrative text identifying the employee's key development areas, skill gaps, and recommended improvement actions for the next review period, as documented by the reviewer.
reference: Workday HCM Talent Management; GDPR Article 5

description: Numeric score for the digital marketing and data analytics competency dimension, reflecting proficiency in performance optimization, DSP/SSP platforms, ROAS analysis, and data-driven decision-making.
reference: Workday HCM Talent Management; IAB Digital Competency Framework

description: The deadline by which the performance review must be completed and submitted by the reviewer, as defined in the review cycle configuration in Workday HCM.
The date on which the employee formally acknowledged receipt and review of their performance evaluation in Workday HCM. Null if not yet acknowledged.
reference: Workday HCM Talent Management; GDPR Article 5 (accountability)

description: Free-text self-evaluation submitted by the employee prior to the manager review, capturing their own perspective on goal attainment, contributions, and development needs.
reference: Workday HCM Talent Management; GDPR Article 5

description: Percentage or weighted score (0.00–100.00) reflecting the degree to which the employee achieved their defined KPI and business goals during the review period, as assessed in Workday HCM.
reference: Workday HCM Talent Management; IAB KPI Framework

description: Number of formally defined performance goals that the employee fully achieved during the review period, as assessed by the reviewer in Workday HCM.
Total number of formal performance goals established for the employee at the start of or during the review period, as recorded in Workday HCM goal management.
The date and time at which this performance review record was most recently updated in Workday HCM, supporting audit trail requirements and Silver layer incremental load processing.
reference: Workday HCM Talent Management; GDPR Article 5

description: Numeric score for the leadership and people management competency dimension, assessing the employee's ability to inspire, direct, and develop team members on campaigns and accounts.
reference: Workday HCM Talent Management; 4As Leadership Competency Model

description: Recommended merit salary increase expressed as a percentage of the employee's current base compensation. Populated only when merit_increase_recommended is True and subject to HR calibration approval.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll; SAP S/4HANA CO

description: Indicates whether the reviewing manager has recommended a merit-based salary increase for the employee as an outcome of this performance review, subject to compensation calibration.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll; SAP S/4HANA CO

description: The holistic performance rating assigned to the employee for the review period, reflecting aggregate assessment across all competencies and goals. Sourced from Workday HCM rating scale. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: exceptional|exceeds_expectations|meets_expectations|needs_improvement|unsatisfactory — promote to reference product if rating scale changes]
reference: Workday HCM Talent Management; ANA Talent Standards

description: Numeric score corresponding to the overall performance rating on the agency's defined scale (e.g., 1.00–5.00), enabling quantitative ranking, trend analysis, and compensation calibration.
Indicates whether a formal Performance Improvement Plan (PIP) has been initiated as a result of this review, typically triggered by an unsatisfactory or needs_improvement overall rating.
reference: Workday HCM Talent Management; FTC Employment Practices

description: Indicates whether the reviewing manager has formally recommended the employee for promotion to a higher job level or title as an outcome of this performance review.
reference: Workday HCM Talent Management; 4As Career Progression Framework

description: The proposed job level or title to which the employee is recommended for promotion (e.g., Senior Account Manager, Associate Creative Director). Populated only when promotion_recommended is True.
reference: Workday HCM Talent Management; 4As Job Level Framework

description: Externally-known business identifier for this performance review record, used in HR correspondence, audit trails, and Workday HCM reference. Format: PR-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.
The last date of the performance evaluation period being assessed in this review (e.g., 2024-12-31 for an annual review covering calendar year 2024).
The first date of the performance evaluation period being assessed in this review (e.g., 2024-01-01 for an annual review covering calendar year 2024).

reference: GDPR Article 5; Workday HCM Core HR; Databricks Delta Lake Change Data Feed

description: The worker's legal given name as it appears on government-issued identification documents. Used for payroll, tax filings, and work authorization verification.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA; IRS W-2/1099 Requirements

description: The worker's legal family name as it appears on government-issued identification documents. Used for payroll, tax filings, and work authorization verification.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA; IRS W-2/1099 Requirements

description: The worker's primary mobile phone number in E.164 international format. Used for emergency contact, two-factor authentication, and urgent production communications.

A tokenized or masked reference to the worker's national identification number (e.g., SSN, NIN, SIN). The actual value is stored in Workday HCM encrypted vault; this field holds a non-reversible reference token for cross-system reconciliation without exposing the raw identifier.
reference: GDPR Article 9; CCPA; IRS W-2/1099 Requirements; NIST SP 800-188

description: The worker's nationality expressed as an ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Used for work authorization verification, tax treaty eligibility, and international assignment management.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; GDPR Article 9; IRS Tax Treaty Guidelines

description: The agency office or work location code where the worker is primarily based (e.g., NYC-HQ, LAX-WEST, LON-SOHO). Used for tax jurisdiction determination, resource planning, and facilities management.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: The date on which the worker completed all required onboarding activities including I-9/right-to-work verification, system access provisioning, compliance training, and policy acknowledgements. Distinct from hire_date which is the contractual start date.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; USCIS I-9 Requirements; GDPR Article 5

description: The frequency at which the worker is compensated. Drives payroll run scheduling in Workday HCM Payroll and SAP S/4HANA FI. 'project_based' applies to freelancers and contractors paid upon milestone or project completion.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: The worker's base compensation rate as stored in Workday HCM Payroll. For FTEs this represents annual salary; for freelancers and contractors this represents the agreed hourly or daily rate. Used for budget forecasting, P&L reporting, and SOW cost estimation.
reference: Workday HCM Payroll; SAP S/4HANA CO; 4As Compensation Benchmarks

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the worker's pay rate (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Required for multi-currency payroll processing and international contractor management.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI; Workday HCM Payroll

description: The worker's personal email address used for onboarding communications, offboarding notifications, and contact when work email is unavailable (e.g., for freelancers and contractors).
The name the worker prefers to be addressed by in day-to-day agency operations, project assignments, and internal communications. May differ from legal name.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; GDPR Article 4

description: The contracted or expected number of working hours per week for the worker. Used for capacity planning in Workfront resource management, overtime threshold calculations, and FTE equivalency reporting.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; Workfront Resource Management; FLSA Overtime Regulations

description: The worker's primary creative or technical specialization relevant to advertising and marketing production (e.g., Art Director, Copywriter, Director of Photography, Influencer, Actor, Motion Designer, Media Planner, Programmatic Trader, Data Analyst). Used for talent matching and resource allocation in Workfront.
reference: Workday HCM Talent Management; Workfront Resource Management; 4As Job Classification Standards

description: The IRS or applicable tax authority classification governing how the worker is compensated and taxed. Determines payroll withholding obligations, 1099 reporting requirements, and SAP S/4HANA FI vendor setup for corp-to-corp engagements.
reference: IRS Publication 15-A; IRS Form 1099-NEC; SAP S/4HANA FI; FTC Advertising Practices

description: The primary country tax jurisdiction for the worker expressed as an ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code. Determines applicable tax treaties, withholding rates, and payroll processing rules in SAP S/4HANA FI.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3; IRS Publication 515; OECD Model Tax Convention; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: The state or province tax jurisdiction for the worker (e.g., NY, CA, TX for US workers). Used for state income tax withholding, nexus determination, and multi-state payroll compliance in SAP S/4HANA Payroll.
reference: IRS Publication 15; SAP S/4HANA FI; CCPA

description: The date the worker's employment or engagement with the agency ended, as recorded in Workday HCM. Null for active workers. Triggers offboarding workflows, system access revocation, and final payroll processing.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; GDPR Article 17 (Right to Erasure)

description: The categorized reason for the worker's departure from the agency. Used for attrition analytics, workforce planning, and compliance reporting. Null for active workers.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; 4As Talent Standards

description: The expiration date of the worker's visa or work permit. Used to trigger renewal workflows and ensure continuous work authorization compliance. Null for workers with unrestricted authorization.
reference: USCIS I-9 Requirements; UK Right to Work Checks; GDPR Article 9

description: The type of visa or work permit held by the worker where applicable (e.g., H-1B, L-1, O-1, Tier 2, EU Blue Card). Null for workers with unrestricted work authorization. Used for immigration compliance tracking.
reference: USCIS Visa Classifications; UK Home Office; GDPR Article 9

description: The worker's approved work location arrangement. Impacts tax nexus determination, facilities planning, and collaboration tool provisioning. Particularly relevant for multi-jurisdiction agencies operating under GDPR and CCPA.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; GDPR Article 4

description: The current work authorization status of the worker in their primary work jurisdiction. Critical for compliance with immigration laws and I-9/right-to-work verification requirements. Triggers alerts when visa or permit expiry is approaching.
reference: USCIS I-9 Requirements; UK Right to Work Checks; GDPR Article 9; FTC Advertising Practices

description: The agency-issued or primary professional email address for the worker. Used for system access provisioning, Workfront project assignments, and internal communications.

Document reference identifier for the worker's work permit or right-to-work documentation stored in the agency's DAM or Workday HCM document vault. Used for immigration compliance audits and I-9 reverification tracking.
reference: USCIS I-9 Requirements; UK Right to Work Checks; Workfront DAM; GDPR Article 9

description: The native worker identifier assigned by Workday HCM Core HR module. Used for system-of-record cross-referencing and integration with Workday Payroll, Time Tracking, and Talent Management modules.

Current lifecycle status of the worker record within the agency. Drives access provisioning, payroll processing, and resource availability in Workfront. 'pending_onboard' indicates pre-start date; 'on_leave' covers parental, medical, and sabbatical leave.
reference: Workday HCM Core HR; Workfront Resource Management
